title: Phase 6n Wave 1b Witt-class headline is conditional on undischarged DMNO-2010 hypothesis schema CentralChargePreservesDrinfeldCenter (literature wall)
source: docs/roadmaps/Phase6n_Roadmap.md (Wave 1b Stage 5.8/5.9/5.11/5.12, lines 87, 571-597); lean/SKEFTHawking/SymTFTAudit/DrinfeldCenter.lean:122; PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md §5 #6 IsDMNOBiconditional KEEP
description: The Phase 6n Wave 1b SymTFT 'Witt class of MTCs' formalization lifts the Schellekens chain (24|c_- => N_f=3 generations) to the abelian-group / Drinfeld-center level, but the categorical characterization of Witt equivalence (DMNO 2010 Theorem 5.2: Witt-trivial <=> has Lagrangian algebra / central-charge preserves Drinfeld center) is shipped only as an undischarged Prop-level hypothesis schema `CentralChargePreservesDrinfeldCenter` (+ `_braided`, `_pseudoUnitary`, `_strictlyPseudoUnitary` refinements). Theorems consuming it (`wittEquivalentMTC_*_implies_wittClass_eq`) take it as an explicit hypothesis. The roadmap itself states Stage 5.12 'exposes the true Theorem 5.2 substantive content' which the trivial-witness layer does NOT prove.
what_falls_short: The headline categorical Witt-equivalence result is conditional: the forward direction (Witt-trivial => has Lagrangian algebra) is asserted as hypothesis, not derived. The roadmap acknowledges the trivial-witness equivalence is broken precisely by the strict refinement, meaning the substantive DMNO content remains unproved in-project.
remediation: Discharging requires full MTC + Lagrangian-algebra + Anderson-dual infrastructure absent in Mathlib (DMNO 2010 / KEEP_AS_TRACKED per §5 #6). Either build the multi-year Mathlib MTC substrate, or keep the conditional framing and ensure D2/L2 bundle prose explicitly states the Witt-class compression is conditional on the (cited, unproven-in-Lean) DMNO theorem.
evidence: Roadmap line 597: 'Wave 1b.5.12+ refinement ... breaks the trivial-witness equivalence and exposes the true Theorem 5.2 substantive content (S-matrix factorization argument)'; line 169-170 of TRACKED_HYPOTHESES: 'IsDMNOBiconditional KEEP (literature: DMNO 2010 main theorem; forward direction Witt-trivial -> has Lagrangian algebra requires MTC typeclass + Lagrangian-algebra construction infrastructure absent in Mathlib).'

title: Phase 6n Wave 1b SU(2)k pseudo-unitary witnesses inherit native_decide axioms transitively from SU2kFusion.lean
source: docs/roadmaps/Phase6n_Roadmap.md (Session 17 lesson 3, line 602; Wave 1b.5.11/5.12 closures, lines 597, 645)
description: The rank-2 SU(2)_1 Hadamard pseudo-unitary witnesses (`su2k1_isPseudoUnitary`, `su2k1_data_isStrictlyPseudoUnitary_witness`) and the Stage 5.11/5.12 closure theorems inherit `native_decide` axioms transitively from the project-baked `SU2kFusion.lean` substrate (su2k_data). The roadmap explicitly notes these closures are NOT standard-kernel-only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — only the headlines NOT depending on SU(2)k data are.
what_falls_short: Concrete non-trivial witnesses for the pseudo-unitary modular-data predicate rely on native_decide-tainted SU(2)k fusion data, so the closure bundles carry compiled-kernel trust rather than standard-kernel trust. This is the same class of nd-reliance the 6AO Track-3 refactor showed is eliminable via structural lemmas.
remediation: Refactor SU2kFusion.lean fusion-data facts from native_decide to structural/decide-free proofs (cf. 6AO Track-3 which eliminated 4 KMM native_decides via structural lemmas), which would propagate clean-kernel status to the SU(2)k pseudo-unitary witnesses.
evidence: Line 602: 'Project-baked native_decide axioms in SU2kFusion.lean propagate transitively to anything depending on su2k_data; the substantive Wave 1b.5.11 headlines (those NOT depending on SU(2)_1 data) remain ... standard-kernel-only.' Line 645: 'the closure inherits SU(2)k native_decide axioms transitively from Wave 1b.5.11s su2k1_data substrate.'

title: Phase 6n Wave 1a.3 explicit gamma_2^(loop) Beliaev-Galitskii 2-loop coefficient permanently DEFERRED (effort-magnitude descope behind 'no longer load-bearing' framing)
description: The original Wave 1a.3 Path A objective was a definitive Gevrey-1-vs-geometric resurgence verdict for the BEC SK-EFT gradient expansion via explicit per-order loop coefficients gamma_2^(loop) through gamma_5/gamma_7 (Beliaev-Galitskii 1959 2-loop self-energy). This was repeatedly deferred: first as 'multi-week', then re-scoped to 'few-session SymPy' pending Shi-Griffin Phys.Rep.304(1998) which is 'not in primary-source cache' and 'requires institutional access (not on arXiv)'. The verdict was instead delivered via a structural envelope theorem that 'decouples the asymptotic-growth verdict from the explicit per-order computation'.
what_falls_short: The headline 'definitive resurgence verdict' rests on a structural envelope argument conditional on the loop piece being geometric with some rate r_loop in (0,1) (a bounded-coupling assumption), NOT on a computed loop coefficient. The explicit coefficient that would make the verdict unconditional remains uncomputed, gated on a paywalled source. 'No longer load-bearing' is asserted but the verdict is conditional on the unverified loop-boundedness assumption.
remediation: Fetch Shi-Griffin 1998 (institutional access) or reconstruct the Beliaev-Galitskii 2-loop integrand from arXiv-available sources, then compute gamma_2^(loop) via SymPy to convert the conditional envelope verdict into an unconditional per-order result. Per 'no de-scope from effort magnitude', the paywall is a real external dependency, not a justified stop.
evidence: Line 547: 'Stage 4b explicit gamma_2^(loop) DEFERRED pending Shi-Griffin Phys. Rep. 304 (1998) primary-source cache fetch ... No longer load-bearing for substrate verdict.' Line 727: 'requires institutional access (not on arXiv); non-load-bearing for the Wave 1a.3 substrate verdict.' Line 524: envelope verdict holds only 'under bounded-coupling assumption IsGeometric gamma_loop M_loop r_loop'.

title: Phase 6n Wave 2c full measure-theoretic LDP framework (Cramer/Varadhan/Gartner-Ellis) deferred; third Sakharov-style biconditional shipped only at restricted W-form-GC level
description: Wave 2c's stated 'new constraint' was a falsifiable inequality on the analog-Hawking spectrum from trajectory-Crooks detailed balance, derived as a third 'Sakharov-style biconditional' (AnalogHawkingSubstrate.satisfies_GLU_monotonicity <=> EmissionSpectrum.LDP_rate_function.satisfies_GallavottiCohen). What shipped is the linear-response Gaussian substrate + a W-form Gallavotti-Cohen characterization; the full biconditional requires LDP infrastructure (Cramer IID -> Varadhan -> Gartner-Ellis, '~10-15 sessions', Mathlib MeasureTheory) that is flagged as in-program-build / Mathlib-upstream-PR candidate and NOT shipped.
what_falls_short: The claimed Phase-6n deliverable (a third Sakharov-class biconditional with falsifiable spectrum predictions beyond FDT) is delivered only at the FDT-pinned Gaussian / abstract-rate-function-class level. The substrate-to-spectrum biconditional itself is never proved; the DR's own triviality flag ('if satisfied trivially given the thermal spectrum, the contribution is FDT-level only') is not resolved by what shipped.
remediation: Build the abstract LDP rate-function -> Cramer -> Varadhan -> Gartner-Ellis chain on Mathlib MeasureTheory (estimated 10-15 sessions in-roadmap; per dev-rate this is days), then prove the substrate<->spectrum biconditional and check non-triviality vs the bare thermal spectrum on Steinhauer/Weinfurtner/Carusotto parameters.
evidence: Line 557: 'Stage 5c+ Cramer / Varadhan / Gartner-Ellis full LDP framework is the natural Wave 2c continuation ... Total ~10-15 sessions.' Line 343: 'Requires the analog-Hawking spectrum substrate ... + LDP infrastructure ... candidate Mathlib upstream-PR opportunity.' Line 351 DR flag: 'if the inequality is satisfied trivially given the existing thermal spectrum, the contribution is FDT-level only and not novel.'

title: Phase 6n Wave 1b full Mathlib MTC + Witt-group + Lagrangian-algebra infrastructure deferred as 'multi-year community project'; only project-local chiral-central-charge piece shipped
description: Wave 1b repeatedly flags that the genuine deliverable — full Modular Tensor Category + Witt-group + Anderson-dual + Lagrangian-algebra infrastructure — is a 'multi-year community project' and ships only a 'project-local form' capturing the chiral-central-charge-mod-24 piece (WittInvariant := ZMod 24). The Deligne tensor product ⊠ as the group operation was built in-program over many sub-sessions (5.10a-5.10g) but the categorical central-charge-additivity discharge (`CentralChargeAdditiveUnderDeligneTensor` at categorical level) and the connection of WittClass to actual MTCs (not just integers mod 24) remain.
what_falls_short: The 'Witt class of MTCs' compression that would let the program's anomaly content collapse into a single categorical object is shipped only at the integer-mod-24 / abelian-group level. WittClass is proved bijective with ZMod 24 (i.e. it IS just ZMod 24), not constructed as the actual Witt group of modular tensor categories. The categorical leverage claimed in the thesis is not realized.
remediation: Either accept the project-local integer-level form as the honest scope (and ensure bundle prose does not over-claim a categorical Witt-group construction), or commit to the multi-year Mathlib MTC build. The Deligne-tensor groundwork (5.10*) is the start of the latter.
evidence: Line 528: 'WittClass.toInvariant_injective + ...surjective => WittClass IS structurally bijective with ZMod 24 ... Full Mathlib MTC + Witt-group + Anderson dual + Lagrangian algebra remains a multi-year community project.' Line 189: 'full Mathlib MTC + Witt-group + Anderson dual + Lagrangian algebra infrastructure remains a multi-year community project.'

title: Phase 6n Wave 1b continuous-sector SymTFT (gauge_erasure, GD-4-factor orthogonality) deferred indefinitely — requires Brennan-Sun BF fusion the audit verdict marks NOT APPLICABLE
source: docs/roadmaps/Phase6n_Roadmap.md (Wave 1b Stage 2 verdict + continuous-sector follow-on, lines 87, 170, 190, 197-198, 569)
description: The highest-leverage open structural problem the original DR flagged — the GD-orthogonality 4-factor decomposition lifting cleanly into a single SymTFT (compressing the program's anomaly content from four parallel factors into one object) — was the marquee Wave 1b ambition. The Stage 2 primary-source audit (arXiv:2507.05350) returned PARTIALLY APPLICABLE: the discrete dynamical-KMS Z_2 embeds, but the continuous entropic U(1)_T does NOT embed and requires fusion with Brennan-Sun BF technology = 'multi-wave research project, deferred to Phase 6o'. The GD-4-factor and gauge_erasure candidates are deferred as 'speculative connections'.
what_falls_short: The compression deliverable (four orthogonality factors -> one SymTFT object) is not achieved; the audit downgrades it to a partial result + identified obstruction. The continuous-sector content (which is where the program's entropic/thermal physics lives) is precisely what the framework cannot handle.
remediation: Pursue the Brennan-Sun arXiv:2407.07951 BF-fusion route for continuous symmetries (flagged Phase 6o), or document the partial-result + obstruction as the honest deliverable (also publishable per the roadmap's own framing).
evidence: Line 170: 'continuous entropic U(1)_T does NOT embed (paper does not handle continuous symmetries) and requires fusion with Brennan-Sun arXiv:2407.07951 BF technology — multi-wave research project per DR 6.2, deferred to Phase 6o.' Line 190: 'gauge_erasure (Phase 3 W2) and GD_orthogonality (4-factor decomposition) candidates deferred ... speculative connections.'

title: Phase 6n Wave 2a SKEFTAxioms entropy-current / Onsager originally shipped as trivial-discharge stubs (zeroEntropyCurrent, ⟨0⟩), later upgraded — P4/defining-the-conclusion anti-pattern in first pass
source: docs/roadmaps/Phase6n_Roadmap.md (Wave 2a Session 7, lines 11, 90; KMS framework finding, line 222)
description: The Glorioso-Liu axiomatic Wave 2a initially discharged its load-bearing existence theorems trivially: `entropy_current_exists` via `⟨zeroEntropyCurrent, rfl⟩` and `OnsagerReciprocity_from_KMS` via `⟨0⟩` — making the `A : SKEFTAxioms` parameter non-load-bearing (a defining-the-conclusion / P4 self-discharge). Session 7 upgraded both to substantive constructions. Separately, a KMS-framework finding shows strict-invariance KMSSymmetry is 'generically incompatible with non-trivial dissipative SK-EFT actions (canonical KMS shift ... requires gamma_1 = gamma_2 = 0)', forcing the non-trivial existence witness to use an algebraic-FDR form rather than strict invariance.
what_falls_short: The first-pass ship of the program's clean SK-EFT axiomatic main theorems was vacuous (trivial inhabitants); only the strengthening pass made the axiom parameter load-bearing. The strict-invariance KMS form admits only the zero action, so the substantive content depends on a weaker algebraic-FDR predicate whose physical equivalence to genuine dynamical KMS is an additional (documented) modeling assumption.
remediation: Confirm the Session-7 substantive upgrades (Noether construction for entropy current, 9x9 diagonal embedding for Onsager) are the shipped form and that no zero-witness path remains; verify the algebraic-FDR `hasDynamicalKMS_algebraic` is documented as a modeling choice in I1/D3/L3 absorption prose.
evidence: Line 90: 'entropy_current_exists upgraded from trivial-discharge ⟨zeroEntropyCurrent, rfl⟩ to substantive Noether construction ...; OnsagerReciprocity_from_KMS upgraded from trivial-discharge ⟨0⟩ to substantive 9x9 diagonal embedding ... Both make A : SKEFTAxioms parameter load-bearing.' Line 222: 'strict-invariance KMSSymmetry is generically incompatible with non-trivial dissipative SK-EFT actions (canonical KMS shift generates new monomials whose only cancellation requires gamma_1 = gamma_2 = 0).'

title: Phase 6n Wave 2b/2c parametric/W-form no-go theorems carry the physics in an h_disagree hypothesis (hypothesis-restating obstruction; substantive load in the witness)
description: The Perarnau-Llobet quantum no-go `perarnau_llobet_no_go_parametric` proves that no measurement scheme reproduces average energy AND recovers TPM-on-diagonal, but the roadmap states 'The load-bearing physics content (TPM averages disagree with true averages on coherent states) is the h_disagree hypothesis; the algebraic obstruction is proved fully via direct rewriting.' Similarly Wave 2c's `skeft_substrate_yields_WFormGC` activates 'conditionally on c.i_2 != 0 (non-trivial noise)'. The substantive content is loaded into hypotheses/concrete witnesses, not the parametric theorem.
what_falls_short: The parametric headline is an algebraic obstruction whose physical bite lives entirely in the supplied h_disagree witness; without a concrete witness the theorem is content-light. The W-form-GC substrate theorem is conditional on a non-degeneracy hypothesis. These are honest framings but mean the 'no-go' headline alone is not self-supporting physics.
remediation: The concrete Fin-2 ℝ/ℂ and higher-dim witnesses already supply h_disagree non-vacuously; ensure any bundle prose presents the no-go as 'parametric obstruction + concrete witness' jointly, never the parametric theorem alone, and that the c.i_2 != 0 conditional is stated as the degenerate-substrate carve-out it is.
evidence: Line 281: 'The load-bearing physics content ... is the h_disagree hypothesis; the algebraic obstruction is proved fully via direct rewriting.' Line 333: 'the W-form GC clause activates conditionally on c.i_2 != 0 (non-trivial noise; the conditional form is structural — degenerate substrates with c.i_2 = 0 dont carry meaningful rate functions).'

title: Phase 6n Wave 1c Atiyah-Singer reformulation is a memo only (no Lean); APS-eta for analog horizons deferred to Phase 6o
description: Wave 1c delivers a physics-paper-grade memo restating Phase 6e a_n + Phase 6m Sakharov tr(I) + Phase 5z Goldstino zero modes in Atiyah-Singer / APS / Witten-Yonekura language, with NO Lean formalization. The one potentially-new structural item — the APS eta-invariant for analog-horizon backgrounds (BEC-acoustic, ADW, He3-A), which 'would be publishable as first such calculation' — is explicitly promoted to a Phase 6o follow-up. The full Mathlib Atiyah-Singer formalization is 'a multi-year community-coordination effort ... deferred indefinitely.'
what_falls_short: The Wave 1c deliverable is reformulation prose with zero verified content; the only genuinely novel item (substrate-side APS-eta on analog horizons) is pushed to a later phase. (Note: counts.json shows APSEta.* modules now exist, suggesting Phase 6o partially executed this.)
remediation: The APSEta.{ADWHorizon,BECAcoustic,He3A,...} modules now present in counts.json indicate the Phase 6o follow-up shipped; verify those modules deliver the substrate-side APS-eta computation the memo flagged, and that the memo's claims are backed by the Lean modules rather than remaining prose-only.
evidence: Line 88: 'Wave 1c ✅ SHIPPED (memo ... APS-eta for analog horizons flagged as Phase 6o follow-up).' Line 424: 'The full Mathlib Atiyah-Singer formalization is a multi-year community-coordination effort and stays deferred indefinitely.' counts.json module list includes SKEFTHawking.APSEta.ADWHorizon / BECAcoustic / He3A.

title: Phase 6v 'GREEN-NO-FINDINGS' close was a false-positive: adversarial review passed shipped-scope while real substance was 30-90% short of intent (LoE inflation = thread-loss)
description: Phase 6v was declared closed at 'GREEN-NO-FINDINGS' after a 3-round adversarial review, then a 2026-05-26 PM end-of-session audit issued a STATUS CORRECTION: the GREEN verdict reflected review against *shipped* scope, not the original intent of Sub-waves 8.D-8.H (Items A-E). 'Per user audit, real substance is 30-90% short per item. LoE inflation 1.3-2x mid-session was the smoking gun for thread-loss.' A corrective Wave 6v.9 (sub-waves 9.A-9.E) was required, with the explicit discipline note 'Acceptance criteria are the bar; reviewer GREEN is NOT.'
what_falls_short: A passed adversarial review (the project's normal completion gate) certified work that was substantively incomplete — surrogates renamed as deliverables, substrate-encoded values presented as Hamiltonian-derived, ITE wrappers instead of derivations. The failure mode is the gate itself being gameable by scope-narrowing.
remediation: Wave 6v.9 (9.A-9.E) was specified to close all 23 acceptance-criteria checkboxes with kernel-only theorems and a final review briefed that prior GREEN was on shipped-not-intended scope; the roadmap marks 9.A-9.E all GREEN. Verify in source that the 23 acceptance criteria each have a backing substantive (non-ITE-wrapper) theorem, since the original GREEN proved the review alone is insufficient evidence.
evidence: Line 36: 'The GREEN-NO-FINDINGS verdict below reflects adversarial review against shipped scope, NOT against original intent ... real substance is 30-90% short per item. LoE inflation 1.3-2x mid-session was the smoking gun for thread-loss.' Line 38: 'Acceptance criteria are the bar; reviewer GREEN is NOT.'

title: Phase 6v.9 sub-wave 9.D 3D winding-number universality shipped a cheaper surrogate predicate; full Brillouin-zone-integral winding-number construction deferred to future wave
source: docs/roadmaps/Phase6v_Roadmap.md (Wave 6v.9 9.D, line 50; Sub-wave 8.G, line 82, 131; Wave 6v.8 8.C, lines 105, 113, 233)
description: Sub-wave 9.D closed at 'GREEN-WITH-ADVISORIES — cheaper-path was authorized': it ships an `IsSatoFujimotoIntegerWinding` surrogate predicate + parity universality + biconditional characterization, with 'Full BZ-integral construction deferred as future-wave follow-up (~400-800 LoC)'. The underlying NbRe 3D winding-number identity was originally a True-bodied placeholder; Sub-wave 8.C discharged a related Fu-Kane TRIM-product Pfaffian Z_2 invariant (~220 LoC) via 'Pathway A' instead of the ~2000 LoC MapDegree winding-number original, strengthening H_NbReWindingNumberIdentity from True to fuKaneInvariant sc = -1.
what_falls_short: The genuine 3D winding-number (a Brillouin-zone integral of the Berry connection) is replaced by an integer-winding surrogate predicate + a TRIM-product Pfaffian Z_2 invariant. The full BZ-integral topological invariant — the actual SOTA object for the NbRe classification — is not constructed; a cheaper Z_2/parity proxy stands in.
remediation: Ship the full BZ-integral winding-number construction (~400-800 LoC per the roadmap's own estimate; days at project dev-rate) to replace the surrogate, or document in D2/D4 bundle prose that the topological classification rests on the Fu-Kane TRIM Pfaffian Z_2 proxy rather than a computed 3D winding number.
evidence: Line 50: 'Cheaper-alternative path AUTHORIZED ... Full BZ-integral construction deferred as future-wave follow-up (~400-800 LoC).' Line 113: 'Pathway A — Fu-Kane / Sato-Fujimoto TRIM-product Pfaffian Z_2 invariant at ~220 LoC project-local (vs. the ~2000 LoC MapDegree original estimate).'

title: Phase 6v.8 NbRe no-new-axioms discipline names a known Mathlib gap (3D winding-number identity) destined for tracked-Prop treatment
description: The Wave 6v.8 NbRe wave explicitly anticipates an irreducible gap: 'any irreducible gap (e.g., 3D-winding-number identity Mathlib lacks) lands as tracked Prop with discharge plan in the wave roadmap, NOT a new project-local axiom.' This pre-commits a load-bearing topological identity to tracked-Prop status rather than derivation.
what_falls_short: A core topological-classification fact for the NbRe SOTA result was planned to be a tracked hypothesis. (It was ultimately discharged at the Fu-Kane Z_2 proxy level per 8.C, strengthening H_NbReWindingNumberIdentity from True; but the discipline note shows the program's default for hard topological identities is tracked-Prop, which keeps the headline conditional on the cited identity.)
remediation: Verify H_NbReWindingNumberIdentity is now substantively discharged (8.C reports it strengthened from True to fuKaneInvariant sc = -1) and that no NbRe headline still consumes an undischarged winding-number tracked Prop; if the full 3D winding number is needed, build the BZ-integral form.
evidence: Line 233: 'any irreducible gap (e.g., 3D-winding-number identity Mathlib lacks) lands as tracked Prop with discharge plan in the wave roadmap, NOT a new project-local axiom.' Line 105: 'H_NbReWindingNumberIdentity body is strengthened from True to fuKaneInvariant sc = -1.'

title: Phase 6v.5 hashing-bound and 6v.6 cyclotomic-QFT substrates were prematurely deferred as 'future Mathlib waves' when Mathlib already shipped the substrate
description: A post-close scout sweep found '2 of 3 deferrals were premature scoping' — Mathlib v4.29.1 already contained Real.binEntropy (for the Wave 6v.5 Shannon-entropy hashing bound) and CyclotomicField/IsPrimitiveRoot (for the Wave 6v.6 cyclotomic-QFT-basis predicate), which had been labelled 'future Mathlib waves' and deferred. Both were then discharged in follow-up waves 6v.5b (~85 LoC) and 6v.6b (~50 LoC).
what_falls_short: Real deliverables were deferred on a false premise (assumed Mathlib gap that did not exist), illustrating the project's documented failure mode of reflexive deferral / effort-magnitude descope. The work was small (~50-85 LoC) and immediately doable once scoped.
remediation: Resolved in 6v.5b (c303be6) and 6v.6b (52b1eef). The systemic lesson — run a Mathlib substrate scout before deferring as a 'future Mathlib wave' — is the durable remediation; confirm the lint/scout step is in the wave-entry checklist.
evidence: Line 109: '2 of 3 deferrals were premature scoping — Mathlib v4.29.1 already contains the substrate I had labelled as future Mathlib waves.' Lines 111-112: binEntropy DISCHARGED in 6v.5b; cyclotomic predicate DISCHARGED in 6v.6b.

title: Phase 6AH waves 6AH.6 (DEJMPS) and 6AH.7 (GHZ) document live triviality gaps: a coinciding-pairing DEJMPS theorem witnessing nothing, and a defining-the-conclusion GHZ advantage
description: Two Phase 6AH waves were created specifically to close documented triviality/defining-the-conclusion gaps surfaced by adversarial review: (6AH.6) 'the only shipped DEJMPS theorem uses the symmetric Werner input where the corrected diagonal A^2+D^2 pairing COINCIDES with the naive A^2+B^2, so no theorem witnesses the correction' — i.e. the DEJMPS-correction result was vacuous on its only input; and (6AH.7) 'Close the 6AC defining-the-conclusion gap: derive the GHZ_3 randomization advantage = 0 from an explicit local-randomization model so w3_beats_ghz_randomization_advantage is genuinely two-sided' — i.e. the W3-beats-GHZ advantage had its conclusion baked into the definition.
what_falls_short: Two shipped QuantumNetwork (D6 bundle) results were structurally hollow: the DEJMPS correction was never exercised by a distinguishing input, and the GHZ-comparison advantage was defined to equal its claimed value rather than derived. These are exactly the P5/defining-the-conclusion anti-patterns the project's strengthening discipline targets.
remediation: Both fix theorems exist in source (dejmps_diagonal_pairing_distinguishes in DEJMPSConvergence.lean; w3_beats_ghz_randomization_advantage in MultipartiteComparison.lean), indicating the waves shipped. Verify the asymmetric-input DEJMPS theorem genuinely has B != D and the GHZ advantage is now derived from an explicit local-randomization model (two-sided), not still defined-to-conclude.
evidence: Line 88: 'no theorem witnesses the correction'; line 90: 'dejmps_diagonal_pairing_distinguishes — a concrete asymmetric Bell-diagonal input (B != D) on which they differ.' Line 95: 'Close the 6AC defining-the-conclusion gap ... so w3_beats_ghz_randomization_advantage is genuinely two-sided.'

title: Phase 6AH amplitude-damping exact diamond distance had a loose bracket (gamma/2 <= ◇ <= gamma+1-sqrt(1-gamma)) flagged for tightening with a documented fallback
description: Wave 6AH.4 set out to upgrade `diamondDist_ampDamp` from a bracket to an exact closed form, with the explicit hedge 'If exact resists, ship a strictly-tighter bracket + document.' Amp-damp is non-Pauli-covariant so the max-entangled input is not optimal, making the exact value harder.
what_falls_short: The roadmap pre-authorizes a fallback to a non-exact (merely tighter) bracket for a SOTA channel-distance object, which would leave the amplitude-damping diamond distance as an inequality rather than an equality. (Source shows diamondDist_ampDamp_eq = gamma now exists in NamedChannelDiamondExact.lean, so the exact form was achieved — the hedge was not exercised.)
remediation: diamondDist_ampDamp_eq (= gamma) is present in NamedChannelDiamondExact.lean line 301, so the exact closed form shipped and the bracket-fallback hedge was not needed. No action beyond confirming the exact theorem's hypotheses are unconditional (0 <= gamma <= 1).
evidence: Line 77: 'If exact resists, ship a strictly-tighter bracket + document. Risk: MED.' Source: NamedChannelDiamondExact.lean:301 'theorem diamondDist_ampDamp_eq {gamma : ℝ} (h0 : 0 <= gamma) (h1 : gamma <= 1)'.

title: Phase 6AH.1 RB->diamond foundation shipped but not root-imported, with the deep rank-1 trace-norm brick flagged as the only real work and no reusable lemma
description: Wave 6AH.1's foundation (RBCertificate.lean, commit 4530baa5) shipped 'kernel-pure, not root-imported yet', with the headline `avgGateFidelity_diamondDist_bound` and the deep fidelity-at-pure-state brick (`sqrtFidelity(rho, maxEntangled n) = sqrt<Omega|rho|Omega>`) marked 🔜, noting 'needs rank-1 trace norm ... no reusable lemma exists'. A module shipped but un-root-imported is invisible to the build's defaultTargets and to validate.py/graph pipeline.

(Source now shows avgGateFidelity_diamondDist_bound exists in RBCertificate.lean, indicating completion.)
remediation: avgGateFidelity_diamondDist_bound is present in RBCertificate.lean; confirm the module is now root-imported (in SKEFTHawking.lean) so it is captured by lake build defaultTargets, ExtractDeps, and counts — an un-root-imported kernel-pure theorem is not pipeline-verified.
evidence: Line 37-38: 'RBCertificate.lean, commit 4530baa5, kernel-pure, not root-imported yet'; line 40: 'the deep brick (needs rank-1 trace norm ... no reusable lemma exists).' Source confirms headline now present.

description: Phase 5i opened with 17 project-wide sorry (Uqsl2AffineHopf 12, Uqsl3Hopf 3, CenterFunctor 2), with Wave 2 (Uqsl3Hopf comul_respect + antipode_respect + S^2) blocked by 'RingQuot typeclass divergence', and an Aristotle submission (6dbc9447) made with all 17 sorry + RingQuot workaround hints. title: Phase 5i SU(3)_k fusion-rule headlines (commutativity, associativity, charge conjugation) proved exclusively by native_decide
description: All SU(3)_1 and SU(3)_2 fusion-ring properties — commutativity, associativity, charge-conjugation involution, Z_3 simple-current group, Fibonacci subcategory tau⊗tau=1+tau — are 'ALL verified by native_decide'. The deep-research note explicitly chose native_decide as the proof method ('All fusion multiplicities are 0 or 1 — native_decide feasible'). This is part of the project-wide 199 native_decide occurrences concentrated in lattice/fusion files.
what_falls_short: The first-ever SU(3)_k fusion formalization rests on compiled-kernel native_decide rather than standard-kernel proofs, so these headlines carry native_decide axioms rather than [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. Per Invariant #10 + the 6AO Track-3 precedent, finite associativity/commutativity facts are often eliminable via structural/decide-free refactor.
remediation: Where these SU(3)_k fusion facts back published D2/L2 anyon-theory claims, refactor the finite fusion-coefficient checks to `decide` (clean kernel) or structural lemmas (cf. 6AO Track-3 KMM elimination); otherwise document them as project-tolerated native_decide with justification comments (lint_native_decide_comments already enforces the comments).
evidence: Line 72: 'ALL verified by native_decide — zero sorry, zero axioms.' Line 69: 'SU(3)_2: commutativity + associativity PROVED by native_decide.' Deep-research finding line 26: 'All fusion multiplicities are 0 or 1 — native_decide feasible.'

title: Phase 5i restricted quantum group u-bar_q(sl_3) deferred as 'not needed for fusion verification'
description: The restricted quantum group u-bar_q(sl_3) — the small-quantum-group quotient at a root of unity that is the genuine algebraic object underlying the SU(3)_k MTC — is marked '[ ] Restricted quantum group u-bar_q(sl_3) (deferred — not needed for fusion verification)'. The fusion rules were instead verified directly via native_decide on the fusion ring.
what_falls_short: The fusion-rule verification bypasses the restricted-quantum-group construction, so the SU(3)_k MTC is validated combinatorially (fusion ring + S/T matrices over Q(zeta_15)) rather than via the underlying small quantum group. The algebraic provenance (representation category of u-bar_q(sl_3)) is asserted by literature, not constructed in Lean.
remediation: Construct u-bar_q(sl_3) as the RingQuot quotient at a root of unity and its representation category, then derive the fusion rules as a theorem about that category rather than positing the fusion ring — a substantial higher-rank quantum-group build. Only needed if a bundle claims the MTC is derived from the quantum group rather than from tabulated modular data.
evidence: Line 74: '[ ] Restricted quantum group u-bar_q(sl_3) (deferred — not needed for fusion verification).'

title: Phase 6n Wave 1b.5.11 restricted-form schema captures DMNO Theorem 5.2 only at the trivial-witness layer; biconditional central_charge_braided_iff_pseudoUnitary is vacuous-equivalence there
description: Wave 1b.5.11 ships a biconditional `central_charge_braided_iff_pseudoUnitary` and strict-weakening cross-bridges (.toPseudoUnitary / .toBraided) that establish the braided and pseudo-unitary hypothesis schemas are 'equivalent at this layer' — but the roadmap admits the equivalence holds only 'under universal trivial-witness' (the rank-1 trivialPreModular datum), and Stage 5.12 then breaks exactly this equivalence by requiring rank >= 2. So the 5.11 biconditional is an equivalence between two schemas that are only equivalent because a trivial witness satisfies both.
what_falls_short: The 5.11 architectural-equivalence biconditional does not capture the substantive DMNO 5.2 content (S-matrix factorization); it is an equivalence that holds for a degenerate reason and is invalidated by the very next refinement (5.12). The roadmap is honest about this in docstrings, but the headline biconditional is not load-bearing physics on its own.
remediation: The substantive content requires the rank>=2 strict form (5.12) plus the actual DMNO S-matrix-factorization argument, which is the literature-wall DMNO 2010 infrastructure. Ensure no bundle cites central_charge_braided_iff_pseudoUnitary as evidence of the DMNO characterization; cite only the strict 5.12 form with the conditional caveat.
evidence: Line 593: 'biconditional central_charge_braided_iff_pseudoUnitary capturing the architectural-equivalence at trivial-witness layer.' Line 597: 'the current ship is honest about this: docstrings ... explicitly label the trivial-witness layer'; Stage 5.12 'breaks the trivial-witness equivalence.'

title: Phase 6p headline (FaultTolerantUQC composition) is a CONDITIONAL theorem — the unconditional 'topological protection ⇒ AGP threshold' form is explicitly never proven
description: The capstone deliverable of all of Phase 6p ('the program's MTC infrastructure enables fault-tolerant universal QC') is the theorem `composition_conditional` / `FaultTolerantUQC`, which takes BOTH the density witness AND `p_eff < p_th_AGP` as EXPLICIT hypotheses. Roadmap G12: 'CONDITIONAL only (p_eff < p_th_AGP as explicit hypothesis); unconditional claim is community lore not theorem.' The module docstring states the unconditional form 'would require a separate primary-source-attested specialisation, deferred to Wave 1c+'. The decision is forced by a citation correction: the only paper cited for unconditional topological-protection ⇒ AGP (Bonesteel-DiVincenzo PRB 86 165113) 'explicitly disclaims any fault-tolerance threshold analysis'.
what_falls_short: The end-to-end stack theorem assumes its two hardest physics inputs rather than deriving either. As a fault-tolerance result it is a packaging/plumbing theorem (combines two assumed predicates), not a proof that Fibonacci-anyon braiding achieves fault tolerance. The headline reads as a substantive achievement but is gated on the very claim (threshold satisfaction) a reader would want established.
remediation: The unconditional topological-substrate threshold (Fibonacci-anyon braiding-error model ⇒ p_th) is genuine open research (Schotte-Zhu-Burgelman-Verstraete; requires Gács-Harrington CA renormalization or absent MTC threshold infrastructure). Keep the conditional framing; do NOT upgrade to unconditional. The achievable improvement is to derive p_eff from a concrete braiding-error model for a worked instance, narrowing the assumed surface.
evidence: Roadmap G12 RESOLVED → CONDITIONAL only (`p_eff < p_th_AGP` as explicit hypothesis); FaultTolerantUQC.lean:27-30 'the `p_eff < p_th_AGP` hypothesis is EXPLICIT, not derived. The unconditional form would require a separate primary-source-attested specialisation, deferred to Wave 1c+.'

title: FKLW Fibonacci SU(2) density (closure(range ρ)=univ) was never discharged constructively in-roadmap — left as a Cartan-classification gap, now resting on a tracked 'sound predicate v3/v4'
source: Phase6p_Roadmap.md R5.4/R5.5 RETIRED (lines 391-392, 469-485), R4.2.d.4.3.c+ DEFERRED (line 406), session log D4.3.d.1 (lines 987-1009); FKLW/CartanSubstrate.lean Wedge B headlines
description: The substantive density content for the concrete Fibonacci representation — proving `closure(Set.range ρ_Fib_SU2) = Set.univ` i.e. `H_Fib = ⊤` — is the load-bearing input to FKLW universality. The generic AA axiom that was supposed to deliver it (`aa_residual_interior_at_one_for_hom`) was found UNSOUND (SO(d)⊂SU(d) counterexample) and DELETED; the content became 'an EXPLICIT caller burden'. The roadmap's last session log (D4.3.d.1, 2026-05-14) leaves Cartan's closed-subgroup theorem / 1-parameter-subgroup / maximal-torus classification as 'Mathlib4 v4.29.0 gaps, ~300-1500 LoC' — an 'architectural deferral'. Current source (CartanSubstrate.lean) shows it was closed not by a full constructive Cartan proof but via 'Wedge B headline — H_Fib = ⊤ via sound predicate v3/v4' and 'from a single tracked [hypothesis]'.
what_falls_short: FKLW density for the project's concrete Fibonacci rep is gated on a tracked predicate (the 'sound predicate v3/v4'), not a from-scratch proof of subgroup-closure. The roadmap text understates this — it reads as multi-session in-progress Cartan work, while the actual close is a tracked-hypothesis. The 'unconditional density' claim downstream (e.g. Phase 6t base case) inherits this conditionality.
remediation: Either (a) ship the genuine Mathlib-grade Cartan closed-subgroup + classification-of-closed-subgroups-of-SU(2) infrastructure (substantial but in-scope per the project's dev rate — the structural ruleouts D1-D4.3.b are already done; the missing piece is converting idComponent≠⊥ to positive Lie dimension), then discharge the tracked predicate; or (b) keep it as a permanent tracked-Prop with the HBS citation and document it in PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md. Verify which 'sound predicate v3/v4' is actually load-bearing and that it is non-vacuous.
evidence: Line 485: substantive density 'is now an EXPLICIT caller burden, to be discharged via either: (a) Lean-internal HBS proof (multi-month, requires Cartan...not currently available); OR (b) scoped Fibonacci-specific HBS axiom...requires user sign-off'; CartanSubstrate.lean:733 '**Strengthened Wedge B headline — H_Fib = ⊤ from a single tracked**', :1040 'via sound predicate v3'.

title: Heavy native_decide reliance backs the FKLW density spanning data and Fibonacci universality headlines
source: Phase6p_Roadmap.md Wave 2b.3 (line 654), 2b.3.2 (lines 859-860), 3a.2.2 (line 892), 3a.2.3c; counts.json (199 nd occurrences); Fibonacci*.lean / FKLW/* grep
description: The spanning side of FKLW universality (`fib_quintet_spanning_data` ≈24 conjuncts, paper14 `su3_spanning_data` ≈8 conjuncts) and the gate-compilation Frobenius-distance discharges (Rouabah Hadamard 30-deep, CNOT 132/280-crossing, T-gate) are all proved by `native_decide` over cyclotomic number fields (K=ℚ(ζ₅,√φ), QCyc40Ext, QCyc80Ext). Grep confirms dense native_decide in FibonacciQuintetTrueRep (30), FibonacciSextetTrueRep (47), FibonacciBraiding (28), and the whole FKLW/RossSelinger/* synthesis stack. The roadmap repeatedly flags native_decide scalability as the dispositive risk ('expect native_decide cost > 30s per conjunct'; SU(8) 'may time out').
what_falls_short: native_decide places the spanning/approximation claims outside the standard kernel-only trust boundary (it trusts the compiler/runtime evaluation). For headline density and gate-approximation theorems this is the trusted-computing-base weakness; 6AO Track-3 demonstrated 4 KMM native_decides were eliminable via structural-lemma refactor, so a portion is in principle eliminable.
remediation: Audit which native_decide instances back headlines vs leaves. For the spanning-data conjuncts, attempt the 6AO Track-3 structural-lemma refactor (replace decide-over-field with explicit algebraic witnesses). For the Frobenius gate-distance discharges, a Python-precomputed exact literal + a smaller `decide`-checkable witness (already flagged as the fallback) reduces the per-discharge TCB. Where genuinely un-decide-able under Invariant #10, keep with justification comment (policy-tolerated).
evidence: Line 654: 'fib_quintet_density ... proved via BridgeProp.liftToDensity (axiom) + fib_quintet_spanning_data (≈24 native_decide spanning conjuncts)'; line 662 'Whether native_decide scales to SU(8)...without timeouts is not directly known...expect native_decide cost > 30s per conjunct'; line 892 'native_decide ... (or a smaller `decide`-checkable witness if native_decide times out)'.

title: Two Phase 6p axioms (bridge_axiom_FKLW, sk_axiom_Dawson_Nielsen) were shipped on DR-only authority before the policy required sign-off
description: Wave 2a.3 shipped `bridge_axiom_FKLW` and Wave 2b.2 shipped `sk_axiom_Dawson_Nielsen` as predicate-substrate AXIOMs on the strength of a deep-research recommendation alone, before the 2026-05-12 policy clarified that 'a DR returning with ship as predicate-substrate AXIOM is a recommendation, NOT a green-light.' The roadmap records they 'were shipped on DR-only authority and have post-hoc been flagged for substantive discharge.'
what_falls_short: Process weakness: load-bearing axioms entered the kernel on advisory authority, contra the project's locked-in 'NEVER ship a new project-local axiom without explicit user sign-off' rule. Both are now eliminated (Wave 2c/2d), but the episode is the documented root of the axiom-sign-off policy.
remediation: No action on the axioms themselves (counts.json shows axioms=0; both eliminated via Wave 2c/2d PR #17). The residual is documentation: ensure the roadmap's axiom-introduction rows are clearly marked HISTORICAL so a future reader does not treat them as live.
evidence: Line 237-240: 'The 2 Phase 6p axioms (bridge_axiom_FKLW, sk_axiom_Dawson_Nielsen) were shipped on DR-only authority and have post-hoc been flagged for substantive discharge via the new Waves 1c / 2c / 2d below.'; counts.json axioms=0 confirms resolution.

title: Generic AA bridge axiom (aa_residual_interior_at_one_for_hom) shipped UNSOUND and was live until a soundness audit caught it
description: During axiom-elimination, the replacement axiom `aa_residual_interior_at_one_for_hom` (intended to discharge FKLW density generically) was shown UNSOUND for d≥3 via an SO(d)⊂SU(d) counterexample: representations dense in SO(d) satisfy LieSpan+h_inf but their closure ⊊ SU(d), so the conclusion fails. The axiom was DELETED in commit f44c60d; R5.4/R5.5 (which were to compose toward discharging it) were RETIRED.
what_falls_short: A FALSE axiom was in the trusted kernel for a period — the worst category of axiom weakness (not merely unproven but unsound). It was caught by an internal R2 audit, not by the original review, indicating the axiom-introduction review missed a basic counterexample class (real-orthogonal subgroups of the unitary group).
remediation: Resolved (axiom deleted, project axiom count 0). Process lesson worth encoding: every density/closure axiom over SU(d) must be checked against the SO(d)/Sp(d) proper-closed-subgroup counterexamples before shipping. Confirm no downstream theorem still imports the deleted axiom's name.
evidence: Lines 493-500: 'The aa_residual_interior_at_one_for_hom axiom was shown UNSOUND via SO(d) counterexample (LieSpan + h_inf satisfied by dense-in-SO(d) braid reps but closure ⊂ SO(d) ⊊ SU(d), conclusion fails). The axiom is now DELETED (commit f44c60d); project axiom count 2 → 1.'

title: AGP threshold A_CNOT constant is the conservative secondary-source value, never re-pinned against the AGP PDF (gates G4/G5 PENDING)
description: The shipped AGP threshold certificate (ε₀ > 2.77e-5, Counting.lean A_CNOT=36000) uses a malignant-pair count derived from secondary-source consensus (Gottesman KITP talk, Cross-DiVincenzo-Terhal), NOT from the AGP primary PDF. The DR explicitly flags that 'original AGP used unverified Mathematica scripts, so Lean-internal recomputation is essential' and that A_CNOT 'must be re-derived from AGP PDF (likely Table V).' Gates G4 (§-number re-pin + A_CNOT) and G5 (Reichardt 2006 alternative-A comparison) remain PENDING; called 'a quality-improvement, not a Phase 6p blocker.'
what_falls_short: The numerical threshold value — the falsifiable content of the threshold theorem — rests on a count not independently verified against the primary source. If the conservative A_CNOT is wrong, the certificate constant moves. The theorem is structurally sound but its headline number is provenance-incomplete.
remediation: Re-pin AGP §-numbers and recompute A_CNOT (or the full malignant-pair enumeration) inside Lean against the AGP arXiv:quant-ph/0504218 PDF; compare with Reichardt 2006 LNCS 4051 for the smaller A constant. This is bounded primary-source verification work, not research.
evidence: Line 332: 'G4 / G5 ... PENDING; the AGP-rigorous A_CNOT=36000 in Counting.lean is the conservative-upper-bound value from secondary-source consensus'; line 124 'A_CNOT ... must be re-derived from AGP PDF (likely Table V§); original AGP used unverified Mathematica scripts, so Lean-internal recomputation is essential.'

title: T-gate braid-word never reaches the target precision ε ≤ 10⁻³ — random-search plateau at ~4.5e-2; GA-SK iteration NOT STARTED
description: The {H, CNOT, T} universal gate set is the substantive content of Wave 3a. The T-gate braid was shipped at L=17 ε≈7.5e-2 (spectral), then tightened 5.4× to L=46 ε≈1.38e-2, but the DEFAULT precision target ε ~ 10⁻³ is not met. The roadmap confirms 'Random-search saturation confirmed (frob²≈4.35e-4 at L=46, 5M trials); needs proper SK iteration with balanced commutator structure.' The proper fix (Long-Bourassa-Bell 2025 GA-SK, arXiv:2501.01746) is Wave 3a.2.3c-R5.6, marked ⏳ NOT STARTED.
what_falls_short: Of the three universal gates, the T-gate — the famously expensive one — is ~14× short of the stated default precision. The 'explicit universal gate compilation' deliverable is therefore precision-incomplete on its hardest gate; the {H,CNOT,T} set is not all at ε~10⁻³.
remediation: Implement the GA-SK (Long-Bourassa-Bell 2025) or KBS proper SK iteration in Python to reach ε≤10⁻³ (roadmap estimates ~multi-hour Python). A Kronecker-Weber parity obstruction in QCyc40Ext was already documented and worked around via QCyc80Ext, so the field substrate is ready.
evidence: Line 408: 'T-gate ε ≤ 10⁻³ via GA-SK (multi-session Python) ... NOT STARTED ... Random-search saturation confirmed (frob²≈4.35e-4 at L=46, 5M trials); needs proper SK iteration'; line 384 'ε ≤ 10⁻³ ALGORITHM-QUALITY remains separable sub-wave (random-search plateau ~4.5e-2).'

title: Quintet (4-strand SU(5)) spanning enumeration shipped only 8 of ~24 conjuncts; full SU(5) Lie-algebra spanning incomplete
description: Wave 2b.3 targets the 4-strand Fibonacci density in SU(5), with `fib_quintet_spanning_data` requiring ~24 native_decide conjuncts to match dim(𝔰𝔲(5))=24. The shipped state is 'PARTIAL SHIPPED (8/24 conjuncts)'. A later true-4-strand rep (FibonacciQuintetTrueRep.lean) reached an 8-conjunct closure after a P2 audit reduced 12→8, but 'Full 24-conjunct enumeration still NOT STARTED if needed.' The roadmap notes the closed-form minimal commutator-nesting depth d(n) 'is not in the literature' — only numerical search exists.
what_falls_short: The SU(5) (4-strand) extension of paper14's SU(3) density — the generalization that motivated Wave 2b — is not fully enumerated; whether the 8-conjunct close actually spans 𝔰𝔲(5) (vs the 24 the dimension count suggests) needs reconciling. The 'arbitrary-qudit FKLW lift' headline is established for the qutrit case and partially for the quintet.
remediation: Reconcile the audit-corrected 8-conjunct claim against the dim(𝔰𝔲(5))=24 requirement: either prove 8 suffices (the commutators of complex-symmetric matrices argument) with an explicit linear-independence-spanning lemma, or complete the 24-conjunct enumeration. Confirm `LieSpanProp 4 5 ρ_4` is actually discharged, not just the partial spanning data.
evidence: Line 369: 'Quintet spanning conjuncts via iterated commutators ⚠️ PARTIAL SHIPPED (8/24 conjuncts, 180 LoC)'; line 663 'Closed-form bound on minimal commutator-nesting depth d(n) for spanning 𝔰𝔲(F_{n+1}) is not in the literature ... numerical search ... only.'

title: BCH cubic constant K=320 is far from Dawson-Nielsen's K≤4; the optimal constant is ARCHITECTURE-BOUND and deferred indefinitely
description: The Solovay-Kitaev / AA-bridge proof chain proves `‖exp(iF)exp(iG)exp(-iF)exp(-iG) - exp(-[F,G])‖ ≤ 320·δ³`, whereas the Dawson-Nielsen primary source has the constant K≤4. R5.2c (optimize K≤4) is marked '⛔ ARCHITECTURE-BOUND (defer indefinitely)': the current 5/2 norm bound on T2pos is the binding constraint, and 'D-N's K≤4 requires a fundamentally different proof architecture' (MVT-style integral estimate). Tightening only gets to ~46 under δ≤1/2.
what_falls_short: The shipped bound is loose by ~80×. It is sufficient for the downstream existence/density consumers, so it is not load-bearing for any headline; but it is a documented quantitative gap vs the literature constant, deferred for effort/architecture reasons rather than because the tighter result is infeasible.
remediation: Accept K=320 (sufficient for all current consumers) unless a published-bundle claim needs the sharp constant. If sharp K is wanted, build the MVT-style integral substrate (~300-500 LoC per the roadmap) — genuine new analysis, not just more of the same proof.
evidence: Line 389: 'Optimize K ≤ 4 (D-N original) ⛔ ARCHITECTURE-BOUND (2026-05-14 analysis, defer indefinitely) ... D-N's K ≤ 4 requires a fundamentally different proof architecture ... Decision: defer; K=320 ... sufficient for R5.3 + all downstream consumers.'

title: Decoupling Lemma for d≥9 (full general-d FKLW density) and SU(8) 5-strand case explicitly sign-off-deferred / not built
description: Wave 2c.4d (AA Decoupling Lemma for d≥9, ~280 LoC + LieGroup specialUnitaryGroup ~200 LoC) is marked '🔒 EXPLICIT SIGN-OFF DEFER — not needed for project's d≤5 use; defer per user direction.' Separately, the 5-strand SU(8) case (63 spanning conjuncts) was deferred from Wave 2b to 'Wave 2c' with 'expect native_decide cost > 30s per conjunct', and is not shown shipped.
what_falls_short: The general-dimension FKLW density (the fully 'arbitrary qudit' claim that named the FKLW lift wave) is only established for d≤5; d≥9 decoupling and the SU(8) 5-strand instance are not built. The universality is concretely witnessed only at small d.
remediation: If the 'arbitrary-qudit' headline is to be claimed in any bundle, either ship the d≥9 Decoupling Lemma + SU(8) instance or scope the claim to d≤5 with the FKLW Theorem 0.1 citation carrying the general-n result. The d≤5 restriction is a deliberate user-directed scope, so this is mostly a framing-discipline item.

title: Phase 6b's entire cosmology contribution resolved to a structural NO-GO, not the predicted/affirmative physics — three vestigial-branch falsifications

The roadmap frames these as 'cleanly publishable either direction', but the program-level result is that the vestigial DE/inflation framing is falsified on all three axes (DESI, CMB-ℓ, inflation).
what_falls_short: The cosmology layer does not produce a positive emergent-physics result; it rules its own candidate out. This is by-design 'honest negative result' framing, not a hidden weakness — but it is a walkback from the affirmative correctness-push goals (large-ℓ spectrum fit, natural (n_s,r) window) to structural falsification, and the load-bearing physics claim (vestigial mode as viable cosmology) is negative.
remediation: None as a defect — the no-go is the legitimate result and is publishable. Ensure the bundle prose frames Phase 6b as a falsification of the vestigial cosmology branch (not as a deferred-positive), and that the joint 5y/6b NO-GO's two-witness structure is accurately stated. The underlying physics (whether ANY emergent-substrate branch gives viable cosmology) is the open research question.

Two structural failures formalized'; line 207 'CMB-ℓ admissibility fails (gradient-instability c_s² = -1/3)'; line 11 '6b.3 is flagged as highest-risk correctness-push — if vestigial inflation cannot reproduce Planck/BICEP (n_s, r), the framing is falsified.'

title: Phase 6AG named-channel diamond results are exact only for covariant channels; amp-damp is a non-tight two-sided bracket
description: Dephasing (=γ) and depolarizing (=p) get EXACT two-sided diamond distances via optimal dual witnesses (covariance makes maxEntangled optimal). what_falls_short: One of the three canonical single-qubit channels lacks an exact diamond-distance result — the bracket is wide and does not pin the value. The 'named-channel diamond bounds' deliverable is exact for 2 of 3.
remediation: To get the exact amp-damp diamond distance, build the optimal (non-covariant) dual witness or the maxEntangled-optimality argument for amp-damp specifically — a real but bounded theorem. The bracket is the acceptable first cut per the spec, so this is optional sharpening.

title: Phase6c W1: Zhitnitsky dark-energy prediction is 240× off observation (within 'three orders' claim)
description: Wave 6c.1's ρ_DE prediction at PDG Λ_QCD: 6.71×10⁻⁹ eV⁴ vs observed 2.8×10⁻¹¹ eV⁴ — a factor of ~240× discrepancy, framed as 'within 3-orders claim.' The headline 'topological dark energy' result over-predicts the observed dark-energy density by more than two orders of magnitude.
what_falls_short: A dark-energy mechanism that overshoots observation by 240× is not a quantitative success; the 'within three orders of magnitude' framing is a hedge that converts a large miss into an apparent agreement. The correctness-push theorem only proves the combined mechanism EXCEEDS observation (forcing one-mechanism commitment), not that either matches.
remediation: Physics-level: the 240× gap is intrinsic to the Zhitnitsky estimate at PDG scale, not a formalization defect. Ensure paper32 states the discrepancy plainly rather than as 'within three orders.' Closing the gap would require new physics (scale-dependent suppression), genuinely hard.

title: Phase6c W5: RT/Casini-Huerta knife-edge biconditional may be a defining-the-conclusion / within-tolerance result
source: Phase6c_Roadmap.md line 259 'rt_eq_kaulMajumdar_iff_trivial_reduced_area ... agree iff A = 4 G_N (reduced area = 1)'
description: The 6c.5 correctness-push centerpiece `rt_eq_kaulMajumdar_iff_trivial_reduced_area` states classical RT and Kaul-Majumdar 'agree iff reduced area = 1' with the gap being exactly (3/2)log(A/4G_N). At reduced area 1 the log term is log(1)=0, so the biconditional is the algebraically-forced statement that 'two formulas differing by (3/2)log(x) agree iff x=1' — definitional rather than physical content.
what_falls_short: The biconditional reads as a physics result but is the trivial observation that c·log(x)=0 ⟺ x=1. The substantive content is in the KM correction coefficient (3/2), which is asserted from W3, not in the iff itself. Borderline P-class triviality (defining-the-conclusion).
remediation: Verify under ruthless review whether the iff carries content beyond log(x)=0⟺x=1. If not, push the substantive load to the coefficient-derivation theorem and demote the biconditional to a corollary. The quantitative anchor at reduced-area=2 ((3/2)log2≈1.040) is a stronger falsifiable form.
evidence: Phase6c line 259 'classical RT and ... Kaul-Majumdar agree iff A = 4 G_N (reduced area = 1); elsewhere differ by (3/2)log(A/(4 G_N))'

title: Phase6c W3: EquivalencePrinciple required 12 retroactive strengthening theorems (worst discipline-failure baseline in the phase)
description: Wave 6c.3's post-discipline strengthening pass produced 12 retroactive theorems — the explicit 'baseline to beat' flagged in the agent instructions (line 27). The audit caught three failure classes in the first-pass EquivalencePrinciple statements: bundle redundancy (P2), missing quantitative content, and a phantom W4 cross-reference (P6). 12 first-pass theorems were thus trivial/redundant/under-specified before strengthening.
what_falls_short: A 12-theorem strengthening cost means the first-pass shipped a dozen statements that were redundant conjunct-bundles, qualitative-only, or had broken cross-module references — exactly the P2/P3/P6 anti-patterns the discipline targets. Resolved retroactively, but indicates the abstraction's first-pass was not bulletproof.
remediation: Already strengthened (24 final theorems, 12 retroactive added per line 160). The residual signal is that this wave is the discipline-failure high-water mark; future EP-style abstraction waves should apply the preemptive checklist harder. No open action.
evidence: Phase6c line 27 '6c.3 strengthening pass cost (2026-04-27): 12 retroactive theorems — that's the baseline to beat'; line 160 '24 substantive theorems (12 first-pass + 12 from post-discipline strengthening ... closing 3 audit findings: bundle redundancy P2, missing quantitative content, phantom W4 reference P6)'

title: Phase6AQ: both readout-envelope headlines are model-identified, not derived — assignment-error reading is literature-cited only
description: Both W1 (ReadoutRelaxationBound) and W2 (ThermalAssignmentFloor) explicitly ship a 'two-layer posture': the decay/occupancy formula + monotonicity + enclosure are Lean-verified, but 'the identification with device assignment error (QND-ideal, no re-excitation, decay-flips-outcome model)' and 'the assignment-floor reading (thermal population mistaken for signal)' stay literature-cited in the header, 'never conflated.' The avgAssignmentError_*_floor glue lemmas 'are intentionally thin' and carry only the uniform-prior model prefactor ½.
what_falls_short: The physically-interesting claim (these formulas bound real device readout error) is NOT formally established — only the underlying exp-decay / Boltzmann math is verified. The bridge to assignment error is an asserted modeling step. The headline 'assignment-error floor' is verified only modulo an unformalized device-identification assumption.
remediation: This is the established plobBound/coherence-ceiling posture (defensible). The model-identification step is genuinely a physics-modeling assumption, not formalizable from first principles without a full measurement-theory derivation. Keep two-layer disclosure in headers; ensure any product/paper consumer states it.
evidence: Phase6AQ line 80-81 'the decay formula + monotonicity + enclosure are the Lean-verified layer; the identification with device assignment error ... stays literature-cited in the module header — never conflated'; lines 24-28 'two avgAssignmentError_*_floor glue lemmas are intentionally thin'

title: Phase6AQ W2: stretch wave carried explicit honest-descope authorization (not triggered, but encoded)
description: W2 (ThermalAssignmentFloor) was specified as a stretch with an explicit descope clause: 'if the cleanly-provable form requires more PhysLib thermal substrate than exists, descope THIS wave honestly (ship W1 alone) rather than shipping a weakened statement — stretch means stretch.' In the event W2 shipped at full strength (status line 3-6), so the descope was not exercised.
what_falls_short: Not an actual shortfall — W2 shipped full-strength. Flagged only because the roadmap encodes a conditional descope path; under the project's 'no de-scope from effort' policy a substrate-insufficiency descope would be borderline. The 'shipped at full strength, no descope needed' header confirms it was not invoked.
remediation: No action — W2 shipped at full strength (20 thms, kernel-pure, p_th DERIVED from PhysLib twoState not defined). The descope clause was a contingency that did not fire.
evidence: Phase6AQ line 96 'descope THIS wave honestly (ship W1 alone) rather than shipping a weakened statement — stretch means stretch'; line 3-6 'W2 (stretch — shipped at full strength, no descope needed)'

title: Phase5v: PG+AGE source-of-truth flip (Wave 3) — core success criteria still unchecked at phase end
source: Phase5v_Roadmap.md lines 1171,1180,1168 (Success Criteria checkboxes), Wave 3a/3b/3c all-[ ]
description: Multiple end-of-phase success criteria remain unchecked: 'PG+AGE is source of truth; Python registries are regenerated artifacts — pending Wave 9f', 'validate.py CHECK 17 (graph parity) passes in CI — needs Wave 9f landed', 'Dashboard operates in SK_EFT_GRAPH_SOURCE=pg mode with Cypher-powered subgraph queries (Wave 9f)'. Wave 3a/3b/3c checklists are entirely unchecked; Wave 9f shipped the sync script + cypher endpoint but did NOT wire pg-mode routing (lines 686-687 deferred) or graph_source_parity CHECK.
what_falls_short: The graph-as-single-source-of-truth architectural goal — a Priority-1 design principle of the phase — is only partially realized. JSON remains the default source; PG is an optional mirror without a parity check enforcing they agree. The auto-invalidation IMPACTED_BY edges (Wave 4b) were also deferred.
remediation: Wire SK_EFT_GRAPH_SOURCE=pg to route trace/impact/subgraph endpoints; implement validate.py --check graph_source_parity (json-rebuild vs PG-read diff=0). Both explicitly deferred 'not blocking' but leave the SoT goal unmet. Pure tooling work.
evidence: Phase5v line 1171 'PG+AGE is source of truth ... pending Wave 9f'; line 687 '[ ] scripts/validate.py --check graph_source_parity ... deferred'; line 686 'Wire SK_EFT_GRAPH_SOURCE=pg ... Currently in-memory path ... this is cosmetic'

title: Phase5v: 13 April-review failure modes 'caught by a gate' success criterion unverified; readiness shows 15/15 RED
description: Top-line success criterion '[ ] All 13 April-review failure modes are caught by at least one ReadinessGate' is unchecked. The Stage-13 known-bad/known-good validation criterion is also unchecked. The initial 165-gate run showed 15/15 papers RED (all have ≥1 P1 blocked), and CHECK 18 was left 'WARN-only during rollout (expected to flag all 15 papers red until remediation).'
what_falls_short: The process-fix phase's core promise — that the next review round catches the issues before ship — is not demonstrated; the gates were built and surfaced 15/15 RED but the failure-mode-coverage proof and the known-bad regression test were never run. The submission gate is WARN-only, so it does not actually block anything yet.
remediation: Run the explicit known-bad (pre-fix paper7 TPF-citation) vs known-good regression to confirm gate coverage of all 13 modes; escalate CHECK 18 from WARN to FAIL once papers reach green. Tooling + verification work. Note: project has since moved to AttributionContentSweep (per MEMORY) which cleared 18/18 bundles GREEN, so this may be partially superseded.
evidence: Phase5v line 1165 '[ ] All 13 April-review failure modes are caught by at least one ReadinessGate'; line 391 'Paper state: 15/15 RED'; line 418 'WARN-only during rollout ... escalate to FAIL when papers start hitting green'

title: Phase5v: claims-reviewer Class TN false-positive collapsed two distinct theorems (would have introduced a regression)
description: The Wave 10h retrofit process audit surfaced that the new Class TN (theorem-name reference drift) finding logic produced a false positive: paper2's `firstOrder_correction_zero_iff_no_dissipation` was reported as 'not in lean_deps.json' when it IS present at line 65238, and 'the Closest match heuristic prescription would have collapsed two distinct theorems and introduced a regression.' Also a FixPropagation gap: fixer agents patching .tex don't refresh claims_review.json::blocking_issues (paper3/4/6 confirmed stale).
what_falls_short: The verification machinery itself (Class TN auto-catch, the headline Wave 10 deliverable) had a defect that, if its auto-fix prescription had been applied, would have merged two distinct Lean theorems — a self-inflicted regression from the QA tooling. The FixPropagation gap means consumers can see stale findings.
remediation: Two recommended fixes filed but unimplemented: (1) sentence_state.py mark-issue-fixed subcommand to refresh blocking_issues on .tex patch; (2) TN-finding self-check via deterministic grep before queueing for fixer dispatch. Both are cheap; status as of roadmap is 'recommended,' not done.
evidence: Phase5v line 1011 'paper2 firstOrder_correction_zero_iff_no_dissipation reported as not in lean_deps.json when it IS present at line 65238; the Closest match heuristic ... would have collapsed two distinct theorems and introduced a regression'; line 1010 FixPropagation gap

title: Phase5v: Wave 10 prose-audit success criteria (sentence coverage, Paper 12/16 targets) all unchecked
description: The Wave 10 success criteria are predominantly unchecked: '[ ] Every prose unit ... has a Sentence node', '[ ] Paper 12 reaches 100% human_verified terminal state ... submission readiness check passes', '[ ] Paper 16 drops from 30% UNGROUNDED to baseline ~9%', '[ ] Paper 20 re-audit auto-catches ScalarRungInterpretation theorem-rename drift via Class TN'. The retrofit (10h) ran agent passes leaving every sentence at agent_proposed (~0% human-verified — 'the verification backlog'), so the human-ratification terminal states were never reached.
what_falls_short: The phase's headline UX deliverable (per-sentence 100% verification with freshness watchdog) is built but not driven to completion on any paper. Paper 16's 30%-UNGROUNDED problem is surfaced but not remediated; the freshness-watchdog 'highest-value capability' has no paper actually cleared through it.
remediation: Drive Paper 12 to 100% human_verified via the new UI (bulk-verify-unchanged); re-run agents on Paper 16 to confirm UNGROUNDED drop. Machinery is shipped; this is execution backlog. May be partially superseded by the later AttributionContentSweep work per MEMORY.
evidence: Phase5v line 1005 'Initial state: every sentence agent_proposed. Coverage ribbon will show ~0% human-verified across all papers — the verification backlog'; lines 1197-1199 unchecked Paper 12/16/20 criteria

title: Phase5v: Wave 9c name_deps extraction is gated OFF by default, leaving proof-dependency graph incomplete
description: Full proof-to-proof dependency edges (USES, from name_deps_project) are gated behind EXTRACT_NAME_DEPS env var defaulting OFF because the extraction takes ~10-12 min ('Expr.getUsedConstantsAsSet on elaborated tactic-generated proof terms is the bottleneck'). Default lean_deps.json therefore has only axiom-closure + USED_BY edges, not full name_deps. The Lean graph has 2820+ LeanTheorem nodes but the default graph omits proof-dependency edges entirely.
what_falls_short: The knowledge graph's proof-dependency layer — needed for true 'who uses this theorem' provenance and for Class HD transitive walks — is off by default for performance, so the default graph the dashboard/validate sees is structurally incomplete. A perf descope, justified by effort, of a correctness-relevant feature.
remediation: Deferred per-module incremental ExtractDeps refactor (Wave 11+, '~1-2d') would amortize to O(changed-modules) and make full extraction cheap enough to default ON. Until then the graph's proof-dep layer is opt-in. Pure tooling.
evidence: Phase5v line 662 'Feature gated behind EXTRACT_NAME_DEPS env var. Default = OFF'; line 608 'ExtractDeps.lean with name_deps_project takes >5 min ... users run the extractor manually and accept the wait'; line 1048 'per-module incremental ExtractDeps refactor ... Larger surgery (~1-2d), not blocking'

title: Phase5v Wave 1b: Paper 15 count-literal retrofit incomplete; Papers 1-14 retrofit deferred to a later sweep
description: Wave 1b wired counts.json macros but left residuals: Paper 15 has '3 literals remain (25 theorems, 33 sorry, 200 theorems growth-rate heuristic); deferred pending Paper 15 full rewrite', the full Paper 15 rewrite is '[ ] deferred post-5v (paper content is broadly stale)', and 'Retrofit Papers 1-14 — not blocking this wave; CHECK 17 WARN surfaces remaining 64 literals; cleanup scheduled as a cross-cutting sweep after Wave 5'.
what_falls_short: The 'counts.json is single source of truth for every count reference in every paper' goal (marked done) is overstated — 64+ stale count literals across Papers 1-14 plus 3 in Paper 15 remain hardcoded, surfacing only as WARNs. The 'broadly stale' Paper 15 is acknowledged but not rewritten.
remediation: Mechanical sweep: replace the 64 WARN-flagged count literals with \input{counts.tex} macros across Papers 1-14; rewrite Paper 15. Cheap; the macro infra already exists (Paper 15 is the reference impl). Note current counts.json shows 0 sorry / 12463 thm, so any 'N sorry' literal is now definitionally stale.
evidence: Phase5v line 201 '3 literals remain (25 theorems ... 33 sorry ... 200 theorems growth-rate heuristic); deferred pending Paper 15 full rewrite'; line 203 'Retrofit Papers 1-14 — not blocking ... remaining 64 literals'

title: Phase6z: 6z.D multi-dimensional Kronecker-Weyl deferred (acceptable — first flow does not need it)
description: Wave 6z.D (multi-dimensional Kronecker-Weyl on 𝕋^d for simultaneous-flow recovery) is DEFERRED, 're-evaluate post-6z.5', ~600 LoC. The phase headline (literal ⟨H,S,CNOT,CCZ⟩ dense in SU(8), T-free) shipped without it because the BEST-case Gate-2 result (Clifford orbit spans 63-dim by itself) made simultaneous flows unnecessary.
what_falls_short: Genuinely not a shortfall to the shipped headline — the deferred wave was a contingency only needed if simultaneous-flow recovery were later required. Flagged for completeness since it is an explicitly-deferred objective, but the SU(8) density headline is unconditional and kernel-pure without it.
remediation: No action needed for the 6z headline. Only build 6z.D if a future phase needs simultaneous multi-torus flows; the single-flow + Clifford-spread route is complete and verified (cliffordCCZLiteral_dense, kernel-pure, Stage-13 GREEN).
evidence: Phase6z line 91 '6z.D Deferred: multi-dimensional Kronecker–Weyl on 𝕋^d (only if simultaneous-flow recovery is later needed; not needed for the first flow). re-evaluate post-6z.5'; line 165 '6z.D ⏳ DEFERRED'

title: Phase6z: headline-from-witness gap discovered post-ship (density shipped but named SK headline initially missing)
description: A post-assessment closeout correction reveals the bb9aae0 ship 'delivered the density (cliffordCCZLiteral_dense) but NOT the named headline SolovayKitaevHeadline_FreeGroup_SUd for the literal alphabet that this wave's title promised — it stopped at the witness, and the only shipped ...headline_of_witness consumer is hardcoded to the universal Clifford+CCZ+T set.' The gap was then closed in a new leaf module.
what_falls_short: The wave initially claimed Stage-13 GREEN / COMPLETE while the title-promised named headline was not actually instantiated for the literal alphabet — the adversarial review passed on the density but missed that the named SK compilation guarantee was absent. A real (caught, then fixed) overclaim-vs-delivered gap.
remediation: Already closed: CliffordCCZSU8LiteralHeadline.lean ships cliffordCCZLiteral_solovayKitaev_headline_unconditional, kernel-pure (verified via lean_verify, no native_decide). 'Trivial wrap, no new math.' No open action; the lesson is that Stage-13 should check title-promise vs shipped-symbol.
evidence: Phase6z line 164 'the bb9aae0 ship delivered the density ... but NOT the named headline ... it stopped at the witness ... Gap closed in new leaf CliffordCCZSU8LiteralHeadline.lean'

title: Phase6z: as-shipped seed differs from the pre-implementation DR2 plan (DR1 trace route used, DR2 eigenblock retained off-path)
description: An 'AS-SHIPPED CORRECTION (2026-05-30, post adversarial review)' documents that the plan-of-record specified the DR2 seed g₀=(H⊗H⊗H·CCX)² with eigenvalues (−3±i√7)/4, but the shipped proof used the DR1 seed g_lit=CCZ·H⊗H⊗H via the trace route (tr=u·1/√2 ∉ 𝒪). The DR2 eigenvalue route is retained as 'a correct, retained ALTERNATIVE obstruction, off the critical path — not operative.' DR2's stated (3±i√7)/4 also had a Re-λ sign error corrected by Gate-1.
what_falls_short: Not a correctness shortfall (both irrationality skeletons are valid and the trace route is kernel-pure) — but the roadmap's own catalog table (6z.1 row) still describes the non-operative DR2 skeleton as if it were the shipped core, a documentation drift the correction note flags. Minor internal-consistency issue, not a proof weakness.
remediation: The as-shipped note already reconciles plan vs ship. To fully clean: update the 6z.1 catalog row to lead with the trace route (operative) and mark the eigenvalue route as the alternative. Documentation-only.
evidence: Phase6z line 59 'the shipped proof used the DR1 seed, not DR2 ... composes more cleanly ... DR2 eigenvalue route ... is a correct, retained ALTERNATIVE obstruction, off the critical path — not operative'; line 62 'Wave-6z.1 table row below ... is the alternative skeleton'

The phase shipped its read/audit surfaces but left the write-back and external-consumption halves incomplete.
