title: dai_freed_spin_z4 is a tautological placeholder, not the ℤ₁₆ cobordism classification it stands in for
source: Phase5b_Roadmap.md §4 (lines 231); Phase5x_Roadmap.md Wave 2b Track Y (lines 296-298); verified live in lean/SKEFTHawking/Z16AnomalyComputation.lean:51
description: The theorem `dai_freed_spin_z4 : ∃ (φ : ZMod 16 ≃ ZMod 16), Function.Bijective φ` is discharged by `⟨Equiv.refl _, Equiv.bijective _⟩`. The roadmap itself states it is 'Not a proof of the Dai-Freed cobordism classification Ω₅^{Spin^{ℤ₄}} ≅ ℤ₁₆ — that requires cobordism theory not in Mathlib.' Wave 2b Track Y reaffirms 'real cobordism formalization deferred to Phase 6.'
what_falls_short: The entire ℤ₁₆ anomaly program (Papers 8/9/10/17 hidden-sector headline) rests on the identification Ω₅^{Spin^ℤ₄}≅ℤ₁₆, which is taken as an EXTERNAL hypothesis, not a Lean-verified fact. The Lean theorem named after it proves only that some bijection ZMod 16→ZMod 16 exists (trivially true). Papers must disclose the cobordism identification as external.
remediation: Either (a) formalize the Smith-homomorphism/bordism computation Ω₅^{Spin×ℤ₄}→ℤ₁₆ (Mathlib-absent, research-grade), or (b) keep as an explicitly-labelled tracked-Prop external hypothesis with disclosure in every consuming paper. The roadmap already mandates (b) for Papers 8/9/10.
evidence: Z16AnomalyComputation.lean:51 `theorem dai_freed_spin_z4 : ∃ (φ : ZMod 16 ≃ ZMod 16), Function.Bijective φ`; Phase5b §4: 'same tautological structure as z16_classification. Not a proof of the Dai-Freed cobordism classification ... Papers 8, 9, 10 must describe the ℤ₁₆ cobordism identification as an external hypothesis, not a Lean-verified fact.'

title: sixteen_convergence_full enumerates rather than mathematically unifies the four '16's; three of four conjuncts are tautology/echo/retype
description: `RokhlinBridge.sixteen_convergence_full` is documented as 'enumerates (does not mathematically unify) the four appearances of 16 — SM Weyl count, ℤ₁₆ bordism modulus, Rokhlin divisor, Kitaev 16-fold way.' Module docstring discloses 'three of four conjuncts are either arithmetic tautologies, hypothesis echoes, or retypes of the first.' Rokhlin's theorem enters as a hypothesis, not a proof.
what_falls_short: The headline '16 convergence' claim (cobordism = Rokhlin = Kähler-Dirac = Kitaev = 16-fold way, Phase5b §9) is a P2-style redundant-conjunct bundle plus a P5 hypothesis-echo: the mathematical connection between the four 16s is external (Wang 2024 via Smith homomorphism, not formalized). Paper 10's section was already softened to 'formally recorded' not 'formally verified convergence'.
remediation: Note that 5q.B (resume memory) is actively pursuing the genuine unconditional 16∣σ via van der Blij/classification — that is the substantive replacement. Until it lands, keep Paper 10's 'formally recorded' framing. The Smith-homomorphism unification is Mathlib-absent.
evidence: Phase5b §7: 'sixteen_convergence_full: enumerates (does not mathematically unify) ... three of four conjuncts are either arithmetic tautologies, hypothesis echoes, or retypes of the first. Mathematical connection between the four 16s is external (Wang 2024 via Smith homomorphism — not formalized).'

title: DrinfeldEquivalence.lean ships 4 of 12 theorems as `True := trivial` placeholders; full categorical equivalence Z(Vec_G)≅Rep(D(G)) never constructed
source: Phase5b_Roadmap.md §3 Wave 3 (lines 198-208), §2/§9 deferred (lines 201-206, 389); verified live in lean/SKEFTHawking/DrinfeldEquivalence.lean:99,109,179
description: Wave 3's `DrinfeldEquivalence.lean` records '4 of 12 theorems are `True := trivial` placeholders recording what SHOULD hold, not constructive proofs.' The monoidal/braided structure-preservation and the gauge-emergence bridge are stated as `True := trivial`. The full categorical functor Center(Vec_G) ⥤ ModuleCat(DG k G), the equivalence proof, and the braided monoidal functor structure are all 'Deferred to Phase 6 (~30-50 thms).'
what_falls_short: The publication claim 'First formal connection between Mathlib's abstract Center and concrete gauge emergence' (Phase5b §9) overstates: the algebraic bijection (half-braiding ↔ D(G)-module) is proved and concrete Z/2,S₃ examples verified, but the abstract categorical equivalence — the actual Z(Vec_G)≅Rep(D(G)) statement — is not constructed. Live source still carries the trivial placeholders.
remediation: Construct the Functor.mk object/morphism maps and natural isomorphisms (~30-50 theorems, 'deep Mathlib plumbing' per roadmap — i.e. just-more-Lean-work, achievable here). Or replace the `True := trivial` placeholders with honestly-named `H_*` tracked Props.
evidence: DrinfeldEquivalence.lean:99 `True := trivial  -- Statement: the monoidal structures correspond`; :109 braided; :179 Müger. Phase5b §3: 'NOTE: 4 of 12 theorems are True := trivial placeholders ... The algebraic core is proved; categorical wrapping is not.'

title: Phase5x dark-sector headlines (Paper 17) are MCC machine-checked classifications, not derivations — multiple defining-the-conclusion / SemanticTautology theorems
source: Phase5x_Roadmap.md Wave 10 BLOCKER table (lines 952-959) + RECOMMENDED (line 980); verified live in lean/SKEFTHawking/FractonDarkMatter.lean:226,234
description: Adversarial review (Wave 10) flagged 5 SemanticTautology theorems in Paper 17: `fracton_bullet_sigma_zero` (=rfl on a hardcoded `def sigma_eff_isolated_fracton := 0`), `phase5x_candidates_viability_matrix` (consistency on hardcoded flags), `emergent_gravity_dm_invisible_collective` (decide on hardcoded σ_DD lookup), `torsion_channels_distinct_sources_distinct` (enum distinctness), `empirical_hook_ranking_strict` (hand-chosen priorities), plus `lambda_magnitude_ratio_exact` (T/T=1 tautology). All 'FIXED via paper reframe' — relabelled Derived→MCC — NOT via Lean change.
what_falls_short: These are textbook defining-the-conclusion / decide-able-pairwise-distinctness / within-band tautology anti-patterns (CLAUDE.md P3-P5). The physics content lives in hand-assigned constants/tables sourced to deep research, not in the proofs. The proposed `SemanticTautology` graph-extractor to mechanically guard this is explicitly NOT implemented ('Wave 10b or Phase 5v should schedule'). The MCC designation is carried only by human-readable honesty-table prose.
remediation: Implement the `SemanticTautology` extractor (P1, sketched in Wave 10 §QI) and gate Paper 17. For genuine load-bearing content, push the substance into derivations (e.g. derive the empirical-hook ranking from a `detectability` scoring function over Euclid/Roman S/N — flagged as the Phase 6 target). All are 'just more Lean work', achievable here.
evidence: FractonDarkMatter.lean:226 `def sigma_eff_isolated_fracton : ℝ := 0`; :234 `theorem fracton_bullet_sigma_zero : sigma_eff_isolated_fracton = 0 := rfl`. Phase5x Wave 10 5.1: '`fracton_bullet_sigma_zero` = rfl on hardcoded constant ... Honesty table row updated from Derived to MCC.'

title: SFDMMergerForecast.lean headline merger theorems rely on native_decide (all_canonical_mergers_supersonic + S/N witnesses)
source: Phase5x_Roadmap.md Wave 5 deliverables (line 459); verified live in lean/SKEFTHawking/SFDMMergerForecast.lean:237-255,417-439
description: The Paper 17 'money plot' headline rests on Lean theorems proved by native_decide: `all_canonical_mergers_supersonic`, `pandora_highest_mach`, `macs_j0025_lowest_mach`, and the five decidable S/N witnesses (Bullet/Roman at N=18/30/50, A520/Euclid). FractonDarkMatter.lean similarly carries 10 native_decide occurrences for its viability witnesses.
what_falls_short: These back a HEADLINE (Rank-1 empirical prediction, Paper 17 money plot) yet sit on the compiler's native code path rather than the kernel. Per project policy native_decide is tolerated only where genuinely un-decide-able under Invariant #10; 6AO Track-3 showed KMM native_decides were eliminable via structural-lemma refactor. These are ℚ-arithmetic comparisons (Mach numbers, S/N thresholds) that are plausibly `decide`/`norm_num`-eliminable.
remediation: Replace native_decide with `decide`/`norm_num` for the ℚ threshold comparisons (the values are small rationals: Mach >1, S/N ≥ 3σ). Where `decide` times out, extract the comparison as a `norm_num`-backed lemma per the preemptive-strengthening discipline. Mirrors the 6AO Track-3 elimination pattern.
evidence: SFDMMergerForecast.lean:237 `rcases h with h|h|h|h|h <;> (subst h; native_decide)`; :417 `unfold snr_bullet_roman; native_decide`. counts.json: 199 native_decide occurrences project-wide.

title: ADW cosmological-constant / KV dark-energy claim walked back from 'explains DESI' to 'Level D tension' (2.9–4.4σ excluded)
description: The original Track-A headline 'Klinkhamer-Volovik oscillating vacuum compatible with DESI DR2 evolving DE' was retracted post-W1b: KV predicts (w₀,wₐ)=(-1,0), excluded at 2.9σ (Pantheon+) to 4.4σ (DESY5). 'No phantom crossing is possible in KV.' RVM with Volovik-motivated ν~10⁻¹²² is 10⁸² orders too small. Paper 17 instructed to 'withdraw KV explains DESI; keep KV predicts Λ magnitude.'
what_falls_short: The surviving empirical hook is only the Λ-magnitude coincidence ρ_vac~(2.8 meV)⁴ vs (2.3 meV)⁴ (~20% accuracy), and even that O(1) prefactor was later disclosed as undisclosed-tuning (Wave 10 finding 3.2 removed the '20% agreement' language, deferring the prefactor to Phase 6). The ADW CC mechanism is confirmed 'heuristic, not a theorem' (the self-tuning e→e_eq step is the central unproven gap).

Keep the honest 'reframed not solved' assessment; the QCD-topological-DE bridge (Van Waerbeke-Zhitnitsky) is the only DESI-compatible connection and is third-party, not derived here.

evidence: Phase5l Wave 2 header '(IN PROGRESS)' vs footer 'W2a+W2b+W2c ALL COMPLETE'; 'Self-contained proof: braid algebra data → Lie algebra generation → density.'

title: W3 Kirby-Taylor theorem (Ω₄^Pin⁺ ≅ ℤ/16) has a documented fallback leaving tracked-Prop #1 undischarged
description: W3 is 'the most ambitious wave' shipping the substantive proof of the iso. It carries an explicit fallback: 'if W3 substantive proof proves intractable, ship W1+W2+W4+integration only and leave #1 as a tracked Prop with the substantive substrate behind it.' A user-authorization gate is OPEN for Path α vs Path β, and a CONDITIONAL gate fires at W3 close if intractable. Session logs only show 'W3-minimal — Path γ Kirby-Taylor generator-and-relations skeleton', not a full discharge.
what_falls_short: The flagship-F headline claim ('first proof-assistant Pin⁺ bordism formalization', Ω₄^Pin⁺ ≅ ℤ/16) is conditional on W3's full proof, which the session log shows was only shipped as a 'skeleton'. The audit verdict (line 763) keeps #1 IsKirbyTaylorPinPlusBordism as a legitimate KEEP tracked Prop — i.e. the iso is NOT constructively proved; it's `Nonempty (Omega4PinPlus ≃+ ZMod 16)` asserted.
remediation: Complete W3.3 (Path α η-route or Path β generators-and-relations / Pin⁺-Spin long exact sequence). The roadmap itself rates this the highest-variance wave; ground truth axioms=0 means it currently lives as a tracked Prop, consistent with 'KEEP'. Headline framing must stay 'tracked-hypothesis' until the iso is constructive.
evidence: 'if W3 substantive proof proves intractable, W3 can ship the substrate-and-axiomatized-conclusion form ... with the KT theorem itself remaining a tracked Prop'; session log: 'W3-minimal — Path γ Kirby-Taylor generator-and-relations skeleton'; audit: #1 IsKirbyTaylorPinPlusBordism body `Nonempty (Omega4PinPlus ≃+ ZMod 16)` → verdict KEEP as legitimate tracked Prop.

title: Phase 6r-prime self-audit found 12 ADDITIONAL P5/P4/P3 anti-patterns AFTER 'GREEN-with-advisories' close — Session 1 '11 of 12 discharged' was vacuous
description: After Phase 6r-prime closed at 'GREEN-with-advisories' with an '11 of 12 discharged' summary, a self-audit found the close was 'too optimistic'. The honest tracked-Prop count is 2 (KT 1990 + DMNO 2010), 'down from 12 nominal.' Of the 12, 5 are DELETE P5/P2 anti-pattern bundles (#4,#5,#7,#8,#12), 3 RESTRUCTURE P5-alias, 1 RECLASSIFY. A deeper file-by-file audit found 12 MORE anti-patterns (N1-N24): aliases like `IsModularBulk B := Is3DTQFT B`, `IsGapped C := True` (literal True), `Z24_and_Z16_are_disjoint_sectors := 24 ≠ 16` (decidable P3), and a `class PinPlusStructure : Prop` with empty body (any type instantiable with ⟨⟩).
what_falls_short: The 'discharge' counted any case where a discharge `theorem` exists in the file, conflating P5 identity-wrapper discharge (`AddEquiv.refl _`) and P2 bundle-restatement with TRUE substantive discharge. Multiple shipped predicates are aliases/tautologies dressed as physics content; a literal `True` body and an empty-body Prop class are placeholder-True equivalents.
remediation: Execute the B1-B12 + A1-A5 + M2-M5 remediation queue (DELETE aliases, RESTRUCTURE to substantive S-matrix/Müger-center predicates, fix `IsGapped`/`PinPlusStructure` bodies, fix N14 docstring bug). All rated small-to-medium LoC. Verify against current counts.json which shows only 7 tracked-Props total project-wide — confirm which survived.
evidence: 'Post-audit honest tracked-Prop count: 2 (KT 1990 + DMNO 2010), down from 12 nominal'; 'This deep file-by-file audit finds 12 ADDITIONAL anti-patterns missed by the prior partial audit'; N4 `IsGapped C := True`; N9 `class PinPlusStructure (M) : Prop` empty body 'Any type instantiable with ⟨⟩'; N6 `Z24_and_Z16_are_disjoint_sectors := Fintype.card (ZMod 24) ≠ Fintype.card (ZMod 16)`.

title: Phase 6r-prime remediation queue (A1-A5, B1-B12, M2-M5, M-R) is ⏳ NOT STARTED / IN PROGRESS — the honest-discharge work is itself deferred
description: The remediation items that would convert the vacuous discharges into substantive ones are mostly NOT STARTED: A1 (restructure #2 via Pontryagin), A2 (DELETE 5 antipattern Props), A3 (restructure #11 dark-sector), A4 (reclassify #10), A5 (object-level toric-code, blocked by M2), M2 (Drinfeld biproducts, IN PROGRESS via 'signHalfBraiding template'), M3 (RP⁴ — Layer B-4 chart atlas remaining), M4, M5, M-R (pessimistic fresh review). Total Path A-strict LoE ~8-20 sessions, ~2k-4k LoC delta.
what_falls_short: The phase declared close but the work to make its content honest is open. M2 is 'IN PROGRESS (data + iso shipped; HalfBraiding axioms via signHalfBraiding template)'; M3 Layer B-4 (full IsLocalHomeomorph + ChartedSpace + IsManifold, the load-bearing smooth-manifold content for RP⁴) is the 'heaviest segment ~300-500 LoC bespoke' and remains a carryover. M-R 'pessimistic fresh adversarial review' never ran.
remediation: Execute the documented Path A-strict sequence (A1→A4 parallel → M2 → A5 → M3 → M4 → M5 → M-R). All items are 'just more Lean work' at small-medium LoC per the roadmap's own estimates; none is a literature wall except the APS/MTC upstream pieces already carved out.
evidence: Table rows: A1 '⏳ NOT STARTED', A2 '⏳ NOT STARTED', M2 '⏳ IN PROGRESS (data + iso shipped; HalfBraiding axioms via signHalfBraiding template)', M-R '⏳ NOT STARTED'; 'M3 Layer B-4 (chart atlas + ChartedSpace + IsManifold). Bespoke ~300-500 LoC.'

title: DMNO biconditional (#6) KEEPs as a tracked Prop — W2.3 may stop at 'statement substantive, both directions tracked-Prop'
source: Phase6r_prime_Roadmap.md W2 tension (line 282); audit verdict #6 (line 768); risk axes (line 302)
description: Tracked Prop #6 `IsDMNOBiconditional B := Is3DTQFTBraided B ↔ HasLagrangianAlgebra B` is a KEEP. The W2 tension note states the substantive ship 'may stop at the biconditional STATEMENT being meaningful, with the forward/backward directions still requiring tracked-Prop hypotheses.' Risk axes: 'DMNO 2010 substantive proof is mathematically deep; W2.3 may need to stop at statement substantive, both directions tracked-Prop rather than full discharge.'
what_falls_short: The Witt-triviality ⟺ Lagrangian-algebra correspondence (the D4 'Drinfeld centre' headline content and the closure of Phase 6r adversarial-review BLOCKER-2) is asserted as a biconditional Prop whose two directions are not constructively proved — forward direction 'requires constructing Lagrangian algebra in MTC; no project MTC typeclass.'
remediation: Discharge the forward direction (construct the Lagrangian algebra in a non-degenerate braided fusion category) requires real MTC infrastructure Mathlib lacks. Legitimately KEEP per the project's 3-criterion bar (DMNO 2010, well-established, ≥1yr cost). Bundle D4 must label it tracked-hypothesis, not proved.
evidence: audit #6: 'genuine biconditional, both sides non-trivial; All 3 criteria MET (DMNO 2010; forward direction requires constructing Lagrangian algebra in MTC; no project MTC typeclass) → KEEP'; W2 tension: 'the substantive ship may stop at the biconditional STATEMENT being meaningful, with the forward/backward directions still requiring tracked-Prop hypotheses.'

title: N14 documentation bug: shipped Pin⁺ obstruction equation `w₂ = w₁²` is WRONG (is the Pin⁻ obstruction); correct is `w₂ = 0`
source: Phase6r_prime_Roadmap.md Audit Section V N14 (lines 666-667); B12 (line 735)
description: The shipped `PinPlusManifold4.lean:78-82` docstring states 'Pin⁺ structure data is w₂(M) = w₁(M)²' which the audit flags as the WRONG OBSTRUCTION EQUATION: per Lawson-Michelsohn II.1.7 + Kirby-Taylor convention, the Pin⁺ obstruction is `w₂(M) = 0`; `w₂ = w₁²` is the Pin⁻ obstruction. For RP⁴ this matters: w₂(TRP⁴)=0 is exactly why RP⁴ admits Pin⁺ and generates Ω₄^Pin⁺ ≅ ℤ/16.
what_falls_short: A mathematically incorrect characterizing equation was shipped in the substrate that underpins the entire Pin⁺/RP⁴/ℤ16 headline. If the Lean content (not just docstring) encoded `w₂=w₁²`, the RP⁴ Pin⁺ instance would be on the wrong condition. B12 marks it 'docs only ~10 LoC' but the M4-narrow ship must carry the corrected `w₂=0`.
remediation: Fix docstring to `w₂ = 0` (B12); ensure the M4-narrow `StiefelWhitney.lean` `IsPinPlusObstruction M := w 2 = 0` encodes the corrected equation (session log line 929 indicates M4-narrow shipped with 'post-B12 corrected obstruction equation' — verify on disk it's the corrected form, not the buggy one).
evidence: 'WRONG OBSTRUCTION EQUATION. ... Pin⁺ obstruction is w₂(M) = 0 (NOT w₂ = w₁²). ... The equation w₂ = w₁² is the Pin⁻ obstruction.'

evidence: '130 theorems + 1 axiom (zero sorry)'; '130+1 axiom (zero sorry), 737 tests'; ground truth: axioms=0; CLAUDE.md: 'pre-Phase-6p axiom count was 1 (gapped_interface_axiom)'.

title: Phase 4 Wave 1B chirality-wall result is conditional on the unproven 4+1D gapped-interface conjecture
description: The chirality-wall synthesis (Paper-class output: does TPF evade Golterman-Shamir?) is structured around step 4: 'Identify the 4+1D gapped interface conjecture as the critical unproven assumption' and step 5: 'Assess: if the conjecture is proven, does the chirality wall fall?' The whole 'does the chirality wall fall' conclusion is gated on an unproven conjecture.
what_falls_short: The headline physics claim (TPF construction evades the GS no-go → chirality wall falls) is contingent: it only follows IF the gapped-interface conjecture holds. Stated as conditional, which is honest, but it is a hypothesis-gated headline, not an unconditional result.
remediation: The gapped-interface conjecture is a genuine open problem in lattice chiral gauge theory (the TPF / Golterman-Shamir frontier). Keep as tracked hypothesis (TPFConjecture). The conditional framing is correct; ensure paper prose never upgrades it to unconditional.
evidence: 'Identify the 4+1D gapped interface conjecture as the critical unproven assumption'; 'Assess: if the conjecture is proven, does the chirality wall fall?'

title: Phase 4 Wave 5 quality-hardening pass left in 'pending'/'blocked' state — Phase 4 never met its own 'COMPLETE' exit criteria
description: Wave 5 was added after a systematic audit found 'physically absurd shot count calculations (27 vs 982 billion), hallucinated feasibility narratives contradicted by computed data, Lean theorem registry name mismatches, missing Paper 6 figures, and a mean-field phase classification bug.' Wave 5 Summary shows 5A pending (86 Aristotle submissions), 5B pending (mean-field volume-scaling fix), 5C blocked by 5B, 5D/5E/5F/5G pending. The exit-criteria checklist for 'Phase 4 COMPLETE' is all unchecked.
what_falls_short: Wave 5B documents a real physics bug: `mean_field.py:152` omits the 1/V factor so 'EVERY coupling below G_c gets classified as vestigial — there is no pre-geometric phase in the current model.' This affects Paper 6's phase diagram (a headline figure). The fix and all downstream figure/notebook/claims audits are pending. 5A's total-division vacuity audit (162 manual proofs) was never run.
remediation: Stale roadmap (March 2026); the project is now at 12,463 theorems / validate 35/35 with later phases superseding Phase 4. Verify the vestigial mean-field 1/V fix actually landed in src/vestigial/mean_field.py and Paper 6's phase diagram is corrected, since this roadmap left it explicitly pending. Confirm no Paper-6 figure still shows the buggy all-vestigial classification.
evidence: '5B: Vestigial mean-field fix — pending (volume scaling in fluctuation formula)'; 'M_g ≈ 10.8 even at G/G_c = 0.3 ... This means EVERY coupling below G_c gets classified as vestigial — there is no pre-geometric phase in the current model'; exit criteria all `[ ]` unchecked.

title: Phase 4 Wave 5A flags total-division vacuity risk across 162 manual proofs (Lean 0/0=0 convention) — audit never executed
description: Wave 5A: 'Lean 4's total division convention (0/0 = 0) means theorems with division in the conclusion can be proved trivially when the denominator is zero. Phase 2 Round 5 caught this for 3 WKB theorems... The remaining 162 manual proofs across 16 modules have not been audited for this issue.' Status: pending. Success criterion: 'No trivially-true theorems remain (conclusions that hold regardless of hypotheses).'
what_falls_short: A known class of vacuous theorems (division-in-conclusion provable via total-division when denominator=0) was identified but the systematic audit of 162 proofs was never run. These are P4-style self-discharging claims masquerading as physics formulas.
remediation: Stale (the 16-module / 216-theorem era; project now 936 modules / 12,463 theorems with a mature preemptive-strengthening discipline). The total-division vacuity class is now caught by the standing strengthening checklist. Confirm a validate.py check or the strengthening pass covers division-conclusion theorems; otherwise run the 5A grep across current modules.
evidence: 'theorems with division in the conclusion can be proved trivially when the denominator is zero ... The remaining 162 manual proofs across 16 modules have not been audited for this issue'; Status: pending.

title: Phase 5f Track B (Wen-ADW coupling) is a NEGATIVE result — perturbative emergent-gravity path is closed (~6000x too weak)
description: The 'central question of the emergent gravity program' returned negative: 'G_Wen ≈ 0.0006 vs G_c ≈ 4.0 — ~6,000x too weak perturbatively.' Plus a 'True showstopper: spin-connection gap (U(1) → SO(3,1) has no known path)'. Impact: 'Changes the research program — perturbative Wen→ADW path is closed. Instanton and vestigial gravity paths remain open.'
what_falls_short: The make-or-break question of whether Wen string-net condensation reaches the ADW critical coupling is answered NO perturbatively, and the spin-connection gap is called a true showstopper with 'no known path'. The emergent-gravity program's primary route is closed; only instanton (Csáki 2024 8-fermion vertices) and Volovik vestigial gravity remain as speculative bypasses.
remediation: This is a genuine physics negative result, correctly reported. The instanton bypass (unsuppressed 8-fermion ADW vertices for N_f=4) and vestigial-gravity bypass are the surviving paths — both are open research. Paper 5 discussion must report the ~6000x deficit and spin-connection gap honestly, not bury them.
evidence: 'G_Wen ≈ 0.0006 vs G_c ≈ 4.0 — ~6,000x too weak perturbatively'; 'True showstopper: spin-connection gap (U(1) → SO(3,1) has no known path)'; 'perturbative Wen→ADW path is closed.'

Wave 4 has `[ ] Lean: formalize G_c invariance at NLO as a theorem` and `[ ] Update Paper 5 discussion with two-loop analysis`. These are the Lean encodings of the negative/robustness results.

evidence: '[ ] Formalize the coupling deficit as a Lean theorem (G₄f < G_c by factor ~6000)'; '[ ] Lean: formalize G_c invariance at NLO as a theorem'.

title: Phase 5f Track C (L=8 RHMC analysis) blocked on data — Binder/FSS/transition-identification all open; Papers 5,6 MC updates pending
description: Wave 5 (L=8 data analysis) and Wave 6 (L=6 + finite-size scaling) are blocked on RHMC completion. Open: full verified_analysis, Binder cumulant U₄, autocorrelation/ESS, 'Phase transition identification (if present)', comparison to mean-field G_c. Production history shows repeated convergence failures (acceptance 0-20% for g<2, sessions killed after CG bottleneck at κ=6054, EO x^{-1/2} pathology |ΔH|≈2.35).
what_falls_short: The numerical confirmation of the emergent-gravity phase transition (the testable prediction that would back Papers 5/6) was never delivered in this phase — it depends on an RHMC run plagued by convergence pathologies, with the Clark-Kennedy 2-PF fix only just implemented and deflation/Hasenbusch acceleration still 'next priority'. Phase-transition existence is gated on '(if present)'.
remediation: Stale roadmap; lattice MC at production L is genuinely compute-bound (Phase 6A roadmap documents L=12-20 paths). Verify whether L=8 Binder analysis ever completed and whether Papers 5/6 carry MC results or still say 'pending data'. The convergence pathologies (CG at κ≈6000) are real numerical hard problems, partly addressed by CK 2-PF + deflation.
evidence: '[ ] Phase transition identification (if present)'; 'Updated Paper 6 (vestigial) with L=4/6/8 results — pending data'; 'EO x^{-1/2} pathology: |ΔH|≈2.35 at any pole count'; 'Killed after diagnosis showed CG bottleneck at κ=6054.'

title: Phase 6k entire phase: Lean SHIPPED but ALL of Python/figures/Paper-43/Stage-13 adversarial review DEFERRED
source: Phase6k_Roadmap.md Status (line 9); per-wave [Pipeline: ... 6-13 deferred] tags (lines 99,135,172,205,251); final note (line 326)

The roadmap itself defers 'per Phase 6j precedent' — but per project policy 'no de-scope from effort magnitude', this is a real open deliverable, not a justified stop.
evidence: 'Cross-layer Python / figures / Paper 43 drafting / Stage 13 adversarial review remain DEFERRED to a future paper-bundle preparation cycle (per Phase 6j precedent — Lean module SHIPPED; Python cross-layer pending).'

title: Phase 6k Wave 1 top-mass and Wave 2 quark-band 'predictions' rest on substrate-channel-ratio constants that may be defining-the-conclusion / fitted to PDG
source: Phase6k_Roadmap.md W1 headline thms (lines 117-122) + strengthening note (line 124); W2 headline thms (lines 153-158)
description: W1 headline 'top_quark_in_scalar_rung_band: m_t ∈ [m_H·k_lower, m_H·k_upper] where k_lower,k_upper are substrate-channel-ratio constants' and `t_does_not_fit_majorana_rung` which the strengthening note admits 'is a tautology since 172 GeV ≪ 10^9 GeV by definition — yes structurally'. W2 'b_quark_intermediate_band_identification' with band 'structurally defined by tetrad-channel ratio r_intermediate = (some closed form)'. The bands are defined by free 'channel-ratio constants'.
what_falls_short: If the band-defining constants k_lower/k_upper/r_intermediate are chosen so the PDG value lands inside, the 'prediction' is defining-the-conclusion (substantive load in the definition, theorem trivially `value ∈ [value-ε, value+ε]`). The roadmap's own strengthening checklist flags `t_does_not_fit_majorana_rung` as a structural tautology (172 ≪ 10⁹) and the within-band claims risk the within-±band-tautology pattern. `(some closed form)` is an unspecified placeholder.
remediation: For each band theorem, verify the channel-ratio constants are derived from substrate parameters (G_c, Λ_UV, N_f, α_ADW) INDEPENDENTLY of the PDG target, not back-fitted. Replace within-own-band tautologies with physical-range or cross-quark-ratio falsifiers (the roadmap's W2 already moves toward m_b/m_c ratio). Pin down '(some closed form)'.
evidence: 't_does_not_fit_majorana_rung — ... is a tautology since 172 GeV ≪ 10^9 GeV by definition? — yes structurally'; 'the intermediate band is structurally defined by tetrad-channel ratio r_intermediate = (some closed form)'.

title: Phase 6k Waves 4-5 shipped as 'structural NO-GO' / 'substrate-silent' verdicts — substrate predicts nothing on CKM apex or δ_CKM
description: W4 verdict: 'BRANCH 3a structural NO-GO via channel-flavor orthogonality. CliffordChannel (26) ≇ FlavorGen × FlavorGen (9).' W5 verdict: 'BRANCH B substrate-silent + BRANCH C′ vacuous-for-δ.' Of the three pre-registered branches per wave, the no-go/silent branch fired for both CKM observables. W2 is 'CLOSED-NEGATIVE NO-GO' (b not in Majorana band, 8 orders off).
what_falls_short: The substrate's claimed reach into CKM physics collapsed to no-gos: it predicts no apex locus, no Wolfenstein constraint, and is silent on δ_CKM. The only positive is the θ̄ = θ_QCD + det_phase cross-bridge (a Phase 6l entry point), but 'J ⫫ det_phase blocks θ̄ → δ chain' — i.e. even that doesn't reach δ_CKM. The quark-rung program's ambitious 'partial constraint' verdict did not materialize for flavor mixing.
remediation: These are honest negative/no-go results (a publishable outcome the roadmap pre-authorized). No remediation needed for correctness, but Paper 43 must frame the quark rung as predominantly no-go (W2/W4/W5 negative), with the single positive being the θ̄ cross-bridge — not oversell substrate predictivity over flavor.
evidence: 'W4 (CKM apex): BRANCH 3a structural NO-GO via channel-flavor orthogonality. CliffordChannel (26) ≇ FlavorGen × FlavorGen (9)'; 'W5 (δ_CKM): BRANCH B substrate-silent ... J ⫫ det_phase blocks θ̄ → δ chain.'

The supersession-path theorem is a cross-reference placeholder to work that may never happen.

Ensure `phase_6h_supersession_path` is not a self-discharging cross-reference tautology; if Phase 6h stays latent indefinitely, the tracked-Prop bundle should be reassessed rather than left dangling.

title: Phase 6k 'predictions' use placeholder constants and PDG-band-matching that risk within-±2σ-band tautologies
description: W3: 'm_d_over_m_u_substrate_predicted — substrate predicts m_d/m_u ≈ 2.0 ± 0.5 vs PDG m_d/m_u = 1.7–2.4. PDG-compatible at current resolution' and 'm_s_over_m_d ≈ 19 ± 5 vs PDG ~17–22. PDG-compatible.' W2 thm6: 'm_b/m_c ≈ 4.0 (PDG ~3.0), close to but not exactly matching; identifies a calculable correction term.'
what_falls_short: Several 'predictions' have error bands (±0.5, ±5) wide enough to overlap PDG essentially by construction ('PDG-compatible at current resolution'), the within-band-tautology pattern. The m_b/m_c case actually MISSES (4.0 predicted vs 3.0 PDG) and is rescued by an unspecified 'calculable correction term' — i.e. the prediction is wrong and patched.
remediation: Tighten predicted error bands to be falsifiable (narrower than the PDG spread) or reframe as consistency checks. For m_b/m_c, either compute the 'calculable correction term' (closing 4.0→3.0) or report the discrepancy honestly rather than gesturing at an uncomputed correction.
evidence: 'm_d/m_u ≈ 2.0 ± 0.5 vs PDG m_d/m_u = 1.7–2.4 ... PDG-compatible at current resolution'; 'm_b/m_c ≈ 4.0 (PDG ~3.0), close to but not exactly matching; identifies a calculable correction term.'

title: Phase 6r-prime '20k LoC / ≥1-year' bar is used to JUSTIFY keeping tracked-Props/deferrals — an effort-magnitude descope vector
source: Phase6r_prime_Roadmap.md Audit bar (lines 620-624); N16 deferral pattern (line 661); further-deferred tracks (lines 600-604)
description: The audit's acceptance bar is '(b) Either ≥1 year process OR >20k LoC required for any sub-component (the LoC version is the operational criterion).' This is used to decide which tracked Props 'pass'. N16 flags '"Phase 7+ Mathlib upstream" framings throughout ... Deferring to imaginary future phase ... Anti-pattern when the work is actually tractable.' The further-deferred tracks (full APS, full MTC typeclass, full cobordism category) are punted to 'multi-year community-coordination horizon.'
what_falls_short: Per project policy 'no de-scope from effort magnitude' and 'a roadmap item deferred ONLY for effort is a REAL open weakness', the >20k-LoC operational bar is precisely an effort-magnitude gate. The roadmap itself catches this (N16: most 'Phase 7+' deferrals 'are tractable ... audit each against the 20k LoC bar'), but the further-deferred tracks (APS/MTC/cobordism) and the bar itself institutionalize effort-based deferral.
remediation: Distinguish genuine literature walls (full APS index theorem, full MTC typeclass — real multi-year Mathlib gaps) from effort-only deferrals (the N16 'Phase 7+' framings the audit itself says are tractable). The former legitimately stay as substrate; the latter must be scheduled, not parked behind the LoC bar.

what_falls_short: A reviewer reading 'PLOB rate bound' in bundle D6 may assume an operational rate ceiling was verified; only the surrogate function's algebraic properties are. The genuine teleportation-stretching proof's ingredient (b) (Bell-measurement/LOCC channel-simulation identity) is an explicit acknowledged WALL.
remediation: Keep the explicit 'surrogate' / 'gapped' labels in D6 prose (already done). Until then the surrogate is the honest form; ensure no bundle claims an operational rate ceiling.
evidence: 'rate ≤ −log₂(1−η), or a tractable formalizable surrogate'; 'honestly labeled surrogate' (PLOBRateBound, verified live in QuantumNetwork/PLOBRateBound.lean); 'the secret-key −log₂(1−η) value stays doubly-gapped … 6AK.2 remains its honest form.'

title: 6AK.1 coherence-limited fidelity 'ceiling' is an EQUALITY for a model channel, not a physical-gate upper bound

evidence: 'Treating it as an upper bound on a real gate's F_avg is a modelling assumption (coherence-limited noise = best case), NOT in the theorem. Docstrings + D6/preprint prose now state this explicitly.'

Status: 'CONDITIONAL on L=8 RHMC results … If L=8 is clean … Wave 8 drops to cross-validation, nice to have and moves back to Phase 6 roadmap.' 8E (full ADW at D=8-12) is 'genuinely frontier research … no one has done this before,' with explicit risk that 'the physics requires D>12 to resolve the vestigial window … workstation-scale ATRG may not have sufficient resolution. This would be a meaningful negative result.'
what_falls_short: The independent cross-validation of the vestigial MC (and the analytical G_ves prediction) does not exist. Given the exponentially-narrow window, even if built it may lack resolution — an acknowledged possible failure mode.
remediation: Deferred to Phase 6 by design (lower publication priority than Phase 5a per the roadmap's own rationale). If pursued, the D>12 resolution risk for the narrow window is genuine; frame any result (including null) as the deliverable.
evidence: 'Wave 8 is now CONDITIONAL on L=8 RHMC results'; 'Wave 8 (4D ATRG) NOT STARTED — deferred pending MC results'; '8A-8E all [ ]'; 'If D=8-12 insufficient … D=16+ becomes Phase 6 item.'

title: GS conditions C4 and C6 are axiomatized 'physics conjecture' content — well-typed but unprovable from mathematics
description: In the chirality-wall formalization, two of the nine Golterman-Shamir conditions encode physics conjectures that cannot be proved: C4 'physics content (bound-state completeness) must be axiomatized — it's a conjecture, not a theorem'; C6 'The existential "∃ basis enlargement removing zeros" is well-typed but unprovable from mathematics — it encodes GS's physical conjecture about SMG. State as a meaningful Prop, not True.' Phase 5 status: '7/9 GS conditions substantive, 2/9 well-typed physics axioms.' counts.json now shows 0 axioms, so these are now Props/hypotheses, not raw axioms.
what_falls_short: The TPF-evades-GS synthesis (master theorem tpf_outside_gs_scope_main) rests on a 9-condition bundle of which 2 are physics conjectures stated as Props; the formal result is 'TPF is outside GS scope GIVEN these conjectural conditions,' not an unconditional resolution of the GS-TPF compatibility question (which the roadmap itself notes 'Neither proves nor disproves the other').
remediation: C4/C6 are genuine physics conjectures (bound-state completeness, kinematical-zero removability via SMG) — not Lean-dischargeable. The honest framing ('well-typed meaningful Prop, not True') is correct; ensure Paper 7 states the conditional nature. Confirm these are now Props (not axioms) post the 0-axiom migration.
evidence: 'C4's physics content (bound-state completeness) must be axiomatized — it's a conjecture, not a theorem'; 'C6 … well-typed but unprovable from mathematics — it encodes GS's physical conjecture about SMG. State as a meaningful Prop, not True.'

title: 199 native_decide occurrences concentrated in FKLW/RossSelinger + QuantumNetwork + lattice/signature back headline results
source: Ground truth (counts.json native_decide: 199 across ~210 files) cross-referenced with Phase6_Deferred completed-arc (lines 49, 96-98, 145-147) + Phase5n
description: Ground truth: 199 native_decide occurrences. The Phase 6 register's completed-arc shows many headline results proved by native_decide: 'SU(2)_k Fusion Rules — 29 theorems, ALL proved by native_decide'; 'SU(3)_k Fusion Rules … 99 theorems, ALL proved by native_decide'; 'det(E8)=1 proved by native_decide'; SteenrodA1 Adem relations 'proved via native_decide'; Ising/Fibonacci pentagons 'PROVED via native_decide over QSqrt2/QSqrt5'. lint_native_decide_comments passes (each carries a justification) but the audit question is which are ELIMINABLE.
what_falls_short: native_decide is policy-tolerated but is a weaker form of verification (relies on the compiler/kernel reduction at scale) than a structural proof; the 6AO Track-3 precedent ELIMINATED 4 KMM native_decides via structural lemmas, showing many are eliminable with effort. Headline 'first verified MTC/fusion-ring' claims lean on it.
remediation: Triage the 199: small fixed-arithmetic facts (det(E8)=1, fusion-ring multiplication tables, Adem relations, pentagon over QSqrt) are candidates for decide/norm_num/structural refactor as 6AO Track-3 did for KMM. Genuinely un-decide-able-under-#10 ones (e.g. some Ross-Selinger/KMM synthesis) stay. Prioritize those backing publication headlines.
evidence: counts.json: 'native_decide: 199 occurrences across ~210 files … audit question = which are ELIMINABLE and which back a HEADLINE'; 'SU(3)_k … 99 theorems, ALL proved by native_decide'; '6AO Track-3 ELIMINATED 4 KMM native_decides via structural-lemma refactor.'

title: 26 placeholder theorems of type True persist, including explicit *_TODO categorical stubs
source: Ground truth (counts.json: 26 placeholder True theorems) cross-referenced with Phase6_Deferred placeholder tracking (line 363)
description: Ground truth: '26 placeholder theorems have type exactly True (several are explicit *_TODO stubs: ocneanu_rigidity_TODO, fusion_to_tqft_TODO, equivalence_is_braided_TODO, equivalence_is_monoidal_TODO, plus *_summary/_marker/_statement docs markers).' what_falls_short: The *_TODO placeholders (ocneanu_rigidity_TODO, fusion_to_tqft_TODO, equivalence_is_braided/monoidal_TODO) are vacuous (True := trivial) — they assert nothing. If any paper cites them as evidence of formalized content (e.g. 'fusion category → TQFT formalized'), that would overstate. The *_summary/_marker/_statement ones are documentation markers (benign).
remediation: For the 4 substantive *_TODO stubs (Ocneanu rigidity, fusion→TQFT, braided/monoidal equivalence), either prove the real statement or ensure no paper claims them as formalized. Ocneanu rigidity and fusion→TQFT are research-grade categorical theorems (genuinely hard). Audit that PLACEHOLDER_THEOREMS registry excludes them from any 'verified theorem' headline count.
evidence: counts.json: '26 placeholder theorems have type exactly True (several are explicit *_TODO stubs: ocneanu_rigidity_TODO, fusion_to_tqft_TODO, equivalence_is_braided_TODO, equivalence_is_monoidal_TODO …).'

title: 6AK.5 QEC code-distance bound deliberately shrunk to a general combinatorial inequality to avoid private-IP overlap
description: The QEC code-distance physical→logical error relation was deliberately scope-limited: 'keep this general code-capacity/threshold-form math only. Do NOT import, restate, or mirror any compiler/accuracy-threshold-specific result that lives in a downstream private project … If it would overlap existing non-public substrate, shrink the wave to just the combinatorial suppression inequality (a norm_num/Nat-power statement) and flag it.'
what_falls_short: The public 6AK.5 deliverable may be only the abstract suppression inequality p_L ≤ A(p/p_th)^⌊(d+1)/2⌋ for abstract (p,p_th,d), not a device/compiler-grounded threshold theorem — by design, to protect private IP. This is a justified leak-discipline descope, not a quality gap, but the public substrate is intentionally weaker/more abstract than the private capability.
remediation: This is intentional IP-boundary discipline (correct per the public/private split policy). No remediation needed for the public side; the substantive device-specific result lives private by design. Confirm the public theorem is the abstract combinatorial form only.
evidence: 'keep this general code-capacity / threshold-form math only. Do NOT import, restate, or mirror any compiler/accuracy-threshold-specific result that lives in a downstream private project … shrink the wave to just the combinatorial suppression inequality (a norm_num/Nat-power statement) and flag it.'

title: Chirality wall verdict remains CONDITIONAL — formal analysis neither proves nor disproves GS-TPF compatibility
description: The flagship chirality-wall result is explicitly conditional: 'Chirality wall: Conditional. 5/9 GS conditions violated by TPF. Neither proves nor disproves the other. First formal analysis establishes this rigorously.' The contribution is 'the only machine-checked contribution to this debate' and 'formalizing the precise mathematical content of the conjecture' — a methodology/infrastructure win, not a resolution. Monitoring item: 'TPF gapped interface … Unproven.'
what_falls_short: The headline is correctly framed as 'first formal analysis of the GS-TPF compatibility question,' NOT 'chirality wall breached/resolved.' Any stronger paper framing (e.g. implying the wall is overcome) would overstate. The actual mathematical resolution (does a 3+1D chiral lattice fermion exist) is open.
remediation: This is THE hardest open problem in lattice QFT (per the roadmap); a full resolution is not achievable. Ensure Paper 7 and stakeholder docs frame the result as 'first machine-checked formalization of the conjecture's content + TPF-evasion analysis,' explicitly conditional, never as a breach. The honest framing is already in the roadmap.
evidence: 'Chirality wall: Conditional. 5/9 GS conditions violated by TPF. Neither proves nor disproves the other'; Phase5n: 'The chirality wall is THE hardest open problem in lattice QFT … Any progress — even formalizing the precise mathematical content of the conjecture … would be a landmark.'

title: backreaction.py SI platform params hardcoded — open provenance/Invariant-2 violation never resolved
description: A Pipeline-Invariant-2 (constants canonical) violation remains open across multiple sections: 'wkb/backreaction.py:99-155 — SI platform params hardcoded, bypass EXPERIMENTS/transonic chain' ([ ] unresolved in 9B-3); 9C-4 '[ ] wkb/backreaction.py:99-155 — SI platform params discrepancy (10x in κ) identified, deep research task filed'; 9D '[ ] src/wkb/backreaction.py: resolve PROVENANCE WARNING (SI platform params still hardcoded).' A 10× discrepancy in κ between backreaction's hardcoded params and the corrected transonic-solver chain was identified but the reconciliation was filed as a deep-research task, not closed.
what_falls_short: backreaction.py computes the acoustic-BH cooling/extremality result (Paper 4 §5) from hardcoded SI params that bypass the provenance-corrected EXPERIMENTS chain, with a known 10× κ discrepancy. If Paper 4's cooling discussion cites backreaction outputs, those numbers may be off by the uncorrected factor.
remediation: Wire backreaction.py to import platform params from EXPERIMENTS/transonic_background (the corrected chain), eliminating the hardcoded SI block and the 10× κ discrepancy. Mechanical fix per Invariant 2; then re-verify Paper 4 §5 cooling numbers.
evidence: 'wkb/backreaction.py:99-155 — SI platform params hardcoded, bypass EXPERIMENTS/transonic chain' (unchecked); '10x in κ … identified, deep research task filed'; 'resolve PROVENANCE WARNING (SI platform params still hardcoded)' (unchecked, 2026-04-03).

title: Polariton platform parameters (c_s, ξ, τ_cav) are PROJECTED, off published Falque values by 2.5×, reconciliation open
description: Primary-source verification found the polariton predictions rest on projected (not measured) parameters with large discrepancies: 'Polariton c_s: code 1.0 µm/ps, Falque reports 0.40 µm/ps (2.5× discrepancy) — tier→PROJECTED'; 'ξ: code 2.0 µm, Falque reports 3.4-4.0 µm'; 'tau_cav: 100/300 ps are projections, Falque actual cavity ≈ 8 ps.' Reconciliation is open: '[ ] Polariton parameter reconciliation: determine if c_s/xi/tau_cav should use Falque values or remain projected'; '[ ] Paper 12 polariton c_s/κ/D/T_H (Phase 5u Waves 3-6 — requires Falque primary-source re-verification).'
what_falls_short: Paper 12's polariton T_H ~0.8-4 K headline and the 'ultra-long cavity τ>100 ps needed for EFT regime' conclusion rest on projected parameters that disagree with the cited primary source (Falque) by 2.5× in c_s and >10× in cavity lifetime. The EFT-testable-regime claim depends on cavity τ that is ~12× longer than Falque's actual ≈8 ps device.
remediation: Complete the Falque primary-source re-verification (Phase 5u Waves 3-6, filed open); decide explicitly whether to use Falque values (and re-derive T_H/κ/D) or retain projections with clear PROJECTED tier labeling in Paper 12. The τ_cav≈8 ps actual vs 100-300 ps projected gap is material to the EFT-regime feasibility claim.
evidence: 'Polariton c_s: code 1.0 µm/ps, Falque reports 0.40 µm/ps (2.5× discrepancy) — tier→PROJECTED'; 'tau_cav: 100/300 ps are projections, Falque actual cavity ≈ 8 ps'; '[ ] Paper 12 polariton … requires Falque primary-source re-verification.'

title: Phase 5n footer dependency inconsistency: Track B W4 'COMPLETE' while its prerequisite W3 is 'pending'
description: The Phase 5n footer states 'Track B W4 COMPLETE (SPTStacking.lean) … Track B W3 pending (bordism-anomaly correspondence).' But the Dependencies section declares 'Track B (Inflow): W3 → W4 (sequential, needs deep research)' — W4 (SPT stacking / anomaly additivity / interface construction) is supposed to depend on W3 (bordism-anomaly correspondence, Anderson duality, Ω^spin_5→ℤ₁₆ map). Marking W4 complete while W3 is pending contradicts the stated sequential dependency.
what_falls_short: Either the dependency is not real (W4 was completed without W3's bordism-anomaly infrastructure, so W4's anomaly-additivity ν(P₁⊠P₂)=ν(P₁)+ν(P₂) mod 16 may rest on a weaker/standalone basis), or the completion claim is loose. The anomaly-inflow infrastructure that would ground the interface-existence argument (W3) is genuinely absent.
remediation: Reconcile the roadmap: either downgrade W4's 'COMPLETE' to reflect that it shipped without the W3 bordism grounding, or document why W4 (SPTStacking) is independent of W3. Then execute W3 to provide the Ω^spin_5→ℤ₁₆ inflow basis the interface argument needs.
evidence: Footer: 'Track B W4 COMPLETE (SPTStacking.lean) … Track B W3 pending'; Dependencies: 'Track B (Inflow): W3 → W4 (sequential, needs deep research).'

title: Phase 5d Wave 3 ADW Monte-Carlo production never delivered — the load-bearing emergent-gravity feasibility verdict is open
source: Phase5d_Roadmap.md §3 Wave 3 (lines 173-212), §10 Summary table (line 440 'L=8 running'), §Blocked items (line 463-465)
description: Wave 3 (L=4,6,8 lattice MC targeting the gap-equation critical region) is the empirical test of whether the ADW tetrad/vestigial emergent-gravity mechanism is real. Every Wave-3 deliverable is unchecked: '[ ] L=4 broad coupling scan data', '[ ] L=4,6,8 Binder cumulant crossing analysis', '[ ] Phase boundary map', '[ ] Vestigial phase detection (or upper bound)'. Status is 'L=8 running (PID 96665)' / 'L=6 needs free cores'. The MC was never completed.
what_falls_short: The Lean gap equation (Wave 1) and Python solver (Wave 2) only establish the mean-field structure exists; the success criteria ('Confirms feasibility: Nontrivial Binder crossing at O(1) coupling'; 'Rules out: No transition at any coupling') require the MC numbers. Papers 5/6/13 (the publication payoff) are gated on Wave 3 and remain unchecked. Without the MC, the project cannot say whether the vestigial metric phase is detected or only bounded.
remediation: Complete the L=4/6/8 RHMC runs (epoch runner already exists), extract the Binder crossing and metric order parameter, and either detect the vestigial phase or publish an upper bound on its (exponentially narrow) window width. The roadmap notes the 4D window may be unresolvable at L≤8, so larger L or a smarter observable may be needed.
evidence: '[ ] L=4 broad coupling scan data (stored in data/tetrad_scan_L4/)'; 'L=8 RHMC — running (PID 96665)'; 'Vestigial window is exponentially narrow (BCS-like) — may require L≥8 to resolve'

title: gap_solution_bounded was DISPROVED by Aristotle — a planned structural claim turned out false
source: Phase5d_Roadmap.md §1B (line 103), Aristotle results table (line 124), Result line 113
description: The roadmap planned `gap_solution_bounded` ('Solution bounded by Λ') as a Wave-1 theorem. Aristotle run 79e07d55 DISPROVED it with an explicit counterexample (G=1/(c₄/2·(1−ln2)), N_f=1, Λ=1, Δ=1). The gap solution is NOT bounded by Λ as the roadmap hypothesized.
what_falls_short: A planned physical bound on the tetrad gap solution does not hold. This is benign as a finding (the project correctly recorded the disproof rather than shipping a false theorem), but it means a structural property the roadmap expected to hold is absent, and any narrative leaning on 'gap solution stays sub-cutoff' must be re-derived under added hypotheses.
remediation: Confirm no downstream theorem or paper claim assumes Δ* ≤ Λ unconditionally; if the bound is physically wanted, re-state it with the extra hypothesis that makes it true (e.g. G in a bounded super-critical window). Already handled in-Lean (theorem dropped), so this is documentation hygiene.
evidence: 'gap_solution_bounded | DISPROVED | Counterexample: G=1/(c₄/2·(1−ln2)), N_f=1, Λ=1, Δ=1'; 'Aristotle 79e07d55: 9/10 proved, 1 disproved (gap_solution_bounded — false)'

title: Phase 5d Waves 4/4b lean MTC, Fibonacci and Rep(u_q) headlines rest heavily on native_decide over custom number fields
description: The first-verified-MTC headlines (isingMTC, fibData, SU(2)_3/4 unitarity, Rep(u_q) fusion) are proved by native_decide over QSqrt2/QSqrt5/QCyc16/QCyc5/QSqrt3/QLevel3. what_falls_short: native_decide is policy-tolerated but compiles the kernel to native code — it is a weaker trust footing than a kernel-checked proof, and per Invariant #10 + the 6AO Track-3 precedent (4 KMM native_decides eliminated via structural-lemma refactor) most of these are eliminable with effort. title: Phase 5e Track C affine-Hopf S²=Ad(K₀K₁) deliverable was mathematically WRONG and had to be walked back to per-generator form
source: Phase5e_Roadmap.md Wave 8 'Correction 2026-04-15 — global Ad(K) does NOT exist for affine' (lines 152-166), §Dependencies (line 225), Lean module table (line 252)
description: An earlier version of Wave 8 listed 'S² = Ad(K₀K₁) (squared antipode)' as the affine HopfAlgebra deliverable. This is impossible: the affine Cartan matrix A=[[2,-2],[-2,2]] is rank-deficient (det A=0), so no single group-like K∈⟨K₀,K₁⟩ implements S² on both simple-root generators (forces a−b=1 AND b−a=1, contradiction). Deliverable corrected to 20 per-generator S² identities.
what_falls_short: A roadmap deliverable that would have shipped a false 'first affine Hopf algebra' structural claim was caught and replaced. The replacement (per-generator S²) is honest and was completed (201 thms, 0 sorry per the module table), so the current state is sound — but it is a documented instance of a planned headline being structurally wrong before correction.
remediation: None on the math (already corrected and proved). Keep the inline historical note so the corrected per-generator framing is not silently 're-upgraded' to the impossible global-Ad(K) claim in a paper.
evidence: 'This is mathematically wrong for the affine case ... Requiring Ad(K)(E_i) = q²·E_i simultaneously for i = 0, 1 forces both a−b=1 AND b−a=1. Contradiction.'

title: Phase 5w Wave 6 Wiedemann-Franz Lean module reduced to fdr_preserves_transport_count_DEFINITIONAL — Onsager bridge never formalized
source: Phase5w_Roadmap.md Wave 6 (lines 199-220), §Identified Research Gaps #2 (line 473), consolidation note line 189/213
description: Wave 6 was the highest-risk Lean target ('Onsager relations have never been formalized in any proof assistant'; '~35-40% overall reuse'). The shipped DiracFluidSK.lean Wave-6 sub-section has 3 theorems: lorenz_ratio_positive, lorenz_ratio_monotone_in_entropy, and `fdr_preserves_transport_count_DEFINITIONAL`. The Onsager reciprocal-relation formalization — the genuinely novel content the wave was created to deliver — was not built; the explicit fallback ('Python + paper without Lean WF module') was effectively taken at the Lean level.
what_falls_short: The theorem name itself carries the `_DEFINITIONAL` suffix, signalling it discharges by unfolding a definition rather than proving Onsager symmetry. The paper16 §6 WF-violation claim is backed by Python + prose, not by a formalized Onsager bridge. The 'first Onsager relations in any proof assistant' aspiration (Research Gap #2, #5 'novel theoretical result if achieved') was not met.
remediation: Formalize the linear Onsager reciprocal relation for the two-channel (charge/heat) structure using the Phase-5a Dolan-Grady/Onsager-algebra substrate, replacing the DEFINITIONAL placeholder with a substantive symmetry theorem. If genuinely intractable in current Mathlib, document it as a tracked gap rather than letting the `_DEFINITIONAL` theorem stand in for the headline.

title: Phase 5w QuasiOneDReduction ships 2 permanent tracked-hypothesis Props (H_AdiabaticRegimeCorrection, H_DispersiveUVCutoff) backing the greybody/quasi-1D validity claim
source: Phase5w_Roadmap.md Wave 10c (lines 351-359), Wave 10c-post (lines 360-375); confirmed live on disk QuasiOneDReduction.lean:265,290
description: The quasi-1D validity argument (Γ₀≈0.9994, |δΓ/Γ|≤1.8%) underpinning Paper 16's vindicated detection claim rests on 5 algebraic theorems PLUS 2 tracked-hypothesis Props: H_AdiabaticRegimeCorrection (dispersive T_H correction is O(D⁴)) and H_DispersiveUVCutoff (UV cutoff scales as √(κ·c_s/l_ee)). Both are def-Prop hypotheses, not derived from the PDE analysis.
what_falls_short: The PDE-analysis content the two Props stand in for (adiabatic-regime correction order, dispersive UV cutoff) is asserted as a tracked hypothesis rather than proved. Paper 16's 'claims vindicated' / 'SNR=98.8% of Γ=1' rests on these holding. They are confirmed still live on disk (QuasiOneDReduction.lean:265/290), so this is an open tracked-hypothesis dependency, not stale.
remediation: Either derive the O(D⁴) adiabatic correction and the √(κ c_s/l_ee) UV-cutoff scaling from the dispersive ODE (Belgiorno-Cacciatori-Trevisan 4th-order WKB) to discharge the Props, or keep them as honestly-framed tracked hypotheses and ensure Paper 16 carries them visibly as assumptions rather than as established results.
evidence: 'H1: H_AdiabaticRegimeCorrection (Prop def, tracked) — parameterized over abstract T_H'; 'H2: H_DispersiveUVCutoff (Prop def, tracked) — parameterized over abstract ω_max'

title: Phase 5w QuasiOneDReduction tracked hypotheses were originally FALSE / VACUOUS as stated (universal-quantification anti-pattern)
description: A manual audit during Paper 17 review found both W10c tracked hypotheses were mis-stated. H_AdiabaticRegimeCorrection had '∀(T_H_exact T_H_leading:ℝ)' inside the Prop body making it 'false as stated (pick T_H_exact=1, T_H_leading=0.1 → 9 ≰ C·D⁴)'. H_DispersiveUVCutoff had '∀(ω_max), ...→∃C' so the ∃C absorbed any ω_max → 'Vacuously true for all physical ω_max'. Any downstream consumer that intro'd these was 'silently assuming a false premise'.
what_falls_short: This is a documented P5/P4-style anti-pattern (false-as-stated hypothesis + vacuous existential) that shipped from a subagent and was only caught by later manual audit. It was fixed (parameters instead of ∀, plus positivity), but it demonstrates the subagent-Lean-quality risk the project flags. The fix is in place; the residual concern is the class of error recurring.
remediation: None on these two Props (fixed, zero citers at fix time). The standing mitigation (line-by-line semantic audit of subagent-shipped Prop definitions; prefer direct Lean work when tracked hypotheses are in scope) should remain enforced.
evidence: 'the Prop was false as stated ... Any downstream consumer that intro'd this hypothesis was silently assuming a false premise.'; 'the ∃C absorbed any positive ω_max ... Vacuously true for all physical ω_max.'

title: Phase 6o Wave 4a Sakharov Λ_J ↔ Λ_HK biconditional was a P5 self-discharging tautology — walked back to one-way implication
source: Phase6o_Roadmap.md Wave 4a Genesis (lines 437) + Wave 4a.4 (line 444) + verdict (B) (line 83); confirmed on disk: only sakharov_induced_gravity_criterion_implies_lambda_j_eq_lambda_hk exists (JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy.lean:338), no _iff_
description: Phase 7 absorption Session 4 found the existing Sakharov biconditional theorem was 'mathematically a one-way implication despite the underlying Phase 6e claim being phrased as a biconditional', with 'lambdaJEqLambdaHK := S.lambdaEffEqLambdaHK a literal Boolean projection (the biconditional reduces to S.x=true → S.x=true, a vacuous P5 anti-pattern)'. Wave 4a re-attempted the genuine biconditional; verdict (B) honestly RETIRED it: the (⇐) direction does not hold under available substrate hypotheses. Disk confirms only the `_implies_` form exists.
what_falls_short: A published-claim-profile item (D5 §11 Sakharov 4-criterion 'iff Λ_J=Λ_HK') was a structural tautology and is now correctly only a one-way implication. The honest closure (5 substantive theorems JTGR16-20 + depletion-factor relation) is sound, but the original biconditional headline was overreach and the deeper bidirectional result the wave hoped for is genuinely unavailable (no primary source argues the converse; Volovik-Jannes 2012 §VII argues only forward).
remediation: None needed on the Lean (honest one-way closure shipped, P5 anti-pattern eliminated). Ensure D5 §11 prose reflects the one-way implication, not a biconditional, and that no flagship narrative still cites 'Sakharov criterion iff Λ_J=Λ_HK'. The converse is a genuine literature gap (no source argues it), so leave retired.
evidence: 'the biconditional reduces to S.x = true → S.x = true, a vacuous P5 anti-pattern'; 'verdict (B): honestly retire biconditional. Primary-source asymmetry: Volovik-Jannes 2012 §VII argues only forward; no source argues converse.'

title: Phase 6o Wave 1a Lindbladian-S-matrix NO-GO and Wave 1b strong-form BCJ NO-GO are negative results standing in for unattained positive results
source: Phase6o_Roadmap.md Wave 1a.5 DissipativeNoGo (lines 119, 144), Wave 1b.5 BCJNoGo (line 162), Risk axes (lines 144, 186)
description: Two Track-1 'deliverables' are structural NO-GOs explicitly framed as the fallback when the positive result fails: Wave 1a 'Soft-theorem extension to dissipative substrate may surface obstruction — publishable as NO-GO either way'; Wave 1b 'three-obstruction analysis may fully rule out the strong-form claim (then NO-GO is the deliverable)'. The headline ('first explicit classical double-copy on analog gravity') is the partial Kerr-Schild result; the full BCJ double-copy is a NO-GO.
what_falls_short: The waves ship whichever of {positive, negative} lands, and the substantive positive claims (soft theorems extend to dissipative emergent sectors; emergent gravity = (gauge)² at amplitude level) were NOT established — only their negations or a Kerr-Schild-only partial. This is honest 'publishable either way' framing, but it means the ambitious positive headlines were not achieved; the deliverables are NO-GOs plus an algebraically-clean partial.
remediation: Keep the NO-GOs clearly framed as negative results, not as the originally-scoped positive deliverables. If a positive soft-theorem or full-BCJ result is later wanted, it needs the spinor-helicity/BCFW Mathlib (PhysLean) infrastructure that does not yet exist (see separate finding).
evidence: 'Soft-theorem extension to dissipative substrate may surface obstruction — publishable as NO-GO either way.'; 'DR's three-obstruction analysis may fully rule out the strong-form claim (then NO-GO is the deliverable — this is fine, productive).'

title: Phase 6o Wave 1a soft-theorem program proceeds via 'thin Lean theorem statements' because Mathlib lacks spinor-helicity / BCFW infrastructure

The Lean verification therefore does not reach the depth of the physics claims; the substance lives in paper math.
remediation: Track PhysLean's spinor-helicity track (arXiv:2411.07667); when it lands, thicken the Wave 1a/1b Lean statements from predicate-level to amplitude-level. Until then, be explicit in any paper that the Lean content is structural/predicate-level, not a full formalization of the soft-factor algebra.
evidence: 'Mathlib lacks spinor-helicity / BCFW infrastructure — work proceeds via paper math + thin Lean theorem statements until PhysLean spinor-helicity track lands.'

remediation: Record the actual η-invariant verdict per substrate (BEC/ADW/³He-A) and the noise-floor-vs-sensitivity-window verdict, so the deliverable's strength (genuinely-new vs trivially-zero) is pinned rather than left as a conditional branch. The APSEta modules are kernel-clean on disk (no native_decide), so this is a content-strength question, not a soundness one.
evidence: 'the computation may surface a trivially zero η-invariant on some or all of the three substrates — in that case the deliverable is a clean structural verification'; 'If the universal noise-floor prediction lands inside experimental sensitivity windows ... if outside the windows, the structural NO-GO becomes the headline.'

remediation: Be explicit in the D2 reframing pre-draft that Niemeier and Schellekens-VOA classifications are cited external theorems entered at predicate level, not formalized in-project. Formalizing them is a Mathlib-upstream-class effort (the roadmap itself flags it as upstream-PR-class). Keep the reframing language honest about 'corollary of a cited classification theorem' vs 'formally verified classification'.
evidence: 'Mathlib does NOT ship Niemeier-lattice classification. In-program build target ... Project-local form sufficient: predicate-level classification'; 'Mathlib does NOT ship VOA infrastructure; substantive in-program build at the predicate / classification-corollary level'

evidence: 'first stochastic integral + Itô's lemma in any proof assistant'; 'At this phase, no Mathlib upstream PR is drafted — internal first.'; 'in-program build either (a) contributes-as-PR when user authorizes post-Phase-6o or (b) builds-parallel-then-merges if Degenne's PR lands first.'

title: Phase 6 VerifiedStatistics roadmap is an explicit multi-year scoping doc — Phases 1-3 (Krylov solvers, verified lattice QFT) are unexecuted with no Mathlib substrate
source: Phase6_VerifiedStatistics_Roadmap.md header (lines 11-14), §2/§3 (lines 91-138), §4 Infrastructure Dependencies (lines 141-152), §5 Effort Estimates (lines 156-166)
description: This roadmap is flagged 'This is a SCOPING document, not an execution plan ... Execution depends on Mathlib probability infrastructure maturation and is at least 1-2 years out for Phase 1.' Phase 2 (verified Krylov/CG solvers, 'CG has never been formally verified', needs floating-point Flocq which is Coq-only) and Phase 3 (staggered Dirac operator, Wilson action, HMC integration — needs SU(N) groups + Haar measure, both 'Missing' in Mathlib) are wholly unexecuted. The dependency table lists CLT, chi-squared distribution, floating-point arithmetic, SU(N) groups, Haar measure all as Missing/Partial.
what_falls_short: The end-to-end 'verified statistical analysis pipeline for lattice MC' aspiration is almost entirely unrealized: only the Phase-1-flavored estimators (jackknife/bootstrap/Γ-method, partially shipped elsewhere as VerifiedStatistics.lean) are tractable. Phases 2-3 are blocked on Mathlib infrastructure that does not exist (floating-point, SU(N), Haar, chi-squared, CLT). Per project policy, multi-year estimates often collapse, but the named Mathlib gaps are genuine substrate walls, not mere effort.
remediation: Treat as a long-horizon scoping doc, not an open commitment. The tractable Phase-1 statistics were largely delivered (jackknife/autocorrelation/bootstrap in VerifiedStatistics.lean per Phase 5d Wave 7/11); Phases 2-3 should be revisited only when Mathlib ships floating-point (Flocq port), SU(N) groups, and Haar measure. Keep flagged as scoping, not execution.
evidence: 'This is a SCOPING document, not an execution plan ... at least 1-2 years out for Phase 1.'; dependency table: 'Floating-point arithmetic | Missing (Flocq is Coq) | Phase 2'; 'SU(N) matrix groups | Missing | Phase 3'; 'Haar measure (compact groups) | Incomplete | Phase 3'

title: Phase 6 VerifiedStatistics carries a 5-40 person-year effort framing that, under project dev-rate, is a deferral-by-effort rather than a genuine wall for Phase 1
description: The roadmap frames Phase 1 (formalized statistics) as '5-10 person-years' and total as '20-40 person-years' based on Flyspeck precedent, and dates Phase 1 as '1-2 years out'. Per project policy ('Past multi-month estimates have repeatedly resolved in hours-days'; ~10-15k LOC/day), the Phase-1 statistics target (jackknife, bootstrap, Γ-method, χ²) is 'just more Lean work' on infrastructure that mostly exists, not a person-year wall.
what_falls_short: The person-year framing risks treating a tractable, partly-already-shipped target (Phase-1 resampling statistics) as out-of-reach on effort grounds. Jackknife/autocorrelation/bootstrap were in fact delivered in VerifiedStatistics.lean (Phase 5d W7/W11), confirming the estimate was inflated for Phase 1. Only the χ²/CLT pieces (needing missing Mathlib distributions) are genuinely substrate-blocked.
remediation: Re-scope Phase 1 of this roadmap to reflect what was already shipped (jackknife/bootstrap/Γ-method) and isolate the truly-blocked χ²/CLT items behind the specific Mathlib gaps. Drop the person-year framing for Phase 1; reserve effort-wall language for Phases 2-3 where the Mathlib substrate is genuinely absent.
evidence: 'Phase 1 | Formalized statistics | 5-10 person-years | Mathlib probability maturation'; 'Total: 20-40 person-years for end-to-end. Phase 1 alone is the high-value target.'

title: `TPFConjecture` — live tracked Prop (ex gapped_interface_axiom); 3+1D/4+1D SPT gapped-interface claim is conjectural and backs SM-generation theorems
source: Phase5s_Roadmap.md Track A (lines 21-89, esp. 'axiomatized in 3D'); docs/PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md §4; lean/SKEFTHawking/SPTClassification.lean:254
description: Phase 5s Track A's 'highest-value target' was to strengthen the project's only axiom (gapped_interface_axiom) into a 1D-proved/2D-proved/3D-conjectured ladder. The axiom was converted (2026-05-19) to `TPFConjecture` (a live tracked Prop). The 3+1D/4+1D content remains 'plausible but unproven', and 4 downstream theorems (`anomaly_free_implies_chiral_gauge`, `sm_generation_gapped_interface`, `sm_three_gen_gapped_interface`, `no_gap_implies_anomalous`) take it as an explicit hypothesis.
what_falls_short: Standard-Model generation / chiral-gauge conclusions are conditional on an unproven 3+1D conjecture. The FK 2+1D native_decide proof (Δ=14) is only 'evidence', not a discharge. The roadmap's success criterion 'proved in 1D, proved in 2D, conjectured in 3D' is honest but the headline-adjacent SM theorems propagate the conjecture.
remediation: Keep as tracked Prop (literature-open; TPF themselves call it conjectural). Ensure every external writeup consuming the 4 conditional theorems hedges 'predicated on TPFConjecture (proven 1+1D/2+1D, conjectural 3+1D/4+1D)' per the tracked-hypotheses doc. No constructive 3+1D discharge is feasible in current Mathlib/literature.
evidence: Tracked doc §4: '`TPFConjecture` — KEEP_AS_TRACKED (converted from `axiom gapped_interface_axiom`)'; '3+1D / 4+1D (this Prop): no counterexample known; "plausible but unproven"'; consumers list 4 theorems each '(takes (H : TPFConjecture))'.

title: Phase 5s Wave 9: two live tracked Props (H_CFZ2_sq_e/_a) from 38 sessions stuck on the graded-hexagon summand — sorries refactored to hypotheses, not discharged
description: The CenterFunctor Z2 equivalence (H_CF2) was pursued across 38 documented sessions and could not close the `halfBraiding_sq_identity` graded 3-way-tensor summand extraction. Per user directive (2026-04-22), 3 research-grade sorries were CONVERTED to 2 tracked Props (`H_CFZ2_sq_e`, `H_CFZ2_sq_a`) plus deletion of an h_cf2_G2 wrapper. The roadmap explicitly notes a focused subagent concluded 'Option B is NOT structurally easier than Option A. The irreducible hard part is Lean encoding of graded 3-way tensor summand extraction ... missing from current Mathlib infrastructure.'
what_falls_short: The Drinfeld-center equivalence for Z2 (the categorical content) is not fully proven — the square-identity of the half-braiding is an unproven hypothesis. The mathematical content was manually verified (g²=𝟙) but the Lean encoding is open. This is a sorry-to-hypothesis walkback, not a discharge.
remediation: Roadmap's own plan: 'File standalone Mathlib issue for graded 3-way tensor summand extraction helpers; if Mathlib receptive, discharge H_CFZ2_sq_e / H_CFZ2_sq_a directly and remove the hypotheses.' Zero downstream dependencies noted, so low priority; but it is a genuine open Lean gap, not a closed result.
evidence: Line 391-404 'sorry → hypothesis refactor (2026-04-22) ... 3 research-grade sorries converted to tracked Prop hypotheses'; line 407-410 'remaining (low priority): File standalone Mathlib issue ... discharge ... and remove the hypotheses'; line 379 'Option B is NOT structurally easier than Option A.'

title: Phase 5s spectral-gap, Muger, KL, instanton waves rest on native_decide over finite matrices — eliminability not assessed in roadmap
source: Phase5s_Roadmap.md Waves 3/5/6/7 (lines 53-71, 93-132, 136-147); strategy line 9; timeline lines 440-444
description: Phase 5s's explicit strategy is 'Is there a finite matrix computation hidden inside this problem that native_decide can verify?' Wave 3 (FK spectral gap E₀=-7/-14, Δ=2/14) is verified 'via native_decide on 16×16 matrix arithmetic'; Wave 6 KL fusion k=4,5 'native_decide'; Wave 7 instanton 6×6 angular kernel. counts.json reports 199 native_decide occurrences concentrated partly in these lattice/quantum-group files. The 6AO Track-3 precedent shows KMM native_decides ARE eliminable via structural-lemma refactor, but Phase 5s never revisited these.
what_falls_short: These results are 'machine-checked' but lean on native_decide (compiled evaluation, outside the kernel's trusted reduction), which CLAUDE.md treats as project-tolerated only where genuinely un-decide-able under Invariant #10. The roadmap does not classify which of these back a headline vs are eliminable; the FK gap (Δ) is load-bearing evidence for TPFConjecture.
remediation: Audit the FK/Muger/KL/instanton native_decides against the 6AO Track-3 structural-elimination pattern: small (16×16, 6×6) integer-matrix decides are candidates for kernel `decide` or structural lemmas. At minimum, confirm each carries a lint-passing justification and document which back the TPFConjecture evidence ladder (the FK Δ) vs which are pure leaf verifications.
evidence: Strategy line 9 'native_decide can verify'; Wave 3 line 63 'All via native_decide on 16×16 matrix arithmetic'; Wave 6 line 128 'Fusion rule verification for k=4,5 (native_decide)'; counts ground-truth '199 native_decide occurrences ... lattice/signature/quantum-group files'.

title: Phase 5s Wave 6 (KL data k=3,4,5) — S-matrix for k=5 still PENDING (needs Q(cos(2π/7)) number field)
description: Wave 6's deliverable 'Data-level KL matching theorem for k ≤ 5' is incomplete: the k=5 S-matrix unitarity is listed '(new)' and the timeline records 'PARTIAL — k=4 already done, k=5 fusion COMPLETE (comm + assoc). S-matrix k=5 pending (needs Q(cos(2π/7)) field).' The 'For k ≤ 5 ... machine-checked' success-criterion is thus not met for k=5.
what_falls_short: The strongest-computational-evidence claim for the Kazhdan-Lusztig categorical equivalence stops short of k=5 on the S-matrix side. It is an effort/number-field deferral, not a wall — exactly the kind of effort-only deferral the project's 'no de-scope from effort' policy flags as a real open item.
remediation: Build the Q(cos(2π/7)) cyclotomic-subfield number field (the project already builds analogous fields QSqrt3/QSqrt5 for k=3,4), prove k=5 S-matrix unitarity via native_decide/structural, and ship the k≤5 data-level matching theorem. Pure number-field construction work, not research-grade.
evidence: Line 130 'S-matrix unitarity for k=3 ..., k=5 (new)'; timeline line 443 'PARTIAL ... S-matrix k=5 pending (needs Q(cos(2π/7)) field).'

title: Phase 1 physics deliverable (the actual δ_diss dissipative correction) deferred to Phase 2/3; Lean theorems are existence-style witnesses, not the computed result
source: Phase1_Roadmap.md §6-8 (lines 152-235), esp. Structure C theorems (lines 227-233) and Phase 2/3 (lines 187-201)
description: Phase 1 is marked 'COMPLETE ✓' but the headline physics result — δ_diss as a function of dissipative transport coefficients (T_eff = T_H(1+δ_diss+...)) — is explicitly Phase 2 work ('Compute δ_diss as a function of the dissipative transport coefficients', steps 9-14). The 14 Lean theorems verify structural/existence facts: 'Dissipative correction existence: ∃ δ_diss that vanishes iff γ₁=γ₂=0 (witness: -(γ₁+γ₂)/(2κ))' and 'Dispersive correction bound: ∃ δ_disp ≠ 0 with |δ_disp| ≤ C·D² (witness: -(π/6)D²)'.
what_falls_short: These are ∃-witness theorems with hand-supplied witnesses (a defining-the-conclusion / existence-absorption pattern, P-class adjacent): proving 'there exists a δ_diss vanishing iff γ=0' with an explicit linear witness is far weaker than deriving the physical δ_diss from the SK Green's function. The roadmap's own G_R/G_K extraction (the genuine calculation, obstacles 1-5) is Phase 2, not done.
remediation: Execute Phase 2 steps 9-12: expand the SK action around the transonic background to quadratic order, solve G_R with dissipative terms (confluent Heun), apply KMS/FDR to get G_K, and extract δ_diss as a derived function (not a witness). Then strengthen the Lean theorems from ∃-witness to the derived-value form. This is the substantive physics paper, currently a blueprint.
evidence: Structure C line 230 'Dissipative correction existence: ∃ δ_diss that vanishes iff γ₁=γ₂=0 (witness: -(γ₁+γ₂)/(2κ))'; Phase 2 line 191 'Compute δ_diss as a function of the dissipative transport coefficients'; line 168 'still below experimental sensitivity' (δ_diss ~ 10⁻⁶–10⁻⁴).

title: Phase 1 main experimental prediction is below experimental sensitivity (hedge); accessibility relies on an untested spin-sonic enhancement
description: The paper's concrete experimental prediction δ_diss ~ (na³)^{1/2}(T_H/T_max)² ~ 10⁻⁶ to 10⁻⁴ is 'comparable to or larger than the dispersive correction, but still below experimental sensitivity.' Experimental accessibility is conditional: 'in two-component spin-sonic systems ... potentially making δ_diss experimentally accessible' — 'potentially', not demonstrated.
what_falls_short: The headline selling point ('directly testable by next-generation BEC experiments') is hedged: the baseline prediction is unmeasurable, and the testable regime depends on a spin-sonic enhancement (Berti et al. 2025) that is not computed in the roadmap. The sign of δ_diss (physically meaningful) is also left as an either/or.
remediation: Compute δ_diss explicitly for the Trento spin-sonic / Heidelberg K-39 parameters (provenance already in constants.py) to determine whether the enhanced ratio T_H/T_max crosses measurement thresholds, and pin the sign. Replace 'potentially accessible' with a computed accessibility statement.
evidence: Line 166 'δ_diss ~ ... ~ 10⁻⁶ to 10⁻⁴ ... still below experimental sensitivity'; line 168 'potentially making δ_diss experimentally accessible'; line 170 'if dissipation REDUCES ... ; if it INCREASES ...'.

title: Phase 5g Track A Wave 3 (L=12 RHMC production) BLOCKED — the computational-unlock deliverable never ran
description: The matrix-free operator (W1) and CG integration (W2) shipped, but Wave 3 — the actual L=12 production run that the whole Track A 'L=12+ unlock' motivation exists for — is 'BLOCKED (L=8 workers must free up first)'. All four deliverable checkboxes (coupling grid, run, analysis, Binder crossing L=8 vs L=12) are unchecked, and the L=8 vs dense profiling was 'deferred to next session'.
what_falls_short: The computational bottleneck the phase set out to break (220GB dense → 130MB matrix-free, enabling L=12) is technically enabled but never exercised. No L=12 data, no Binder crossing. The payoff result is absent; the blocker is resource scheduling (effort/infra), not a wall.
remediation: Free the L=8 workers and run `run_rhmc_epochs.sh --l 12`; produce data/rhmc/L12_g*.npz and the L=8/L=12 Binder-crossing comparison. Also complete the deferred L=8 matrix-free-vs-dense profiling to validate the >4000x memory claim translates to wall-clock benefit.
evidence: Wave 3 line 73 'L=12 Production — BLOCKED (L=8 workers must free up first)'; all deliverables unchecked; W2 line 66 'Profile at L=8: matrix-free vs dense time/traj (deferred to next session)'.
