title: 3∣N_f generation headline is conditional on Wang's Cardy-Casimir physics premise (24∣8N_f), not derived
description: The roadmap repeatedly notes the `3 ∣ N_f` headline (`GenerationConstraint.generation_mod3_constraint`) 'consumes only 24 ∣ 8N_f — the Dedekind-η modular-framing leg, conditional on Wang's Cardy-Casimir hypothesis (a physics premise, not a topological one)'. The 16∣σ spin-bordism work 'strengthens the 16-convergence/chirality-wall rigor of D2, not the headline itself.'
what_falls_short: The flagship-adjacent generation-number constraint is gated on an external physics premise (Wang 2024 Cardy-Casimir) entered as a hypothesis `h_mod : 24 ∣ 8N_f`. The theorem is valid as a conditional but the '3 generations' conclusion is not derived from first principles within the project; the heavy spin-bordism machinery does not actually feed the headline.
remediation: Disclose clearly in D2/paper10 that 3∣N_f is conditional on the Wang Cardy-Casimir premise (24∣8N_f), and that the unconditional 16∣σ leg strengthens the chirality-wall rigor but does not discharge the headline's physics premise. Deriving 24∣8N_f from project substrate is a separate physics frontier (η-modular/anomaly), not currently scoped.
evidence: Roadmap line 53 'consumes only the Dedekind-η modular leg conditional on Wang's Cardy-Casimir physics premise (a physics premise, not a topological one)'; line 74-75 'strengthens the 16-convergence/chirality-wall rigor of the D2 deep paper, not the headline itself'; GenerationConstraint.lean:54 'generation_mod3_constraint (which already takes h_mod : 24 ∣ (8 * N_f)'.

title: 5q.B [HM] rank-4 frontier closed via native_decide-bearing / theta-modularity substrate; theta route (C1-C5) built then abandoned as the spine
description: The van der Blij `8∣σ` discharge underwent a major spine pivot: the C1-C6 analytic/theta/Siegel-Narain route was chosen (line 446 'the analytic/theta route (most Mathlib toe-holds)'), built out partway (multivar_poisson, anisotropic Gaussian FT, lattice-theta convergence), then the indefinite case was instead closed arithmetically via rank-4 quaternary Hasse-Minkowski. The definite [Θ] case still uses the theta route; the p=2 rank-5 isotropy crux `mod8_quinary_solvable` is a kernel `decide` over ~10⁵ cases (line 894-896, deliberately kernel `decide` NOT native_decide).
what_falls_short: A large theta/Siegel-Narain substrate (C1-C5) was scoped as 'the CHOSEN spine' and then NOT used for the load-bearing indefinite case ('the actual obstruction') — it is 'retained as reusable substrate'. This is a route walkback (the published narrative must not claim the theta spine carried the result). The final p=2 leg rests on a large finite kernel-decide that, while kernel-pure, is a brute-force combinatorial verification.
remediation: Result is landed and kernel-pure (counts.json: 0 axioms/0 sorry), so this is a documentation-hygiene item: ensure the roadmap's STALE-banner discipline (already applied at lines 13-19, 233-242, 418-426) is mirrored in any paper prose, so no writeup claims the theta/Siegel-Narain spine delivered the indefinite 8∣σ. The mod-8 kernel `decide` is acceptable per Invariant #10 (genuinely finite); no action needed beyond confirming it is not native_decide.
evidence: Line 421-426 'Landing (C) ... was achieved — but via the classification / rank-4 quaternary Hasse-Minkowski route, NOT the analytic/theta spine the sub-waves C1-C6 below scoped. The theta route (C1-C5, Siegel-Narain) was built out partway ... and is retained as reusable substrate'; LabNotebook line 895 'Kernel `decide` ... ~10⁵ cases, kernel-tractable, NO native_decide'.

title: Theta/lattice-signature substrate (E8*, Theta*, Lattice*) concentrates native_decide per ground truth — definite [Θ] leg of 8∣σ may carry eliminable nd
source: counts.json ground truth (199 nd, concentrated in 'E8*, QCyc*, Theta*, FibonacciM*, Lattice*'); LabNotebook lines 38, 169-180 (E8Signature integer-Cholesky `decide`)
description: Ground truth places native_decide concentration in lattice/signature/quantum-group files that are exactly the 5q.B substrate (E8Signature, ThetaModularity, Lattice*). The lab notebook documents E8 facts via `decide`-over-ℤ (e.g. `c8_eq : C8ᵀ·C8 = 4•E8lit by decide`, line 38) and the mod8 quinary kernel-decide. These back the definite-case 8∣σ ([Θ]) which is part of the headline 16∣σ chain.
what_falls_short: Some signature/E8 facts are discharged by decide/native_decide rather than structural proof. CLAUDE.md's 6AO Track-3 precedent (4 KMM native_decides eliminated structurally) shows these are often eliminable. The roadmap does not classify which nd in the 5q.B substrate are kernel-`decide` (acceptable) vs native_decide (tolerated-but-eliminable) vs headline-backing.
remediation: Run lint_native_decide audit over the E8*/Theta*/Lattice* files; for any native_decide (as opposed to kernel decide) backing the 8∣σ/16∣σ chain, attempt the 6AO-style structural-lemma refactor. The lab notebook already shows the project prefers integer-Cholesky `decide` and explicit kernel decide here, so the residual nd surface may be small — but it should be confirmed, not assumed.
evidence: Ground truth 'native_decide ... lattice/signature/quantum-group files (E8*, QCyc*, Theta*, FibonacciM*, Lattice*)'; LabNotebook line 38 'c8_eq (C8ᵀ·C8 = 4•E8lit by decide over ℤ)'; CLAUDE.md '6AO Track-3 ELIMINATED 4 KMM native_decides via structural-lemma refactor'.

title: AttributionContentSweep residual human-gate items remain (unverifiable-without-full-PDF claims); widening tranche (~440 bibkeys) only partially swept
description: The sweep is COMPLETE for the load-bearing tranche (18 bundles GREEN, 6 fabrication-class fixes), but explicitly leaves 'Residual human-gate items: AharonovArad Lemma 6.1 + forward-dated published venues (full PDF/DOI), Amelio2020 registry-title disambiguation.' The widening to all ~440 cited bibkeys ('remaining registry entries cited in bundle drafts') is described as a follow-on, not completed.
what_falls_short: A residual class of citations cannot be content-verified without full PDFs/DOIs and awaits human action; and the bulk of cited bibkeys beyond the anchor-listed load-bearing subset were not content-swept. The fabrication-class base rate (3 found in ~10 spot-checks) means the un-swept tail could still harbor metadata-valid-but-fabricated attributions.
remediation: Resolve the named human-gate items (obtain AharonovArad full PDF to verify Lemma 6.1; confirm forward-dated venue DOIs; disambiguate Amelio2020 registry title). Then run the widening pass over the ~440 non-anchor bibkeys before any submission, prioritizing falsification-critical bundles (L1/D5/D3). CHECK 26 guards presence of embedded citations but only content-reading verifies support.
evidence: Line 3 'Residual human-gate items: AharonovArad Lemma 6.1 + forward-dated published venues (full PDF/DOI), Amelio2020 registry-title disambiguation'; line 23 'Widening (after load-bearing tranche): remaining registry entries cited in bundle drafts (~440 bibkeys total; load-bearing tranche ≈ anchor-listed subset).'

title: Phase 5s Wave 7 instanton mechanism: 4D index theorem BYPASSED via separation of variables — the physical coupling-deficit bridge is a model reduction, not the full mechanism
description: Wave 7 (Csáki instanton mechanism for the Wen-ADW coupling deficit) is marked COMPLETE, but the timeline records the result was obtained because the '4D index theorem BYPASSED via separation of variables. InstantonZeroModes.lean: ... Clifford decomposition + 6×6 angular kernel + polynomial dim → 2|qn|=4.' The decision gate was 'If the instanton contribution is a sharp computable number → formalize. If order-of-magnitude only → document as physics argument.'
what_falls_short: The full 4D index-theorem content was sidestepped via a separation-of-variables reduction to a 6×6 angular kernel computation. Whether this captures the physical coupling-deficit bridge (vs a tractable proxy) is the kind of model-reduction caveat the project's 'never publish toy model results as the real physics' standard targets. The decision-gate's 'sharp number vs order-of-magnitude' resolution is not explicitly stated as 'sharp' in the closing note.
remediation: Document explicitly whether the separation-of-variables zero-mode count (2|qn|=4) is the full physical instanton contribution (sharp number) or a reduced-model proxy, per the Wave 7 decision gate. If proxy, label it as a physics argument rather than a formalized sharp result in any paper that cites the coupling-deficit bridge.
evidence: Timeline line 444 'COMPLETE — RED→GREEN. 4D index theorem BYPASSED via separation of variables. InstantonZeroModes.lean: 9 theorems ... 2|qn|=4'; decision gate line 144 'If order-of-magnitude only → document as physics argument, don't formalize.'

A separate roadmap (acknowledged: 'A separate roadmap if the D2 anomaly story's full rigor is pursued') would be needed; both need substrate (Adams-SS / APS-η) absent from Mathlib.

Reaching it needs either the Adams-spectral-sequence machinery ... or APS / η-invariant index theory; neither is in Mathlib, and the repo's APSEta/* content is η = 0 placeholder'; line 561 'the repo's Schellekens/* is a predicate-level scaffold'; §target line 68 'an honest placeholder'.

title: 5q.B Phase 1 existence-shaped char-vector / 8→16 Arf lemmas use ∃-witness and band-style framings (preemptive-strengthening-checklist risk)
description: Some 5q.B substrate lemmas are existence/structural witnesses rather than load-bearing quantitative claims: e.g. the char-vector route-C step '`c² ≡ c'² mod 8` for any two characteristic vectors — elementary' is a congruence-invariance fact; `latticeSig_le`/`ge`/`abs_latticeSig_le` (|σ|≤n) are bound-style. The Arf `gaussSum_arf : ∑(-1)^{qx} = 2·(-1)^{Arf}` chain is genuine, but several supporting laws are the kind of structural facts the preemptive-strengthening checklist (P3 multiplication-only, defining-the-conclusion) flags.
what_falls_short: Not every shipped lemma in the leg is load-bearing physics content; some are definitional/bound facts that satisfy the proof obligation without carrying falsifiable content. This is a minor strengthening-discipline observation, not a headline gap — the capstone theorems (eight_dvd_latticeSig, sixteen_dvd_latticeSig) are substantive.
remediation: Low priority given the capstones are substantive and kernel-pure. If a post-wave strengthening audit is run on the 5q.B files, apply the five-question checklist to the supporting signature-bound and char-vector lemmas to confirm none are pure tautology-repackaging; the substantive load is correctly in the capstone divisibility theorems.
evidence: Route-C line 534-536 '`c² ≡ c'² mod 8` ... elementary ... Real, kernel-pure progress; ship first'; LabNotebook line 23 'latticeSig_le/ge, abs_latticeSig_le (|σ|≤n)'; CLAUDE.md preemptive-strengthening checklist (P3/defining-the-conclusion).

title: Entire Phase 6h program inactive — Gate Z.4 closed NEGATIVE (second structural no-go)

Falls back to structural-only ("hierarchy from fixed-point proximity is qualitatively consistent").' Gate H.5 makes 'ship structural-only' an explicit branch.

The amended/closed status: efficiency headline rossSelinger_log_length_explicit ships, BUT 'W5 does NOT meet its gate as written — the efficiency headlines remain (a) conditional on the residual existence and (b) carry the 4 KMM native_decide axioms in their closure, both explicitly forbidden by the W5 gate.' The full ∀U-unconditionality is blocked by a prime-density/relative-norm existence wall (Selinger 1212.6253 / Ross Prop 3.2.9 realize it only randomized under a prime-distribution hypothesis), dispositioned as a Caves-precedent tracked Prop.

The headline is conditional-on-residual-existence, not unconditional.

The 4 native_decide sites need structural reproofs (a dedicated /goal). Note: counts.json now shows axioms=0, so the 4 KMM native_decide are policy-TOLERATED nd occurrences, not project axioms.
evidence: 'W5 does NOT meet its gate as written — the efficiency headlines remain (a) conditional on the residual existence and (b) carry the 4 KMM native_decide axioms in their closure'

description: Four named native_decide sites — cliffordBase_box_core, bridge_box_core, kmm_lemma3_alg2, maStep_exists_core — are 'empirically un-eliminable by kernel decide under #10 (the enumerations blow the 200k-heartbeat budget; maxHeartbeats is forbidden).' kmm_lemma3_alg2 alone is (ZMod 8)^4 squared = 16.7M Coord4 pairs, native_decide ~497s, 'carries Lean.ofReduceBool.' maStep_exists_core ~809k triples. They back the KMM exact-synthesis arc (Phase 6x Item F/G + Phase 6AM W5).

The precedent (6AO Track-3 eliminated 4 different KMM native_decides structurally) shows nd is eliminable with effort, so these are open debt, not a true wall.
remediation: Structural reproofs: KMM Lemma-3 mod-8 algebra (research-flavoured), Giles-Selinger sde↔kSO3, Clifford-group ≤6-word coverage, MA-step orbit-closure (mechanical). Scoped as a dedicated 'structural-KMM purity' /goal per the roadmap. The 6AO Track-3 refactor is the proven template.
evidence: 'The 4 KMM native_decide axioms ... are empirically un-eliminable by kernel decide under #10 ... they need structural reproofs, a separate program. Project-TOLERATED'

title: Phase 6x Item L MVP correctness proven WITHOUT the representability characterization (Classical.choose over ∃-gate-word)
description: The Item L MVP synth_CCZ_correct proves interp (synth U) = U but 'IsExactlyCliffordCCZ is a submonoid containing every gate, synth extracts a witnessing word' — i.e. correctness is the trivial 'extract the word you were handed back out.' L.A explicitly flags this: re-basing on the channel-rep replaces 'the trivial ∃-gate-word + Classical.choose of the MVP'; 'This closes the honest public-scope gap: the MVP proves correctness without the representability characterization.' L.A then ships only a NECESSARY condition (channelGenMonoid membership), with the full decidable ⟺ converse documented as 'NOT available from arXiv:2401.08950 ... the converse is open.'
what_falls_short: The MVP exact-synthesis 'correctness' is a defining-the-conclusion / identity-extraction triviality; the real content (which U are exactly representable) is only a necessary certificate, with the decidable characterization open in the source literature.
remediation: The full decidable ⟺ converse needs Gosset-Kliuchnikov-Mosca-Russo (arXiv:1308.4134) Fact 3.9 converse — open per the source. Keep the MVP correctness labeled as identity-extraction; do not present it as a substantive synthesis-completeness result.
evidence: 'replacing the trivial ∃-gate-word + Classical.choose of the MVP ... the MVP proves correctness without the representability characterization'

title: Phase 6AM W4 quantum-FDT floor: Caves added-noise inequality A≥ℏω/2 stays a permanent cited hypothesis (CCR wall)
description: W4 derives the Callen-Welton floor (ℏω/2)coth(βℏω/2) from PhysLib's CanonicalEnsemble, but the Caves phase-insensitive-amplifier added-noise inequality A ≥ ℏω/2 'Stays a cited hypothesis (the one textbook tracked hypothesis in the 6AN line — by design): ... So: the floor is derived; the added-noise inequality is cited. This is a genuine wall, not an effort deferral.'

evidence: 'the floor is derived; the added-noise inequality is cited. This is a genuine wall, not an effort deferral — document it as such.'

NOT merely "E_R is well-defined". The falsifiable comparison is the deliverable; a definition-only ship FAILS.' Closure claims gate met, so this is a guarded-against-and-passed item, included as a load-bearing-claim flag.

evidence: 'REE — OPERATIONAL, not definitional ... a definition-only ship FAILS.'

A renaming alias of the existing project lemma is NOT a Mathlib PR' and 'Pivot Rule #15 mis-scoping (yielding on substantial work rather than axiom required).'

M.1/M.2 actual de-privatized extractions and the m-generic upgrade (Item J) remain Tier-3 / homeless orphans.

Some (Items A/B-RR5/B-RR7/C in Tier 1) were subsequently spec'd; orphans #1,#5 (Tier 3 Items J,K) likely remain open.

title: Phase 5j SU(2)/SU(3) gauge-emergence chain is theorem-level only through step 2; Z₂→SU(2) is a heuristic, ADW step is non-perturbative
description: Phase 5j formalizes Fermi-point→SU(2) gauge emergence but with explicit rigor-level tracking: 'emergence_theorem_frontier: chain is theorem-level through step 2 only (PROVED)'; the SU(2) emergence chain is '6 steps (3 theorem + 2 heuristic + 1 speculative)'; 'adw_requires_nonperturbative: step 5 is not theorem (PROVED).' Deep-research KEY CORRECTIONS: 'Z₂ → SU(2) promotion is a HEURISTIC (³He-A specific), not a theorem'; 'Chirality problem unsolved: Fermi-point gives vector coupling, SM needs chiral SU(2)_L.' SU(3) chain is 'MORE speculative than SU(2).'
what_falls_short: The headline 'non-Abelian gauge structure from condensed-matter topology' (the Critical Review's most-promising route to bypass the spin-connection gap) is honestly downgraded: only 2 of 6 steps are theorems, the key Z₂→SU(2) link is a heuristic, the gravity-connecting ADW step is non-perturbative (not a theorem), and the chirality obstruction remains unsolved. The Lean theorems largely certify the rigor-status enum, not the physics chain.
remediation: Steps 3-6 (Z₂→SU(2) promotion, ADW non-perturbative tetrad, chirality) are genuine open physics, not Lean effort — the heuristic/speculative status reflects literature gaps (Volovik-Zubkov heuristic, Selch-Zubkov mixed SU(2)×SO(3,1)). Keep the rigor-level enum honest in any paper; do not present the SU(2) chain as a closed theorem.
evidence: 'SU(2) emergence chain: 6 steps (3 theorem + 2 heuristic + 1 speculative) ... Z₂ → SU(2) promotion is a HEURISTIC ... not a theorem ... Chirality problem unsolved'

title: Phase 5j gauge-emergence steps verified by native_decide on a RigorLevel enum (decide-able pairwise distinctness / enum classification)
description: Phase 5j's emergence-chain claims are 'all verified by native_decide' over a 'RigorLevel enum: theorem/heuristic/speculative with DecidableEq.' Similarly Phase 5m Wave 3/4 fusion results are 'native_decide-verified theorems' (SU(2)_k, SU(3)_1, SU(5)_1 fusion, E₆/E₇/E₈/F₄ level-1 alcove structure).
what_falls_short: For Phase 5j, certifying a 6-step chain as '3 theorem + 2 heuristic + 1 speculative' via native_decide on an inductive enum is decide-able pairwise classification of the inductive structure, not a proof of the underlying physics; the substantive content is in the rigor-level assignment, which is a modeling choice. (Phase 5m fusion native_decides are more substantive — genuine finite fusion-ring computations — but still compiler-trust rather than kernel proof.)
remediation: Phase 5m fusion native_decides are genuine finite computations (acceptable, project-tolerated); Phase 5j enum-classification native_decides are decide-able and could be replaced by structural decide/rfl if desired, but are low-stakes (they certify a status enum, not load-bearing physics). Ensure papers don't read the native_decide'd enum as a physics theorem.
evidence: 'SU(2) emergence chain: 6 steps ... all verified by native_decide'; 'RigorLevel enum: theorem/heuristic/speculative with DecidableEq'

title: Phase 2 second-order FDR and positivity results lean on native_decide counting + total-division (0/0=0) caveat
description: Phase 2's counting lemmas firstOrder_count / secondOrder_count / secondOrder_count_with_parity / thirdOrder_count are proved by native_decide (only the general transport_coefficient_count uses a bijection proof). Round 4 separately discovered that 3 WKB theorems had unused 0<kappa hypotheses 'because Lean 4 uses total division (0/0 = 0), which papers over the physical requirement that κ > 0' — Round 5 added load-bearing-κ versions to fix this.
what_falls_short: Specific-order coefficient counts rely on native_decide (compiler trust) rather than the general bijection; and the total-division convention silently admitted physically-meaningless proofs (κ>0 unused) until caught and patched in Round 5 — a latent correctness pattern in the WKB layer.
remediation: native_decide on small finite counts is project-tolerated and low-risk (the general formula has a real bijection proof). The total-division gap was already remediated in Round 5 (turning_point_shift_nonzero, firstOrder_correction_zero_iff, dampingRate_eq_zero_iff with genuinely load-bearing hypotheses). No further action needed beyond confirming Round-5 theorems remain in the live tree.
evidence: 'native_decide: count(3) = 3'; 'Lean 4 uses total division (0/0 = 0), which papers over the physical requirement that κ > 0'

title: Phase 2 Direction D required a model restructuring; second-order FDR was a labeled CONJECTURE before CGL derivation
description: Deep research (2026-03-24) found the original FirstOrderKMS algebraic constraints 'were not derivable from the CGL transformation as formulated' — the FDR relations i₁β=−r₂, i₂β=r₁+r₂ 'pair noise with even-m conservative coefficients, which the CGL condition does not do.' Resolution required restructuring the SK-EFT model to separate conservative (even-ω) and dissipative (odd-ω) retarded terms. Impact: 'FirstOrderKMS and FullSecondOrderKMS structures need revision ... their physical interpretation changes.' The second-order FDR was 'already labeled CONJECTURE' in the paper before Direction D.
what_falls_short: A foundational consistency issue (the KMS/FDR pairing) required model restructuring and reinterpretation mid-phase; the prior 40/40 proofs 'remain valid as algebraic results but their physical interpretation changes.' This is now resolved (CGL derivation, cgl_implies_secondOrderKMS), so it is a historical walk-back rather than an open weakness.
remediation: Resolved: Direction D derived the FDR from CGL first principles (CGLTransform.lean, 5/5 proved) and the paper wording fix ('dissipative'→'real-sector', line 79) was noted. Confirm CGLTransform.lean remains in the live tree and the paper no longer labels the second-order FDR a conjecture.
evidence: 'the original FirstOrderKMS algebraic constraints ... were not derivable from the CGL transformation as formulated ... Resolution: Restructure the SK-EFT model'

title: Phase 5p FPDimension Wave 1 — entire FP-dimension derivation rests on per-MTC native_decide (no Perron-Frobenius theorem)
description: FP dimensions are 'derived' by verifying eigenvector equations `N_X · d = FPdim(X) · d` via `native_decide` over QSqrt5/QSqrt2/ℤ rather than via a Perron-Frobenius theorem. Roadmap: 'Perron-Frobenius NOT proved but FPdimCertificate eigenvector approach sidesteps it.' The dominant-eigenvalue (largest-root) property — the actual content of FP dimension — 'is implicit in the eigenvector witness ... formal Perron-Frobenius statement deferred to Wave 2', and Wave 2 leaves it '[ ] State Perron-Frobenius theorem ... (deferred — ... PF theorem is unproved upstream)'. Confirmed: FPDimension.lean still carries 23 native_decide occurrences.
what_falls_short: The phase motivation is 'deriving quantum dimensions FROM fusion rules via Perron-Frobenius eigenvalues' — i.e., a first-principles derivation. What ships instead checks that a guessed eigenvector satisfies the eigenvalue equation for each specific MTC by kernel decision procedure, and does NOT prove FPdim is the LARGEST eigenvalue (the defining property). The 'derivation' is per-MTC computational verification, not the structural Perron-Frobenius result. Per Invariant #10 and the 6AO Track-3 precedent, native_decide is often eliminable with structural refactor.
remediation: State and prove Perron-Frobenius for irreducible non-negative integer matrices (Mathlib has `Matrix.IsIrreducible` scaffolding) to convert eigenvector witnesses into a genuine dominant-eigenvalue derivation; then the per-MTC native_decides become specializations of one structural theorem (mirroring 6AO Track-3's elimination of 4 KMM native_decides).

Largest-root property is implicit in the eigenvector witness ... formal Perron-Frobenius statement deferred to Wave 2.' / Wave 2: '[ ] State Perron-Frobenius theorem for non-negative matrices (deferred — ... PF theorem is unproved upstream)'.

Two instances do not constitute the structural formula. The phase delivers concrete-instance verification while the named structural theorem stays a conjecture-shaped open checkbox.

evidence: 'on type-III von Neumann factor ... −log ρ_A is not well-defined as a self-adjoint operator. The operator-algebraic substitute is K_A = −log Δ_A ... bundled as a hypothesis input in CHEntropyHypotheses rather than constructed.'

title: Phase 5q Ext-over-A(1) computation rests entirely on native_decide on F_2 matrices
source: Phase5q_Roadmap.md lines 39, 64, 84, 168-188; verified A1Resolution.lean has 18 native_decide occurrences
description: The entire algebraic core of the spin-bordism computation (the minimal free resolution of F_2 over A(1) through degree 5, d^2=0 at every level, exactness via RREF witnesses, A(1)-linearity, minimality) is machine-checked exclusively via native_decide on Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) (ZMod 2). The roadmap states this is 'the ONLY viable path' because Mathlib's homology pipeline is noncomputable. A1Resolution.lean still carries 18 live native_decide calls as of current ground truth.
what_falls_short: Per Invariant #10, native_decide is project-tolerated only where genuinely un-decide-able; 6AO Track-3 showed nd is often eliminable via structural-lemma refactor. These nd calls back the generation-constraint headline (3 | N_f) via the Ext dimensions, so they are load-bearing. They are decide-able (finite F_2 matrices) but rely on the compiler rather than the kernel — not 'bulletproof' in the kernel-pure sense.
remediation: Replace native_decide with kernel-level decide or explicit Finset/DecidableEq-driven proofs on the F_2 matrices (small: largest is 24x32). Where decide times out under heartbeat budget, decompose per-block as 6AO Track-3 did for KMM. RREF-witness arithmetic (P*d=RREF, P*P^{-1}=I) is exactly the kind of finite product that explicit computation can discharge.
evidence: 'The brute-force matrix approach with native_decide is the ONLY viable path' (line 64); 'Prove d_{n-1} * d_n = 0 via native_decide. Five proofs, all trivial.' (line 168); grep A1Resolution.lean = 18 native_decide.

(M2) Adams spectral sequence machinery - long-horizon Mathlib milestone, estimated 1-3 years' (lines 399-404); 'H4's discharge must not implicitly use Rokhlin's theorem ... If the discharge requires Rokhlin, H4 graduates from textbook hypothesis to circular dependency' (line 436).

title: H4 (Anderson-Brown-Peterson splitting) carries an unresolved circularity caveat against Rokhlin

remediation: Independently of Mathlib, audit the intended ABP discharge route for a non-Rokhlin proof path and document it explicitly in HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY before any discharge is attempted. If no non-circular route exists, retain H4 permanently as a tracked hypothesis and disclose the dependency.

The post-Phase-6r-prime audit found these were largely anti-patterns: of 12, only 2 survived as honest tracked Props (Kirby-Taylor 1990, DMNO 2010). The substantive content was a fraction of what the headline claimed at 6r close.
remediation: Already remediated in 6r-prime (8 deleted/restructured, ~5000 LoC of substantive substrate added across 18+ modules). Verify the M-R dual-phase adversarial review actually closed GREEN and that the 2 surviving Props (KT 1990, DMNO 2010) are in PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md. The lesson: a single-session 'all 8 waves shipped' close is a red flag for under-strengthening.

remediation: Dispatch the recommended follow-up DR scout on paper 17 before landing any paper-shaped output that consumes AlternativeBoundaries; until then keep IsDarkSectorTopologicalBoundary clearly hedged as paper-17-conditional. 6r-prime restructured it with a hidden-sector witness conjunct, improving non-vacuity.
evidence: 'Paper 17 NOT directly accessed. Wave 3b.1 dark-sector cross-bridge is HOLD pending a follow-up DR scout on paper 17 directly. Plausible Q3 priors documented ... but unverified.' (line 385).

title: Phase 6AA W-state asymptotic D/(D+1)->1 equality deliberately NOT formalized (open conjecture)
description: The W-state distillation rate (Fortescue-Lo) is formalized only as the finite-round lower bound D/(D+1) and the >=1 lower bound; the asymptotic =1 equality is 'the open W-state =1 conjecture' and is explicitly excluded: 'Do NOT formalize: the W-state =1 asymptotic equality (open conjecture)'.
what_falls_short: The W-state rate result stops at the finite-round D/(D+1) bound and does not deliver the asymptotic optimal-rate equality, which remains a genuine open conjecture in the literature. This is a correct, honest descope (the equality is genuinely open), not an effort-descope.
remediation: None - the =1 equality is an open conjecture; formalizing the finite-round bound is the correct ceiling. Ensure papers claim only the D/(D+1) finite-round result and the >=1 bound, never the asymptotic equality.
evidence: 'W-state Fortescue-Lo lower bound >=1 + finite-round D/(D+1) ONLY (NOT the open =1 equality)' (line 27); 'Do NOT formalize: the W-state =1 asymptotic equality (open conjecture)' (line 32).

title: Phase 6AA envelope theorem is a [1/4,1] band - a within-range tautology-risk bound, not a tight certificate
description: The Phase 6AA centerpiece envelope theorem swapChain_fidelity_envelope states that a k-swap Werner chain with per-link F in [1/4,1] has end-to-end fidelity provably in [1/4,1]. The bounds are the same [1/4,1] interval that the inputs are constrained to, plus monotone-in-fidelity and antitone-in-length.
what_falls_short: An end-to-end metric provably in [1/4,1] given inputs in [1/4,1] is close to a within-band tautology (the trivial physical range of Werner fidelity is [1/4,1]); the substantive content is in the monotonicity/antitonicity, not the band itself. Per the strengthening discipline's 'within-+/-2sigma-band tautology' anti-pattern, the headline envelope is weaker than a tight parameterized [lo,hi] certificate would be.
remediation: Strengthen to genuinely tight, parameter-dependent envelopes (e.g. F_e2e^(k) exact closed form (1+3w^k)/4 with w=(4F-1)/3 already shipped in W4) so the [lo,hi] interval is a non-trivial function of (F,k,channel params), not the static physical range. The exact closed form already exists in EndToEnd.lean; promote it to the headline envelope.
evidence: 'swapChain_fidelity_envelope - k-swap Werner chain with per-link F in [1/4,1] has end-to-end fidelity provably in [1/4,1] (model-independent) + monotone-in-fidelity + antitone-in-length' (line 45).

(This was the correct call at the time and the general layer was subsequently built in Phase 6AE/6AI, so this descope is now superseded.)

description: Phase 6AE was declared 'DONE (2026-06-01)' having proven only the step-1 PSD->trace bridge (traceNorm_posSemidef) plus partialTrace/choiMatrix substrate. The entire deep core - trace-norm triangle inequality, traceDist<=1, Uhlmann fidelity + Fuchs-van de Graaf, CPTP contractivity (data processing), diamond-norm sup + norm axioms - was left as a 'clearly-documented deferred frontier' citing the blocker (Mathlib at pin lacks von Neumann trace inequality / Ky Fan / polar decomposition / Schatten norm).

Update the 6AE roadmap status from 'DONE via deferred-frontier' to reflect the subsequent closure, and verify each named deferred lemma (triangle, contractivity, FvdG) was actually proven, not just adjacent modules added.

title: Phase 6AE-A trace-norm triangle inequality is the unproven hard core gating the whole metric structure
description: The trace-norm triangle inequality ||A+B||_1 <= ||A||_1 + ||B||_1 is identified as 'the hard core', provable only via the dual/variational characterization ||A||_1 = sup_{||U||<=1}|tr(U^H A)|. Without it, traceDist in [0,1] (the basic metric-space property of trace distance) cannot be established. At 6AE close this was deferred.
what_falls_short: The most basic structural property of the trace-distance metric (triangle inequality, hence metric-space and [0,1] range) was not proven at 6AE close, leaving the 'metric' framing of traceDist incomplete at that point.
remediation: Verify against current QuantumNetwork/MixedState.lean / FidelityBounds.lean whether the triangle inequality and traceDist<=1 were subsequently proven (later QI arcs 6AF-6AI built the dual/variational substrate, e.g. HermitianCarrier + InnerProductSpace, used in 6AI). If still open, it is a tractable dual-norm build, not a wall.
evidence: 'Trace-norm triangle inequality ||A+B||_1 <= ||A||_1+||B||_1 - the hard core; via the dual/variational characterization' (line 33); '6AE-A step 1 ... REMAINING: traceDist in [0,1] upper bound (needs triangle), step 3 triangle (hard core), step 4 fidelity, step 5 contractivity, step 6 diamond norm' (line 25).

title: Phase 6AI diamondDist_eq_choiSDP closure was preceded by multiple refuted DR shortcuts and a 'one-lemma-to-multi-week' context-loss episode
description: Phase 6AI ultimately PROVED diamondDist_eq_choiSDP unconditionally kernel-pure (Update 15, 2026-06-03, verified axioms {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}). But the arc included: a refuted naive dual-Farkas route ('confirmed DEAD-END (reproves only weak duality) - STRUCK'); a first DR whose explicit W* formula 'was also wrong (corrected ...)'; a second DR whose sigma=1/d recommendation was 'MATHEMATICALLY FALSE' (numerically refuted); and a stretch where a 'one lemma to multi-week' reduction was diagnosed as lost context. A stale memory ('conic duality absent') was found wrong for the pin.
what_falls_short: The headline itself is fully closed and kernel-pure (NOT a current weakness). The finding is the process residue: two deep-research returns gave mathematically false guidance that was caught only by direct calculation/numerics, and a long dead-end side-path (the (★) operator inequality / diamondWitness route) was pursued before recovery. Useful as a record that DR output here required adversarial verification.
remediation: No remediation needed for the result (proven unconditional kernel-pure). Carry the lesson: DR formulas for hard QI lemmas must be verify-by-calculation before use (the roadmap already flags one as 'verify, do not cite'). Ensure stale 'conic duality absent' memory notes are updated for the v4.29.1 pin.
evidence: 'OUTCOME UPDATE 8 ... BOTH DR SHORTCUTS REFUTED'; 'Its headline recommendation ... is MATHEMATICALLY FALSE' (line 374); 'OUTCOME UPDATE 15 ... diamondDist_eq_choiSDP PROVEN UNCONDITIONAL, kernel-pure (lean_verify: axioms {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound})' (line 547).

The 'all orders in curvature' claim is all-orders-in-curvature-at-mean-field, not all-orders-in-quantum-corrections.

title: Phase 6e cross-channel PPN falsifier (precession = 2/3 x deflection) is the sole load-bearing testable prediction and is a multiplicative structural identity
description: Phase 6e Wave 4's load-bearing testable falsifier for the nonlinear EFE is the cross-channel structural claim 'precession_dev = (2/3) x deflection_dev' relating multi-channel PPN observable deviations. This is presented as the headline observable prediction distinguishing T_emerg from bare matter T_munu.
what_falls_short: A fixed-ratio (2/3) relation between two PPN deviation channels is a multiplication-only structural identity (P3 anti-pattern: 'multiplication-only theorems are not physics') rather than a quantitative bound tied to a measured value. Its falsifiability depends on independently measuring both channels; as stated it is a definitional ratio between computed deviations.
remediation: Strengthen the prediction to a quantitative norm_num-backed comparison against a measured PPN bound (e.g. Cassini gamma, lunar-laser-ranging beta) rather than only the inter-channel ratio, so the falsifier is anchored to an experimental number, per the strengthening discipline's quantitative-connection requirement.

title: Phase 6AL: mutualInformation_nonneg and matrixLog_kronecker carry full-rank/PosDef side conditions
description: The shipped subadditivity/mutual-information results require full-rank (PosDef) marginals — the matrixLog_kronecker identity FAILS at zero eigenvalues, and mutualInformation_nonneg needs PosDef marginals (the standard Klein domain condition). Wave-1 concavity (A) similarly requires ρ̄ PosDef.
what_falls_short: The entropy/mutual-information theorems do not cover rank-deficient (boundary) density operators — a real restriction relative to the fully general QI statement, though it is the standard Klein-inequality domain.
remediation: This is an honest standard domain restriction (Klein's inequality genuinely needs full support). Extending to rank-deficient states requires limiting/continuity arguments (entropy is continuous but matrixLog blows up); document as a permanent honest-scope note rather than a defect unless a downstream consumer needs the boundary case.
evidence: 'Honest scope note: mutualInformation_nonneg needs full-rank (PosDef) marginals (standard Klein domain condition).'

The genuine theorems (Penrose geodesic-incompleteness from a real Lorentzian metric) are abstracted onto comparison hypotheses.

description: The central Phase 5h result that a 3+1D anomaly-free SPT phase admits a chiral lattice gauge theory (anomaly_free_implies_chiral_gauge, sm_generation_gapped_interface, sm_three_gen_gapped_interface) holds only under hypothesis TPFConjecture — the existence of gapped symmetric interfaces, which the roadmap itself flags as 'precisely statable as axiom, but untestable (no numerical evidence)' and 'still conjectural'.
what_falls_short: The positive direction of the chirality-wall result is not unconditional — it is gated on a research-grade conjecture with no numerical support. Only the contrapositive no-go (no_gap_implies_anomalous) and kapustin_fidkowski_nogo are rigorous/unconditional.
remediation: TPFConjecture is correctly catalogued as KEEP_AS_TRACKED (research-grade, the TPF/SMG gapped-interface existence in 4+1D Hilbert space is a genuinely open physics question — Wang-Wen/You). The honest framing (consumers carry (H:TPFConjecture)) is the principled treatment. No discharge is feasible without a new microscopic substrate; ensure any paper headline states the conditionality explicitly.
evidence: 'TPF gapped interface — precisely statable as axiom, but untestable (no numerical evidence)' ... roadmap item 'anomaly_free_implies_chiral_gauge: conditional theorem from axiom'.

remediation: The disentangler-existence question is part of the same TPF/SMG conjectural frontier (non-compact symmetry can't be disentangled by a finite-depth on-site circuit — an open lattice-QFT question). Keep as documented open obstruction; the conditional gauging theorem is the honest deliverable. Track the 3 Golterman-Shamir open questions explicitly.
evidence: 'GT gauging step — deepest mathematical obstruction (non-compact + Onsager algebra)' ... 'The disentangler existence is the conjectural input (from gapped_interface_axiom).'

what_falls_short: At discovery, the load-bearing D2/L2 'Ext^n_{A(1)}(F₂,F₂) dimension' claims were not statements about Mathlib's real Ext functor; change-of-rings (H2) was a True placeholder.

remediation: Execute Wave T4 (depends on T2+T3 shipping real Ext first): build the noncommutative restrictScalars⊣coextendScalars adjunction and lift to Ext via dimension-shifting. Decision gate: if full generality balloons, ship the per-n dimension-equality form (NOT a True placeholder) and leave the natural-iso as tracked follow-up. Flip HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY H2 only on real content.
evidence: 'Replace h2_discharged : True with a proof of Ext^n_A(A ⊗_{A(1)} F₂, F₂) ≅ Ext^n_{A(1)}(F₂, F₂) ... Risk. Medium-high (Mathlib lacks Shapiro; may need upstreamable lemmas).'

The ANCILLA route (Track 2) genuinely removes it for the 2-ancilla headline; the ancilla-FREE single-qubit version remains tracked-Prop pending sieve-theory machinery (Landau-Ramanujan) Mathlib does not have. Keep as tracked Prop; do not axiomatize.

remediation: The amendment is justified by the genuine prime-density wall and was subsequently superseded by the Track-2 ancilla route (KMM) which DOES deliver unconditional O(log 1/eps) with <=2 ancillas. The single-qubit ancilla-free unconditional remains wall-gated. Mostly resolved by the KMM ancilla program.
evidence: 'W5 per the amended gate -- O(log 1/eps) efficiency + axiom-free section-6-gate Prop' (line 46); cross-ref memory note 'W5 AMENDED gate ... full forall-U-unconditional = GENUINE literature-open prime-density wall'.

title: Phase 6AO Track-2 column lemma carries an O(1)-but-large constant (length <= 280*colDenExp + 9; universal <= 50400k+812)
description: The dim-4 Giles-Selinger column lemma headline is quantitative but with an enormous constant: 'length <= 280*colDenExp + 9'. The universal-U headline is 'length <= 50400*k + 812'. These are O(log 1/eps) (exponent 1, asymptotically optimal) but the leading constants (280, 16800, 50400) are far from the optimal T-count constants in the literature.
what_falls_short: While asymptotic optimality (exponent 1) is achieved and genuinely matches the constant-ancilla lower bound, the concrete constants are orders of magnitude above optimal. The roadmap notes the finer kmm_lemma3/SO(3) optimality (which would tighten the constant) is 'ancilla-free-only and not required.' For a publication claiming optimal synthesis, the gap between proven-constant and optimal-constant is a quality caveat.
remediation: The exponent-1 asymptotic optimality is the load-bearing claim and is honestly proven. If the paper claims tight constants, tighten via the SO(3) optimality route; otherwise ensure prose says 'O(log 1/eps), asymptotically optimal exponent' not 'optimal T-count'.
evidence: 'column_lemma_bounded: every unit Z[w][1/sqrt2] column is the first column of an exact 2-qubit Clifford+T word of length <= 280*colDenExp + 9' (line 373); 'length <= 50400*k + 812' (line 409).

title: Phase 6AO Track 1(c) shipped via mid-course correction after a MATHEMATICALLY IMPOSSIBLE original plan (GaussInt2 Euclidean)
description: The original Track-1(b) plan ('GaussInt2 = Z[sqrt2][i] EuclideanDomain') was found 'MATHEMATICALLY IMPOSSIBLE as roadmapped' during primary-source verification -- Z[sqrt2][i] is an index-2 non-integrally-closed subring of Z[w], never a UFD let alone Euclidean. The correct object is the full ZOmega = Z[zeta_8]. Separately a Z[sqrt2][i] 'integral domain' brick was later shipped (line 611) as a partial consolation.
what_falls_short: A roadmapped headline was mathematically wrong and had to be re-routed. The corrected ZOmega Euclidean route was then shipped successfully, so the net is resolved, but it documents that roadmap claims were not always pre-verified against the primary source.
remediation: Resolved: ZOmegaEuclideanDomain shipped (e78d8c43, line 459). Process lesson -- primary-source-verify ring-theoretic claims before roadmapping them as deliverables.
evidence: 'GaussInt2 = Z[sqrt2][i] EuclideanDomain -- MATHEMATICALLY IMPOSSIBLE as roadmapped -- ... so it is never a UFD, let alone Euclidean; caught by the primary-source verification.' (lines 455-458).

title: Phase 5q.C residual hypothesis topo' (Arf(q-bar)=0) is a PERMANENT irreducible tracked input for 16|sigma
description: The Rokhlin 16|sigma theorem depends on the smooth-category input topo' : arfOfForm form = 0 (the Freedman-Kirby Arf-vanishing), refactored from the opaque 2|sigma/8. The roadmap is explicit this is 'the permanent irreducible hypothesis (honest by mathematical necessity -- E8 is the algebraic counterexample), not a pending-a-spike placeholder.' The geometric discharge (Phase 2) is DEFERRED as a bottom-row frontier never formalized in any proof assistant.
what_falls_short: 16|sigma is NOT fully unconditional: it consumes one irreducible smooth-topological hypothesis. The theorem is algebraically false (E8 has sigma=8), so a smooth-category input is mathematically required. The geometric discharge needs singular cohomology + cup product + Poincare duality + spin structure + Wu + Guillou-Marin congruence -- all Mathlib-absent, the capstone never formalized anywhere.
remediation: This is named literature wall (b)-adjacent: the residual is mathematically irreducible at the algebraic level (E8 counterexample) and the geometric discharge requires absent Mathlib cohomology/PD foundations + a tier-2 capstone (Guillou-Marin). The honest move (made) is to name the single tracked input precisely as Arf(q-bar)=0. Cannot be removed without the geometric frontier.
evidence: 'topo' is therefore the permanent irreducible hypothesis (honest by mathematical necessity -- E8 is the algebraic counterexample), not a pending-a-spike placeholder.' (lines 101-102); ADR-003 '16|sigma <=> (8|sigma) and (2|sigma/8); we proved 8|sigma; 2|sigma/8 is irreducible (the theorem is false algebraically -- E8, sigma=8 ...)' (lines 17-18).

title: Note: Phase 5q.C roadmap describes Phase-1 Arf-bridge as a to-do; ground truth suggests it may be shipped
description: Phase 5q.C Phase 1 (the Arf-bridge: prove sigma/8 = Arf(q-bar) mod 2, refactor topo->topo') is written as an active /goal to execute (waves C.1-C.4 with a paste-to-run prompt), not marked complete in the roadmap. Whether it has been executed is not confirmable from this roadmap alone; if executed, the residual is now expressed as Arf(q-bar)=0 (which Phase5qD line 100 references as already-sharpened: 'Phase 5q.C sharpens the one irreducible topological residue (Arf(q-bar)=0)').
what_falls_short: Roadmap status ambiguity: the roadmap body reads as not-yet-done (it contains an executable /goal prompt), but Phase5qD cross-references the residual as already sharpened to Arf(q-bar)=0, implying Phase-1 shipped. Either way the underlying tracked-hypothesis weakness (prior finding) stands; this is a status-freshness note.
remediation: Confirm Phase 5q.C Phase-1 completion status against the live HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY['rokhlin_sigma_mod_16'] and ArfInvariant.lean; update the roadmap status header if shipped (it currently lacks a COMPLETE banner).
evidence: Phase5qC has a full '/goal prompt (Phase 1 -- paste to run autonomously)' (line 117) suggesting un-run; Phase5qD line 100: 'Phase 5q.C sharpens the one irreducible topological residue (Arf(q-bar)=0) on the spectra-free side' suggesting done.

title: Phase 5q.D: H1/H3/H4 ko/Adams hypotheses carried as Prop := True placeholders (narrative not literally true)
description: Three topological hypotheses underpinning the D2/L2 ko/Adams narrative are carried as 'Prop := True pointers (by design, to avoid axioms)': H1 (H*(ko;F2) = A//A(1)), H3 (Adams SS for ko collapses at E2), H4 (Anderson-Brown-Peterson splitting => Omega^Spin_4 = Z). The roadmap states the D2/L2 ko/Adams content 'stays motivational narrative' unless a trigger fires, and these are 'Prop := True' placeholders making the narrative not literally true.
what_falls_short: The ko/Adams spectral-sequence framing in papers D2/L2 is narrative/motivational, not formally backed -- the three load-bearing topological facts are placeholder True. The entire prerequisite stack (spectra, Steenrod action on H*(ko), Adams SS, Thom spectra MSpin, ko, ABP) is Mathlib-absent and partly research-grade (~15-25 PY traditional).
remediation: Phase 5q.B already delivers 16|sigma unconditionally WITHOUT this frontier, so H1/H3/H4 are off the critical path -- their only payoff is upgrading narrative to wired theorem. Keep as Prop:=True (avoids axioms) and ensure D2/L2 prose flags the ko/Adams framing as motivational, not proven. Only build on trigger (Mathlib ships spectra/Adams/Thom).
evidence: 'Discharge the three topological hypotheses currently carried as Prop := True pointers (by design, to avoid axioms), making the D2/L2 ko/Adams narrative literally true' (lines 34-35); 'otherwise the D2/L2 ko/Adams content stays motivational narrative' (line 83).

title: Phase 5q.D H4 (ABP) carries a circularity hazard for Rokhlin
description: H4 (Anderson-Brown-Peterson splitting yielding Omega^Spin_4 = Z) 'is circular for Rokhlin' -- ABP historically uses Rokhlin-equivalent inputs. If ever built, H4 'MUST route through the Adams SS, not any Rokhlin-equivalent fact -- otherwise the derivation is circular.'
what_falls_short: If H4 were discharged naively it would be circular (using a Rokhlin-equivalent input to prove a Rokhlin-leg fact). This is a documented anti-circularity constraint on a deferred build, not an active error, but it flags that the 'wired' narrative would be unsound if built carelessly.
remediation: Constraint is documented (route through Adams SS). Only relevant on activation, which is trigger-gated and off the 16|sigma critical path. No action needed unless Leg D is funded.
evidence: 'H4 is circular for Rokhlin. ABP historically uses Rokhlin-equivalent inputs ... If ever built, H4 must route through the Adams SS, not any Rokhlin-equivalent fact -- otherwise the derivation is circular.' (lines 26-28).

title: Phase 5a Wave 3A: Z16 classification framework shipped as an axiom (z16_classification : Omega_4^Pin+ = Z16)
description: Phase 5a Wave 3A axiomatized the Z16 classification of 4D Pin+ bordism: 'axiom z16_classification : Omega_4^{Pin+} = Z16 -- axiomatized (cobordism = open problem)'. Z16Classification.lean shipped '21 theorems + 1 axiom'. Multiple downstream conditional theorems (z16_strengthens_mod8, smg_phase_classification, the ChiralityWallMasterTheorem z16_axiom field) are gated on this axiom. The full proof is stated as '15-25 person-years away'.
what_falls_short: At the time, a project-local axiom encoded an open mathematical problem (Pin+ cobordism = Z16). Per Pipeline Invariant #15 axioms are temporary scaffolding. Ground truth now reports axiom count = 0, so this axiom has since been converted to a tracked-Prop or otherwise discharged/removed.
remediation: Per ground truth (axioms: 0; PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES has 7 entries), this axiom was converted to a tracked Prop after the roadmap was written. Verify Z16Classification.lean now uses a tracked-Prop, not an axiom, and that the roadmap text (still saying '21 theorems + 1 axiom') is updated to reflect the conversion. The underlying cobordism computation remains a literature wall (15-25 PY).
evidence: 'axiom z16_classification : Omega_4^{Pin+} = Z16 -- axiomatized (cobordism = open problem)' (line 249); 'Z16Classification.lean -- 21 theorems + 1 axiom (ZERO sorry)' (line 258). Ground truth: axioms: 0 -- so HISTORICAL/stale.

title: Phase 5a Wave 5A: SMG spectral gap axiomatized via HasSpectralGap typeclass (Yang-Mills/Millennium difficulty)
description: Wave 5A's SMGClassification.lean ships 'HasSpectralGap typeclass (axiomatized -- gap existence is Yang-Mills difficulty)' and a GappedInterfaceConjecture. All SMG existence theorems are conditional on this axiomatized gap. The full spectral-gap proof is listed as 'blocked -- Millennium Prize difficulty'. This is the historical gapped_interface_axiom (pre-Phase-6p the project's single axiom).
what_falls_short: The SMG result rests on an axiomatized spectral gap (Yang-Mills mass-gap-class difficulty). All downstream SMG conclusions are conditional. Ground truth now reports axioms: 0 and PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES contains 'TPFConjecture [ex gapped_interface_axiom]' -- so this axiom was converted to a tracked Prop.
remediation: Confirmed converted: ground truth lists 'TPFConjecture [ex gapped_interface_axiom]' as a tracked Prop. The spectral-gap existence remains a genuine literature wall (Millennium-class). Ensure roadmap/registry name it as a tracked hypothesis, not an axiom. No discharge feasible (correctly deferred).
evidence: 'HasSpectralGap typeclass (axiomatized -- gap existence is Yang-Mills difficulty)' (line 373); conditional-items table 'Spectral gap proof for SMG | Mathematical breakthrough | blocked -- Millennium Prize difficulty' (line 403). Ground truth: 'TPFConjecture [ex gapped_interface_axiom]' tracked-Prop, axioms: 0.

title: Phase 5a SteenrodA1: A(1) cardinality and Ext computation rely on native_decide
description: SteenrodA1.lean uses native_decide to confirm the A1Basis cardinality: 'A1Basis inductive type (8 elements, native_decide confirms cardinality)'. The A(1) Ext computation (Ext^4 = Z16 = 2^4) is the partial-discharge layer of the Z16 axiom.
what_falls_short: A native_decide backs the A(1) dimension claim, which is load-bearing for the Ext^4=Z16 partial axiom discharge. Per Pipeline Invariant #10 this is project-tolerated only where genuinely un-decide-able; a finite 8-element cardinality is almost certainly kernel-decidable.
remediation: An 8-element Fintype cardinality should be provable by kernel 'decide' or 'rfl' rather than native_decide; eliminate it (cf. Phase 6AO Track-3 pattern of replacing nd with kernel decide). Low priority -- it is a leaf cardinality check, not a headline.
evidence: 'A1Basis inductive type (8 elements, native_decide confirms cardinality)' (line 290).

title: Phase 5a Wave 2B GTCommutation shipped with 3 sorrys at roadmap-write time (later filled by Aristotle)
description: GTCommutation.lean was checkboxed shipped with '10 theorems, 3 sorry (Aristotle in flight)' including the central gt_chiral_charge_non_compact. The status line later notes 'Aristotle run 18969de2 filled the 3 prior sorrys' giving 0 sorry. This is a documented temporary-sorry that was resolved.
what_falls_short: Internal inconsistency in the roadmap (deliverable list says 3 sorry, status says 0 sorry filled). Resolved per the status line; ground truth confirms sorry_declarations: 0 project-wide. Documents a transient Aristotle-dependency gap, not a live weakness.
remediation: Resolved (Aristotle 18969de2 + ground-truth 0 sorry). Optionally clean the roadmap's stale '3 sorry' deliverable checkbox to match the COMPLETE status. No technical action.
evidence: 'GTCommutation.lean -- 10 theorems, 3 sorry (Aristotle in flight)' (line 196) vs 'SHIPPED -- ... 0 sorry, ALL PROVED (Aristotle run 18969de2 filled the 3 prior sorrys)' (line 221). Ground truth: sorry: 0.

title: Phase 5a defers major objectives to Phase 6 on person-year/HPC effort grounds
description: Phase 5a explicitly moves several objectives to 'Phase 6 (requires HPC, collaboration, or breakthrough)': spectral gap proof for SMG, full Z16 cobordism formalization (15-25 PY), verified MC statistical pipeline (5-10 PY), Fidkowski-Kitaev 1D Z8 full construction. The conditional-items table marks these 'blocked' on effort/infrastructure.
what_falls_short: Four substantive objectives are deferred. Of these, the spectral-gap and full-cobordism items are genuine literature walls (Millennium / 15-25 PY algebraic topology). But the project's own policy (no de-scope from effort magnitude; ~10-15k LOC/day) means effort-only deferrals are real open weaknesses, not justified stops. The verified-statistics pipeline and FK-1D are effort/infrastructure deferrals.
remediation: Spectral gap (Millennium) and full Z16 cobordism (absent Mathlib stable-homotopy) are genuine walls -- correctly deferred. Verified-statistics and FK-1D Z8 are infrastructure/effort deferrals: per project policy these should be scheduled as their own /goals with a real-world reason in the roadmap, not left as open-ended 'Phase 6' blocks. FK-1D is marked 'conditional 6-12 months'.
evidence: 'What moves to Phase 6 (requires HPC, collaboration, or breakthrough): Spectral gap proof for SMG ...; Full Z16 cobordism formalization (15-25 person-years ...); Verified MC statistical pipeline (5-10 person-years ...); Fidkowski-Kitaev 1D Z8 full construction' (lines 37-42).

title: Phase 6AF FvdG-upper fence was initially drawn TOO WIDE (claimed Uhlmann-purification wall that was false)
description: The Fuchs-van de Graaf upper bound D <= sqrt(1-F^2) was first fenced as a DEFERRED FRONTIER 'genuinely blocked on Uhlmann purification (grep-verified absent) ... multi-week/blocked.' A later DR found a purification-FREE route (Holevo-Helstrom representation) and FvdG-upper was PROVEN. The roadmap documents this as the FOURTH over-wide fence in the phase (general triangle, diamond-norm def, diamond-distance triangle were the prior three).
what_falls_short: A load-bearing analytic result was incorrectly declared blocked/multi-week four separate times in one phase; each turned out reachable. This is a recurring fence-discipline failure (over-conservative blocked-claims), not a live gap -- all four were ultimately proven. It signals systematic over-fencing risk in the project's risk assessments.
remediation: All four fences discharged (FvdG-upper proven, commit 9e1ed559). The documented lesson (mandatory fresh fan-out + arithmetic-verified absent-brick argument before ANY fence) is the correct remediation and is encoded in the roadmap. No technical action; the meta-finding is that 'blocked/multi-week' assessments in this corpus are unreliable and skew toward false-negative.
evidence: 'FENCE-WAS-TOO-WIDE CATCH ... the FvdG-UPPER fence claimed every route needs Uhlmann purification (absent) => multi-week/blocked. The DR ... found a purification-FREE route' (line 61); 'THREE fences were drawn too wide this phase' (line 38) + FvdG = the fourth (line 53).

title: Phase 6AF Choi-SDP primal=dual EQUALITY remains the one permanently-open frontier (conic strong duality absent)
description: The full Watrous primal=dual SDP characterization of the diamond norm is fenced as a documented-deferred frontier: Mathlib at the pin 'has cone-dual DEFINITIONS ... but NO conic/SDP STRONG-DUALITY theorem (zero gap + dual attainment under Slater) -- grep-verified absent; that is the genuine missing brick.' Only the primal LOWER bound (diamondDist_ge_maxEntangled) and the Choi UPPER bound (diamondDist <= n*||C||_inf) are proven -- the sandwich, not the equality.
what_falls_short: The diamond norm's exact SDP/Choi characterization is not proven -- only a two-sided sandwich (primal lower + Choi upper). The equality requires conic strong duality (Slater zero-gap + dual attainment), absent from Mathlib at the pin. The roadmap correctly does not claim the full SDP identity.
remediation: This is a genuine missing-Mathlib-brick (conic strong duality), not effort-only. The sandwich (||J||_1/d <= ||.||_diamond <= d*||J||_inf) is the shipped, honest deliverable and is sufficient for certification. Build conic strong duality if a publication needs the literal SDP equality; otherwise the sandwich is the correct stopping point.
evidence: 'ONLY remaining documented frontier (no sorry/axiom): the primal=dual Choi-SDP equality (conic strong duality absent).' (line 62); 'NO conic/SDP STRONG-DUALITY theorem ... grep-verified absent; that is the genuine missing brick for Watrous primal=dual.' (line 60).

title: Phase 6m is a deep-research-synthesis phase: verdicts rest on DR dossiers + norm_num bounds, not first-principles derivations
description: Phase 6m's entire scientific content (Tracks A/B/C: 50 substantive Lean thms across CausalSetDarkEnergy/EntropicGravity/JacobsonThermoGR/DarkSectorClassification) encodes verdicts that 'derive' from six-round deep-research dossiers. The Lean theorems are largely norm_num inequality encodings of literature-sourced sigma/log-B thresholds (e.g. 'Verlinde >= 5sigma; Tsallis >= 6.2'), structural flags, and classification-table appends -- NOT independent physics derivations. 'Full causal-set lattice simulation -- beyond formalization scope; deep-research synthesis only.'
what_falls_short: The NO-GO/CLEARED verdicts are as strong as the cited DR dossiers and the external Bayesian-evidence numbers (Tyagi 2504.11308, Luciano 2506.03019, Plaza-Kraiselburd 2504.05432, etc.). The Lean layer formalizes that these numbers exceed Jeffreys-decisive thresholds; it does not derive the numbers. Many verdicts are conditional on r_d-anchoring choice (fixed vs floating) and DESI DR2 contours that DR3/Roman could shift. Several are explicitly 'CLEARED-R4 vacuous' (c_s^2 = 1 by prescription, not derived).
remediation: Appropriate for a review-flavored Phys.Rep./PRD paper (Paper 45), but the paper prose must not present the norm_num encodings as first-principles results -- they are literature-attributed thresholds formalized for consistency. Ensure citation provenance (CHECK 14/15) for every sigma/log-B constant; flag the r_d-anchoring conditionality and 'CLEARED-R4 vacuous' (prescription, not derived) cases as such.
evidence: 'Theorems EGDE1-EGDE12: per-candidate norm_num bounds (Verlinde >= 5sigma; Tsallis >= 6.2; Barrow >= 5.5 ...)' (line 276); 'Full causal-set lattice simulation -- beyond formalization scope; deep-research synthesis only.' (line 135); 'CST-M1, CST-M2 -- c_s^2 = 1 by prescription' CLEARED-R4 vacuous (line 45).

title: Phase 6m Track C survivors are CONDITIONAL on r_d-anchoring choice (fixed-r_d viable; floating-r_d disfavored to 4+sigma)
description: Track C's 'highest-survival track' headline (5+ R5 survivors: pure-Lambda M1/M2/M7/M9) is conditional: 'PARTIAL-VIABLE under fixed-r_d Planck DR3 prior (<=1.5sigma); NEEDS-MONITORING under floating-r_d (2.8-4.2sigma).' The fixed-vs-floating r_d split is named 'the key empirical decider by 2030.' DR3+Roman forecast: floating-r_d disfavor reaches >7sigma; fixed-r_d remains <3sigma throughout.
what_falls_short: The Track-C survival verdict flips depending on an unresolved anchoring choice (fixed vs floating r_d). The 'highest-survival' framing holds only under fixed-r_d; under floating-r_d the same candidates are disfavored at 2.8-4.2sigma now and >7sigma by 2030. JTGR1 encodes this as 'fixed-r_d disfavor < floating-r_d' -- a comparison, not an unconditional viability claim.
remediation: The conditionality is honestly documented and even Lean-encoded (JTGR1 as a comparison). Paper 45 must present Track-C survival as anchoring-conditional, with the fixed/floating r_d split as the empirical decider, not as an unconditional 'survives' headline. No falsifiable over-claim as long as prose preserves the conditional.
evidence: 'Pure-Lambda class (M1, M2/M7, M9) PARTIAL-VIABLE under fixed-r_d Planck DR3 prior (<=1.5sigma); NEEDS-MONITORING under floating-r_d (2.8-4.2sigma); fixed-vs-floating r_d split = key empirical decider by 2030.' (line 59).

title: Phase 6m JTGR/EGDE/CSDE NO-GO theorems risk within-band / norm_num-only triviality patterns
description: Many Phase 6m verdict theorems are norm_num inequalities asserting a published statistic exceeds a fixed threshold (e.g. JTGR3 'Hu-Sawicki best-fit b ~ 0.21 > 100x Solar-System chameleon bound'; EGDE12 'aggregate quantitative bound'; CSDE1-3 'f_DESI < 10^-5 Tier-4 conditioning bound encoded as norm_num inequality'). The roadmap's own strengthening checklist (lines 246-248) warns the NO-GO obstruction 'must be falsifiable (a counterexample ... would falsify the theorem)'.
what_falls_short: A theorem of the form 'literature_value > fixed_constant' proven by norm_num is structurally close to the P3/within-band triviality patterns the project's own discipline warns against -- the substantive content is the literature value (an input), not the inequality (mechanical). If the constants are hardcoded rather than derived from the pipeline, these are definitional-unfolding-as-physics rather than load-bearing physics.
remediation: Audit the Phase 6m verdict modules against the project's preemptive-strengthening checklist (P3/within-band/definitional-unfolding). Ensure each NO-GO theorem is genuinely falsifiable (ties an observable to a published bound such that a different observation would flip it), not a tautological 0.21 > 0.0021. The roadmap flags this requirement (lines 246-248) but does not confirm it was met for every theorem.
evidence: 'JTGR3: Hu-Sawicki best-fit b ~ 0.21 > 100x Solar-System chameleon bound -- no viable parameter window' (line 305); strengthening checklist '(P5 trivial-discharge): if branch NO-GO fires, the obstruction theorem must be falsifiable' (line 247).

title: Phase 6m paper-45 + two short-notes + ARCHITECTURE_SCOPE update remain undelivered (Lean closed, prose not)
description: Phase 6m is 'FULLY CLOSED at the Lean-formalization scope' but the publication deliverables are not: 'ARCHITECTURE_SCOPE.md update + paper-45 deferred (next session work)' (line 319); 'target Stage 13 closure ~16-24 weeks post-Wave 4 (heavy review-paper drafting)' (line 355); two parallel short-note publications (Barrow prescription-dependence; Sakharov Lambda_J vs Lambda_HK) remain at outline stage.
what_falls_short: The phase's stated paper deliverable (Paper 45, Phys.Rep./PRD) and two short-notes plus the ARCHITECTURE_SCOPE.md update are deferred to future drafting sessions. The Lean substrate exists but the publication-facing outputs (the actual scientific deliverable) are not produced.
remediation: Schedule Paper 45 drafting and the two short-notes via the bundle-lift / paper pipeline; the citation_registry batch-add proposal (62 entries) needs human-review merge before drafting. These are normal downstream paper-production tasks, not technical gaps.
evidence: 'ARCHITECTURE_SCOPE.md update + paper-45 deferred (next session work).' (line 319); 'Remaining work: ARCHITECTURE_SCOPE.md update ...; paper-45 (Phys. Rep. or PRD review); two parallel short-note publications ... outline ...' (line 381).

title: Phase 6m Track A/B candidates excluded on phenomenology, with three 'publishable structural caveats' that are non-uniqueness admissions
description: Phase 6m's structural (GD-obstruction) findings do NOT exclude Track A causal-set DE; exclusion is purely phenomenological (DESI+sigma8+ISW at >=5sigma). The three 'publishable structural caveats' are admissions of non-derivation/non-uniqueness: (i) GD-inapplicability (the obstruction does not even apply to causal-set DE -- a negative result); (ii) Barrow-bound 'prescription-dependence' (ZAS-2018 prescription lifts the bound ~3 orders of magnitude -- non-uniqueness); (iii) BDG sigma_Lambda first-principles propagation is 'unfinished' / ' epsilon derivation ... open R4+ question'; and 'c_s^2 = 1 by prescription, not derived.'
what_falls_short: The Phase 5y GD obstruction -- the project's structural workhorse -- is structurally INAPPLICABLE to the causal-set DE family, so Track A's NO-GO is phenomenological (data-driven, DR3-revisable) not structural. The 'structural caveats' headlined as publishable are non-uniqueness / unfinished-derivation admissions, and key inputs (c_s^2=1, Barrow prescription) are by-prescription not derived.
remediation: Honestly documented. Ensure Paper 45 frames Track A exclusion as phenomenological/DESI-driven (revisable at DR3) and the GD-inapplicability as a scope-boundary result, not a structural NO-GO. The BDG first-principles sigma_Lambda derivation is an explicitly open R4+ question -- present as open, and do not credit c_s^2=1 or the Barrow prescription as derived.
evidence: 'Phase 5y GD obstruction is structurally inapplicable to causal-set DE' (line 12); 'BDG first-principles sigma_Lambda propagation through Friedmann is unfinished; ... c_s^2 = 1 is by prescription, not derived.' (line 17); 'epsilon derivation from path-sum-internal consistency open R4+ question' (line 22).

title: Phase 6AO roadmap is self-described post-hoc as CLOSED but contains a large 'REMAINING' circuit-synthesis section and stale SUPERSEDED blocks
description: The roadmap header declares 'PHASE 6AO CLOSED (2026-06-10)' yet the body carries a substantial 'REMAINING Track-2 brick = the CIRCUIT SYNTHESIS only' section (lines 156-166) and an explicitly-flagged STALE/SUPERSEDED block (lines 622-647: 'Two statements below are now STALE and would UNDER-claim if read as current'). The increment log (inc 29-40) shows the circuit synthesis was subsequently completed, but the roadmap retains contradictory in-progress prose.
what_falls_short: Roadmap freshness/coherence: a CLOSED phase doc contains live-sounding REMAINING sections and self-flagged stale blocks, making it hard to read the true current state without tracing the full increment log. This is a documentation-hygiene weakness, not a technical gap -- the work appears complete per the inc-40 Track-2-COMPLETE marker.
remediation: Consolidate the Phase 6AO roadmap: the closure header should reflect that Track 2 (inc 1-40), Track 1 (a-d), and Track 3 (4/4 sites) are all complete; prune or clearly archive the superseded inc-1-6-era REMAINING/SUPERSEDED blocks so the doc states current state without contradiction. The W5-residual /goal (Track 1 ancilla-free tracked-Prop) is the only genuinely-open item.
evidence: 'PHASE 6AO CLOSED (2026-06-10)' (line 42) vs 'REMAINING Track-2 brick = the CIRCUIT SYNTHESIS only (multi-increment sub-program, NOT de-scoped)' (line 156) and 'SUPERSEDED (inc-1-6 era snapshot ...) Two statements below are now STALE' (line 622).

title: gaussianSaddleAsymptotic 'axiom retirement' is a defining-the-conclusion triviality, not the planned Laplace-method derivation
source: Phase6a_Roadmap.md Wave 7 (6a.7) lines 226-232, 341-421; verified live in BHEntropyMicroscopic.lean:191-247
description: Phase 6a Wave 7 was scoped as a substantive axiom-elimination wave: derive the SU(2)_k Kaul-Majumdar bounded-remainder from a real Laplace-method / Watson's-lemma assembly (Sub-task A Mathlib PR `MeasureTheory.Asymptotic.LaplaceMethod`, Sub-task B I0-I1 port, Sub-task C composition). remediation: Execute the originally-planned Wave 7 Sub-tasks A-C: a genuine Laplace-method bounded-remainder lemma (Mathlib PR or project-local), the Kaul-Majumdar I0-I1 port, and discharge `H_VerlindeKMLiteralSumDerivation` so `verlindeEntropy_SU2k` is a literal Verlinde sum rather than its own saddle limit. Until then, paper26 must label the -3/2 coefficient as conditional on the saddle-limit definitional choice.

remediation: Build the general density-matrix layer only on real demand (the explicit posture). If a bundle/customer claim needs trace-distance or diamond-norm distinguishability for the network substrate, that requires the held-out layer (physlib MState integration per the upstream-disposition memo).
evidence: Phase6AB line 21: 'general density-matrix/trace-distance/diamond-norm layer (pre-demand: do NOT build)'. Line 5 inherited: 'Bell-diagonal/Werner real-parameter representation (no density matrices/traceNorm/diamond-norm).'

title: Phase 6AB W4 SKR threshold and W3 general-Bell-diagonal results are parametric (threshold-free / parameter-gated), per the strengthening-discipline pattern
description: W4 (RepeaterChain) ships `endToEndQBER` range/monotonicity with the 'SKR threshold parametric' — the secret-key-rate threshold is left as a free parameter rather than instantiated to a concrete physical cutoff. W3 ships the general Klein-4 swap map with envelope/bridge lemmas. These are kept parametric to satisfy the project's preemptive-strengthening discipline (avoid within-±2σ-band tautologies), but the headline repeater results are bounds/ranges rather than concrete numerical predictions.
what_falls_short: The repeater-chain results prove fidelity/QBER ranges and monotonicity but not a concrete crossover threshold (the SKR cutoff is a free parameter). This is appropriate honest framing but means the substrate provides structure (envelopes, ranges) rather than device-specific predictions — consistent with the 'model-independent FIDELITY target' posture.
remediation: Instantiate the SKR threshold at a concrete physical cutoff (e.g., the standard 11% QBER for BB84) as an additional theorem if a device-specific claim is needed, keeping the parametric form as the general result.
evidence: Phase6AB line 13: 'endToEndQBER range/monotonicity (SKR threshold parametric)'. Line 5: 'model-independent FIDELITY target.'

title: Phase 6AD Rate.lean handshake-inclusive link times remain Tier-3 unbounded — only the model-independent latency floor is formalized
description: Wave 3.2 shipped the physics-only link-rate `τ = L/(c·p_link)` as a Tier-1 model-independent result (latency × expected geometric attempts, with the geometric mean DERIVED not asserted). But the roadmap notes 'handshake-inclusive times remain Tier-3 (unbounded)' — the realistic link time including protocol handshake overhead is not bounded.
what_falls_short: The link-rate result is a clean physics floor but excludes handshake/protocol overhead, which is left 'Tier-3 unbounded'. The formalized rate is a lower bound on realistic link establishment time, not the realistic time itself — a documented scope boundary on the network-rate modeling.
remediation: If handshake-inclusive timing becomes load-bearing for a claim, model the protocol-overhead term and bound it (Tier-3 → Tier-2). Otherwise keep the Tier-1 floor as the honest model-independent result.
evidence: Phase6AD line 19: 'Tier-1 model-independent; handshake-inclusive times remain Tier-3 (unbounded).'
