what_falls_short: The comparison headline partly rests on a defined-to-be-zero baseline (defining-the-conclusion pattern): W beats GHZ partly because GHZ's advantage is set to 0 by modeling fiat. Honestly labeled 'cited modeling input', but the strict-beat result inherits that the gap is partly definitional.
remediation: If a derivation of ghz3RandomizationAdvantage=0 from the GHZ protocol exists, ship it; otherwise ensure paper/D6 §6 flags the zero as a cited modeling input so the strict-beat is not over-read as fully derived.
evidence: 'ghz3RandomizationAdvantage (0, cited modeling input) ... w3_beats_ghz_randomization_advantage (Thm 3.5: W₃ strictly beats GHZ₃).'

title: Phase 6AC BB84 'crossover not hardcoded' — e*≈0.11 only stated as implicit IVT root, decimal never asserted (honest framing, but no closed value)
description: The BB84 secret-key crossover e* is established only as the existence of a root via IVT (bb84_crossover_exists, intermediate_value_Icc') with uniqueness on [0,1/2]; 'The decimal ≈0.11 is never asserted — it is the implicit root of h₂(e)=1/2.' This is the correct rigorous treatment (the root is transcendental), so it is a strength, but the practical threshold value is intentionally not pinned.
what_falls_short: Minor: the operationally-quoted ~11% QBER threshold has no kernel-verified numeric bound (e.g. a norm_num interval enclosure like 0.11 < e* < 0.12); only existence/uniqueness is proven. Not a defect of correctness — flagged for completeness as a place where a tighter falsifiable numeric could be added.
remediation: Optionally add a norm_num-backed enclosure of e* (e.g. 0.110 < e* < 0.111) via sign-change evaluation of bb84KeyRate at rational endpoints, giving the headline a falsifiable numeric without hardcoding.
evidence: 'The decimal ≈0.11 is never asserted — it is the implicit root of h₂(e)=1/2.'

title: Phase 7a: I2 bundle may ship 'software-only' with the Mathlib upstream descoped to a deferred JOSS update if upstream-cycle delays
description: Bundle I2 depends on Phase 5o Wave 5 Mathlib upstream coordination. The roadmap pre-authorizes a descope: 'If Mathlib-acceptance is delayed beyond Phase 7a's drafting timeline, ship I2 as software-only (note in §5); the Mathlib upstream becomes a separate JOSS update later.' Trigger condition 6 likewise allows Phase 7a to close with I2 software-only.
what_falls_short: A planned deliverable (lean-tensor-categories Mathlib upstream contribution) can be dropped from the bundle and deferred indefinitely to a future JOSS update, contingent on an external Mathlib review cycle the project does not control. The bundle ships with a deferred-dependency note rather than the full content.
remediation: Track the Phase 5o Wave 5 Mathlib PR status; if accepted, fold the upstream confirmation into I2; if not, ensure the software-only I2 carries the deferred-dependency note and the JOSS-update plan is scheduled, not dropped.
evidence: 'If Mathlib-acceptance is delayed beyond Phase 7a's drafting timeline, ship I2 as software-only (note in §5); the Mathlib upstream becomes a separate JOSS update later. Annotated in bundle_metadata.json.target_journal ... as a deferred-dependency note.'

title: Phase 7a: LATE_PHASE6_ABSORPTION_PROTOCOL D.3 (revision-required) explicitly NOT automatable — manual author judgment, untested in production
source: Phase7a_Roadmap.md lines 262, 282, 518, 566 (D.3 branch + Risk: protocol untested)
description: The robustness protocol's D.3 branch (Phase 6X output overturns/refines a prior bundle verdict) 'requires manual author judgment. No automation can determine whether new content revises prior content.' The protocol explicitly 'does NOT claim full automation'. The whole protocol's first live invocation will be its first test — 'design risk with no test cycle' — only a synthetic/conceptual rehearsal was performed (7a.4.2).
what_falls_short: The load-bearing robustness machinery for absorbing late Phase 6 outputs has a manual-judgment escape hatch (D.3) and was never exercised on a real late-absorption event in Phase 7a; correctness under a real revision-class absorption is unverified.
remediation: When the first real late-absorption event occurs (a Phase 6X result that revises a drafted bundle), exercise D.3 end-to-end and capture the outcome in LATE_PHASE6_ABSORPTION_PROTOCOL.md v2 per the documented mitigation.
evidence: 'D.3 requires manual author judgment. No automation can determine whether new content revises prior content.'; 'the protocol does NOT claim full automation; it claims structured handling with documented branch points.'; Risk: 'design risk with no test cycle.'

title: Phase 6AJ Wave 6AJ.4 'PLANNED' section remains in roadmap though OUTCOME 3 already discharged it — no live weakness, but earlier reversible-only DP was a hedged restricted ship
description: Phase 6AJ is fully closed (OUTCOME 3: unconditional fidelity DP, kernel-pure, no axiom). However the document records an earlier hedged trajectory: the first ship was fidelity DP for only the REVERSIBLE (unitary-channel) subclass as an equality, with the general/mixed-unitary monotonicity initially mapped as a route, and the roadmap's invariants pre-authorized 'a strictly-weaker-but-honest deliverable ... must be labeled as the restricted result.' The Wave 6AJ.4 'PLANNED' section still sits in the doc despite being completed.
what_falls_short: No current weakness — the unconditional result is proven kernel-pure. Flagged only because the roadmap preserves a now-superseded restricted/hedged intermediate (reversible-only DP) and a stale 'PLANNED' header that could mislead a reader into thinking unconditional DP is still open. The escalation from restricted-subclass to fully unconditional was achieved.
remediation: Doc hygiene: mark Wave 6AJ.4 'PLANNED' as SUPERSEDED-by-OUTCOME-3 and the reversible-only ship as explicitly historical, so no reader mistakes the closed unconditional result for an open restricted one. No mathematical work needed.
evidence: '## Wave 6AJ.4 — UNCONDITIONAL fidelity DP (PLANNED)' header retained; 'If the full DP inequality is unreachable, a strictly-weaker-but-honest deliverable (e.g. fidelity DP for the restricted unital / mixed-unitary case ...) is acceptable and must be labeled as the restricted result, not the general one.'; OUTCOME 3 line 165 shows it was fully discharged.

title: Phase 6q upstream trigger (Phase 6o Wave 1c NO-GO writeup) was unlocatable for ~11 days; whole phase predicated on a reconstructed obstruction set
description: Phase 6q's entire premise is the 'positive-result response' to Phase 6o Wave 1c's NO-GO ('dissipative SK-EFT bootstrap can't produce uniqueness with current axioms'). The substrate scout 'could not locate this file' for ~11 days; the Wave 1a.1 DR was parameterized to RECONSTRUCT the three NO-GO obstructions 'from first principles ... rather than presuming the writeup exists.' It was eventually found at a workspace-root (not project-root) path.
what_falls_short: The foundational trigger result was missing/mislocated at phase dispatch, so the phase initially proceeded on a reconstructed rather than verified obstruction set. Resolved once the 211-line writeup was located, but it surfaces a provenance fragility — a headline NO-GO that the phase responds to was not where the roadmap said it was.
remediation: Already resolved (writeup located 2026-05-23, Wave 1a.1 DR reconstruction cross-checked). Doc hygiene: fix the roadmap §A path reference to the workspace-root location so future scouts find it.

title: Phase 6q two deep-research dispatches are external async dependencies that calibrate the axiom-replacement decision (1a.1) and specialization (2a.1)
description: Phase 6q's substantive entry-point — the axiom-replacement decision (Wave 1a.1) and the SK-EFT-Hawking specialization (Wave 2a.1) — were each gated on a deep-research dispatch. Both returned same-day (2026-05-12) and were integrated, but the roadmap notes Wave 2a.1 'intended to process AFTER Wave 1a.1 return calibrates axiom-set context', i.e. the phase's core decisions were DR-calibrated rather than internally derived.
what_falls_short: The axiom-replacement decision (which the roadmap calls 'the substantive entry-point of the phase; should not be pre-judged') was steered by an external DR return rather than a project-internal derivation. Both DRs returned and were integrated, so not currently blocking, but the headline pivot (CHHK is purely analytic, sidesteps all 3 obstructions) originates from DR, not from the project's own analysis.
remediation: No action — both DRs returned and were integrated; flagged only to note the phase's foundational pivot rests on DR input (advisory per project policy), which is acceptable as long as the Lean substrate independently verifies the CHHK-bypasses-obstructions claim (which Wave 1b/1c did).
evidence: 'Wave 1a.1 DR ... Key DR pivot: CHHK arXiv:2509.18255 is purely analytic ... it sidesteps all three Phase 6o Wave 1c NO-GO obstructions by design.'; 'intended to process AFTER Wave 1a.1 return calibrates axiom-set context.'

title: HEADLINE-backing: Fibonacci-anyon braid-representation + universality theorems rest on native_decide over the degree-8 number field Q(ζ₅,√φ) — genuinely un-decide-able under Invariant #10
source: FibonacciUniversality.lean, FibonacciSextetTrueRep.lean, FibonacciQuintetTrueRep.lean, FibonacciBraiding.lean, FPDimension.lean, QCyc5Ext.lean
description: The flagship anyonic-computation headlines — 'Fibonacci braiding is universal for SU(2)/SU(2)×SU(2)' and 'first self-contained universality verification for anyonic gates' — are discharged by native_decide. FibonacciUniversality.lean's universality_linear_independence (the Lie-algebra spanning conditions: comm_off_diag_nonzero, sigma traceless parts ≠ 0, s2_01 ≠ 0) is native_decide over QCyc5Ext. FibonacciSextetTrueRep.lean verifies the 6-strand braid-group relations (Yang-Baxter sigma_n*sigma_{n+1}*sigma_n = ..., far-commutativity, inverse-identities sigma_n*sigma_n_inv = 1) as 169-entry 13×13 matrix equalities over Q(ζ₅,√φ) — these CERTIFY the literal matrices form a genuine B₆ representation. FPDimension.lean derives FPdim(τ)=φ via eigenvector equations over QSqrt5 ('FIRST Frobenius-Perron dimension derivation in any proof assistant').
what_falls_short: QCyc5Ext/QSqrt5 elements are DecidableEq (pairs of rational-coefficient tuples), so kernel `decide` is type-theoretically possible — but evaluating rational arithmetic over a degree-8 number field (and 169-entry matrix products) blows the kernel heartbeat budget (Invariant #10). native_decide compiles it; the kernel re-runs compiled code, adding a Lean.ofReduceBool/trustCompiler trust axiom. The published universality result therefore carries a compiler-trust dependency, not a fully kernel-checked proof.
remediation: These are the legitimate, project-tolerated core. To eliminate: replace native_decide with the 6AO Track-3 structural pattern — prove the braid relations symbolically via the number-field ring structure (norm-multiplicativity, explicit basis arithmetic lemmas) rather than coordinate enumeration, the way RossSelinger eliminated its KMM cores. Feasible but research-grade (constructing kernel-tractable symbolic identities for Q(ζ₅,√φ) matrix algebra); do NOT down-rate to easy. Acceptable to KEEP native_decide here with the existing justification comments, since the field arithmetic is the canonical un-decide-able-under-#10 case.
evidence: FibonacciUniversality.lean:142 universality_linear_independence ends '..., by native_decide'; header 'kernel-checked modulo native_decide'. FibonacciSextetTrueRep.lean:60 comment 'discharge via native_decide on the 169-entry equality'; lines 395-440 are the Yang-Baxter/far-comm/inverse relations. QCyc5Ext.lean defines K=Q(ζ₅)[w]/(w²+ζ²+ζ³), degree-8 non-abelian (Kronecker-Weber obstruction).

title: HEADLINE-backing: WRT 3-manifold invariants and Ising/SU(2)_k MTC braid data via native_decide over QCyc16/QCyc40 — load-bearing 'first verified quantum invariant' claims
source: WRTComputation.lean, IsingBraiding.lean, IsingGates.lean, SU2kMTC.lean, SU2kSMatrix.lean, SU3k2SMatrix.lean, SU3k2FSymbols.lean, TQFTPartition.lean, KacWaltonFusion.lean, FibonacciMTC.lean, MugerCenter.lean, TgateFibBraid.lean, GateCompilation.lean, FKLW/RouabahSplitBraid.lean
description: WRTComputation.lean ('FIRST verified quantum 3-manifold invariant values in any proof assistant') computes Z(S²×S¹), D², p₊ for Ising and Fibonacci MTCs by native_decide on QCyc16/QCyc5 arithmetic (ising_globalDimSq, fib_globalDimSq, fib_phi_sq). IsingBraiding.lean verifies R-matrix relations (R1_sq, R1_cubed, quantum_trace_value) over QCyc16. GateCompilation.lean + FKLW/RouabahSplitBraid.lean discharge the BHSZ Hadamard/CNOT/T-gate braid-word approximation-error (Frobenius distance) over Q(ζ₄₀) — the file explicitly states native_decide is REQUIRED and norm_num-on-ℝ was rejected, and a 30-deep word is split into a 15-deep prefix + foldl precisely because native_decide tractability is the binding constraint. KacWaltonFusion.lean verifies concrete fusion multiplicities N_{λμ}^ν for SU(4)_1/G₂_1/SO(5)_1 (58 uses).
what_falls_short: Same as Fibonacci: number-field/matrix equalities that are DecidableEq but exceed kernel heartbeats; carry compiler-trust axioms. GateCompilation/RouabahSplitBraid additionally document an EXPLICIT engineering tradeoff (15-deep tractable, 30-deep intractable) showing the result sits at the native_decide tractability frontier.
remediation: KEEP with justification for the gate-compilation Frobenius-distance and S-matrix/F-symbol verifications (genuinely un-decide-able under #10). For the leaf MTC scalar identities (e.g. fib_phi_sq : phi*phi = 1+phi over QCyc5), a structural ring-lemma proof is achievable medium-effort and would remove the trust axiom from the headline closure. Prioritize converting the small per-scalar identities that the big WRT/universality theorems CONSUME, so the headline's transitive native_decide footprint shrinks even if the bulk leaf checks stay.
evidence: WRTComputation.lean:36 ising_globalDimSq native_decide; :62 fib_phi_sq native_decide. GateCompilation.lean:20-28 'REJECT ε~10⁻⁹ (native_decide intractable)... native_decide, NOT norm_num on ℝ... Frobenius-distance discharge via native_decide on Q(ζ₄₀)'. RouabahSplitBraid.lean:23-24 '15-deep native_decide on QCyc40Ext matrix... tractable'.

title: ELIMINABLE (one refactor, many call sites): trivial Cartan-matrix-entry lookups discharged by native_decide should be `decide`/`rfl` — gratuitous trust axioms on integer constants
source: QuantumGroupInstantiation.lean (19), QuantumGroupGeneric.lean (12), QuantumGroupMeta.lean (11), E8Lattice.lean (11), KacWaltonFusion.lean (subset), A1Resolution.lean (17)
description: A large fraction of the quantum-group + E8Lattice native_decides prove trivial finite-integer facts: `cartanA1 0 0 = 2`, `cartanA2 0 1 = -1`, `CartanMatrix.E₈.det = 1`, `hDual_A1 : dataA1.hDual = 1+1`, `e8_diagonal_all_two`. These are tiny `Fin n → ℤ` / small-matrix evaluations well within kernel `decide`/`Matrix.det` + `decide` budget. native_decide here is gratuitous — it attaches a Lean.ofReduceBool compiler-trust axiom to a fact `decide` (kernel) discharges instantly.
what_falls_short: These contribute to the transitive native_decide footprint of downstream quantum-group equivalence theorems (QuantumGroup k cartanA1 ≃ Uqsl2 k) and the E8 unimodularity facts WITHOUT any genuine compute need — pure trust-surface inflation.
remediation: Mechanically replace `by native_decide` with `by decide` (or `rfl`/`by norm_num`) for all sub-2x2/sub-8x8 integer Cartan-entry and determinant lookups; rebuild to confirm kernel tractability. This is bulk find-replace + build, exactly the 'just more Lean work' that should be rated easy. Removes dozens of trust axioms from quantum-group and E8Lattice closures.
evidence: QuantumGroupInstantiation.lean:67 `rw [show cartanA1 0 0 = 2 from by native_decide]`; :441-456 four cartanA2 entry lookups via native_decide. E8Lattice.lean:42 e8_det_one, :52 e8_diagonal_all_two, :72 e8_minor_1 all native_decide; file header line 14 'All proofs by native_decide or decide'.

title: ELIMINABLE: SFDM/dark-matter detection-forecast rational-threshold inequalities use native_decide where `norm_num` (kernel-pure) suffices
description: SFDMMergerForecast.lean's stacked-SNR detection thresholds — e.g. bullet_roman_3sigma_at_N_18 : `9 ≤ snr_bullet_roman^2 * 18` with snr_bullet_roman := 103/100 — are pure ℚ inequalities discharged by `unfold; native_decide`. These are exactly the shape `norm_num` closes kernel-purely. FractonDarkMatter.lean's no-go-theorem exemption theorems (pwave_fully_exempt : `cases t <;> native_decide`, fracton_ww_bypass : `cases p <;> native_decide`) case-split a 4-constructor inductive then evaluate a Bool function — small finite, `decide`-able after the case split (6AO Track-3 pattern).
what_falls_short: These back Tier-4 experimental/forecast paper claims (self-interacting dark-matter detectability via stacked GW/merger SNR; fracton-DM phase no-go exemptions). They carry compiler-trust axioms for arithmetic/finite-case facts that have direct kernel-pure tactics — unnecessary trust surface on a publication-facing forecast.
remediation: Replace `unfold X; native_decide` with `unfold X; norm_num` for the ℚ threshold inequalities (verify each closes; rational arithmetic is norm_num's home turf). Replace `cases t <;> native_decide` with `cases t <;> decide` (or `<;> rfl`) for the small-inductive Bool no-go checks. Both are mechanical and kernel-purify a publication-facing forecast.
evidence: SFDMMergerForecast.lean:415-428 three SNR threshold theorems `unfold snr_*; native_decide`; def snr_bullet_roman := 103/100 (line 402). FractonDarkMatter.lean:200 pwave_fully_exempt `cases t <;> native_decide`; :304 fracton_ww_bypass `cases p <;> native_decide`.

title: SUMMARY (leaf enumeration, KEEP): bulk number-field substrate native_decides (QCyc*/QSqrt*/Mat13/PolyQuot, ~124) are genuine finite ring-arithmetic checks — the canonical project-tolerated case
source: QCyc5.lean (19), QCyc5Ext.lean, QCyc40.lean (17), QCyc40Ext.lean (12), QCyc15.lean, QCyc16.lean, QCyc3.lean, QSqrt5.lean, QSqrt3.lean, QSqrt2.lean, Mat13K5Ext.lean, PolyQuotOver.lean, PolyQuotQ.lean
description: Folding the leaf cases: the number-field substrate modules verify ring-axiom-style identities and specific products/sums over cyclotomic and quadratic fields (e.g. phi*phi = 1+phi, w² = -ζ²-ζ³, matrix-literal multiplications in Mat13K5Ext). These are DecidableEq finite computations that exceed kernel heartbeats due to rational-tower arithmetic. They are the substrate the headline anyon/MTC theorems consume.
what_falls_short: Each is individually un-decide-able under Invariant #10 (heartbeat blowup), so each carries a compiler-trust axiom. Collectively they are the largest single contributor to the library's native_decide trust surface, but no single one is a headline by itself.
remediation: KEEP by default — this is the legitimate, lint-justified, project-tolerated native_decide. Highest-value targeted elimination: the handful of substrate identities directly consumed by headline closures (Finding 2/3) via structural ring lemmas; the rest can stay with their justification comments. Do not attempt a blanket conversion — the bulk genuinely needs compiled evaluation.
evidence: QCyc5Ext.lean multiplication rule (a₁a₂+φb₁b₂)+(a₁b₂+a₂b₁)w; FPDimension.lean:52,56 eigenvector products over QSqrt5 via native_decide; Mat13K5Ext literal 13×13 matrix arithmetic over Q(ζ₅,√φ).

title: Tracked-hypothesis ledger drift: ~11+ undischarged H_* Props consumed as conditional hypotheses but absent from PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md
source: docs/PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md (claims 'honest count: 7', 'last synced 2026-05-31') vs lean/SKEFTHawking/ tree (49 Prop-valued H_* defs)
description: The project adopted an `H_*` naming convention for tracked-hypothesis Props. The central ledger (PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md) documents only 2 of them in detail (H_VestigialModeIsGraviton, H_DESICompatibility) plus 5 Phase-6r/SK-era entries (KT, DMNO, IsSKEFTHawkingSymTFTBoundary, and the SolovayKitaev contract is mentioned only in a summary table). But the Lean tree contains 49 Prop-valued `H_*` defs, of which at least 11 are genuinely consumed as `(h : H_X)` undischarged hypotheses with NO anchor/discharge theorem, making their downstream theorems CONDITIONAL: H_SubstrateNearSMGFixedPoint (3 consumers), H_MR_FromSMGGap (3), H_KLRS_SM_Crossover (2), H_HSCovariantBosonisation (2), H_BilocalPointlikeLimit (2), H_MR_FromADWSubstrate_BCS_LNV (2), H_VestigialRelicCarriesZ16Charge (2), H_PMNSAnglesFromExactSubstrate/_eps (1+), H_DecouplingBoundDim6 (1), H_MixedChannelZ16Cancels (1), H_ScalarChannelIsTetradBifurcationOutput (1). Many are explicitly self-labeled 'Tracked hypothesis' / 'tracked Prop' in their own docstrings (32 docstrings contain the phrase 'tracked hypothesis'), with the exact rationale the central ledger uses ('not derived in our framework', 'out of scope', 'OPEN-AT-LITERATURE-FRONTIER'). They are load-bearing: they surface in published-bundle paper drafts (paper21_majorana_rung, paper17_dark_sector, paper20_scalar_rung, paper33_ewbg_chirality_wall, bundle I1).
what_falls_short: The ledger's own §Scope says it 'covers tracked-hypothesis Props at the project-scope boundary — claims that the SK_EFT_Hawking program does NOT derive from first principles within Phases 1-6 but consumes as external physical input', and §Maintenance requires it to be 'the source of truth for the project's load-bearing tracked-Prop surface area' updated 'whenever a new tracked Prop is added'. The headline 'honest count: 7' is therefore wrong by a factor of ~2-3x: the true count of genuinely-consumed-undischarged project-scope tracked-Props is ~18+ (7 documented + 11 undocumented). The undocumented ones make bundle headlines (Majorana-rung Type-I seesaw, EWBG SM-crossover, dark-sector Z16 stability, PMNS mu-tau symmetry, BHL Higgs-mass) conditional, but the bundles cannot hedge on dependencies the central ledger never recorded.
remediation: Run a sweep enumerating every Prop-valued `H_*` def (grep `def H_[A-Z]...: Prop`), classify each as (a) anchored/discharged (has a non-negated theorem concluding it), (b) genuinely-consumed-undischarged tracked-hypothesis, or (c) unused/local-marker. Add a ledger entry for each (b) with status (KEEP_AS_TRACKED vs DISCHARGE_FUTURE_PHASE), physics-status, consumer list, and 3-criterion-bar check, then correct the 'honest count: 7' headline and 'last synced' date. Pure documentation work, no Lean changes — a few hours.

title: SolovayKitaevQuantitativeContract: full-regime HEADLINE theorem is conditional on an admittedly 'undischargeable' contract; only the strict (ε≤ε₀) regime is unconditional
description: `SolovayKitaevQuantitativeContract : Prop` (the dual error+length bound for the Fibonacci-anyon SK compiler over all `U∈SU(2)`, `ε∈(0,1)`) is taken as a hypothesis `(h_contract : ...)` by the headline theorem `solovayKitaev_dawson_nielsen_quantitative_fibonacci` (lines 102-112). The file itself states (line 144) the contract 'is undischargeable in its full ε∈(0,1) form due to the placeholder skApprox recursion and the length bound's degeneration as log(1/ε)→0+ near ε=1.' A separate UNCONDITIONAL headline `solovayKitaev_dawson_nielsen_quantitative_fibonacci_strict` (lines 185-193) exists but is restricted to the strict regime `ε∈(0,ε₀]`. So the full-regime quantitative SK headline remains conditional on a tracked-Prop the project does not expect to discharge in full.
what_falls_short: The contract is referenced in PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md only as a single table row in §6 ('SolovayKitaevQuantitativeContract (active discharge) | DISCHARGE_IN_PROGRESS | Phase 6t Path A Option C tight-ε SHIPPED 2026-05-23'), with NO detailed §-entry in the ledger body and NOT counted in the 'honest count: 7'. The table's 'SHIPPED' phrasing understates that the FULL-regime contract is self-described as undischargeable; only a strict-ε-regime weakening was shipped unconditionally. This is exactly the Ross-Selinger / KMM prime-density literature-wall territory flagged in the ground truth (full ∀U-unconditional single-qubit/anyon synthesis is a named open input).
remediation: Promote the SolovayKitaevQuantitativeContract row to a full ledger §-entry that (a) states the full-regime form is undischargeable (placeholder skApprox + log degeneration near ε=1) and is the honest project-scope boundary, (b) documents that the unconditional deliverable is the strict-regime headline `..._strict` for ε∈(0,ε₀], (c) cross-references the Ross-Selinger prime-density literature wall. The strict-regime weakening is the correct/honest framing; the full-regime gap is genuinely literature-bounded, not effort-bounded.

title: H_DESICompatibility DISCHARGE_FUTURE_PHASE scheduled for Phase 6b.2 which is 'not currently active' — a genuine deferred objective
description: `H_DESICompatibility` is classified DISCHARGE_FUTURE_PHASE: the ledger states the DESI DR2 (w_0,w_a) compatibility claim 'is expected to follow derivatively from the project's existing substrate (ADW dynamics) once the perturbation-theory machinery is shipped', with LoE '~50 person-hours', 'slotted for Phase 6b.2 (cosmological perturbation theory) which is not currently active.' The Prop exists and matches the doc exactly (FLRWDynamics.lean:272-276), has 3 falsifiers but NO anchor discharge for any actual ADW predictor. It is genuinely undischarged interim framing.
what_falls_short: The ledger's own classification is DISCHARGE (not KEEP) — meaning the project regards this as derivable-in-principle work it owes, not a permanent scope boundary. Per the stated ground-truth policy ('No de-scope from effort magnitude' + dev rate ~10-15k LOC/day making a 50-person-hour item days here), Phase 6b.2 being 'not currently active' is an effort-magnitude deferral of in-scope work, not a justified literature wall. The ~50hr LoE estimate is in the project's words and likely an overestimate given the Lean-pipeline track record cited in CLAUDE.md ('multi-month estimates have repeatedly resolved in hours-days').
remediation: Either (a) execute Phase 6b.2 (couple ADW FLRW perturbations → growth observable → CPL (w_0,w_a) extraction → discharge H_DESICompatibility against DESI DR2 with an actual ADW predictor), or (b) if the multi-scalar-ADW-to-CPL chain hits a genuine wall, reclassify to KEEP with the wall documented. Currently it is honestly framed as interim, so impact is low — the only weakness is the indefinite 'not currently active' parking of DISCHARGE-classified work.

Estimated ~50 person-hours ... slotted for Phase 6b.2 (cosmological perturbation theory) which is not currently active.' title: Paper7 claims the general-G gauge-emergence equivalence Z(Vec_G) ≅ Rep(D(G)) as formally verified, but its Lean is a cluster of `True := trivial` stubs
description: The general-G Drinfeld-center / gauge-emergence equivalence Z(Vec_G) ≅ Rep(D(G)) is stated in Lean only as type-`True` placeholder theorems: gauge_emergence_statement, half_braiding_gives_action, equivalence_preserves_tensor, equivalence_preserves_braiding, center_universal_property, chirality_independent_of_G — each `:= trivial` with a docstring naming the real result (Müger's theorem, the Drinfeld R-matrix correspondence, the half-braiding→action core). gauge_emergence_statement even self-describes as 'the FIRST formal statement ... in any proof assistant'. What is ACTUALLY proven is only the concrete Z/2 case (CenterEquivalenceZ2, 4 toric-code anyons ↔ 4 D(Z/2)-modules, exhaustive case analysis) and S3 (s3_equivalence_verified), plus Hopf-algebra structure on D(G) (coproduct/counit/involutive-antipode). paper7 line 307-309 textually asserts 'the gauge emergence theorem Z(Vec_G) ≅ Rep(D(G))' as part of '4049 theorems ... provide end-to-end formal verification from categorical Layer 1 through gauge Layer 2', WITHOUT disclosing that the general-G equivalence is a True stub.
what_falls_short: A reader of paper7 would conclude the general-G categorical equivalence is machine-checked. It is not — only abelian Z/2 and the single non-abelian S3 instance are verified; the universal statement, tensor-preservation, braiding-preservation and Müger universal property are all `True := trivial`. This is the exact substance failure (naming a theorem after a real result, proving it True) that the project's own prior audit (papers/AutomatedReviews/2026-04-26-1955-lean-substance-audit, items 11/14) flagged. paper9 (line 309) and paper11 (line 62) state the same equivalence more carefully ('algebraic foundation for', 'in companion'), so the overclaim is specifically paper7's unhedged 'gauge emergence theorem' phrasing inside the 4049-theorem verification-coverage sentence.
remediation: Either (a) hedge paper7 line 307-309 to match paper9's careful framing — state the equivalence is verified concretely for Z/2 and S3, with the general-G statement as a documented program target — OR (b) discharge the stubs. Discharging is genuine research-grade category theory (Mathlib lacks the Müger/ENO modular-category infrastructure: braided-functor preservation, half-braiding→D(G)-action), so it is 'hard', not 'just more Lean work'. Minimum acceptable fix: rename gauge_emergence_statement/equivalence_preserves_* with a `_TODO` suffix (as was done for the FusionCategory/CenterFunctor cluster) so the names can't be cited as proved, and add the paper7 disclosure.
evidence: GaugeEmergence.lean:90 `theorem gauge_emergence_statement : True := trivial  -- full proof requires VecG monoidal + D(G) module cat`; DrinfeldEquivalence.lean:108 `theorem equivalence_preserves_braiding : True := trivial`; :178 `theorem center_universal_property : True := trivial  -- Müger's theorem`; paper7:307 'the gauge emergence theorem $Z(\mathrm{Vec}_G) \cong \mathrm{Rep}(D(G))$. Together, these 4049 theorems ... provide end-to-end formal verification'. Concrete proven case: DrinfeldEquivalence.lean:130 s3_equivalence_verified; CenterEquivalenceZ2.lean.

title: Named-result `True := trivial` content stubs across category-theory / quantum-group / algebra modules (ocneanu_rigidity, fusion_to_tqft, equivalence_is_*, dolan_grady_from_chevalley, davies_roan, a1_is_sub_hopf, oq_coideal, small_uq_*)
source: lean/SKEFTHawking/FusionCategory.lean:153,159; CenterFunctor.lean:119,124; CoidealEmbedding.lean:185; OnsagerAlgebra.lean:352; SteenrodA1.lean:195; Uqsl2Affine.lean:244; RestrictedUq.lean:187,202
description: A family of type-`True` theorems each named after a substantive unproven result, body `:= trivial`: ocneanu_rigidity_TODO, fusion_to_tqft_TODO (Reshetikhin-Turaev TQFT functor), equivalence_is_monoidal_TODO, equivalence_is_braided_TODO, dolan_grady_from_chevalley (the cubic Dolan-Grady relation from q-Serre — the ACTUAL Dolan-Grady content; what IS proven in OnsagerAlgebra is only DG_COEFF=16 arithmetic identities), davies_roan_classification (Onsager rep classification), a1_is_sub_hopf_algebra (Δ(A(1))⊆A(1)⊗A(1) Steenrod sub-Hopf), oq_coideal_property_B0_statement (Delta(B_i) coideal), small_uq_dim_statement / small_uq_to_su2k_connection (restricted quantum-group dimension dim=ell^3 and SU(2)_k fusion bridge). The four with `_TODO` suffix were renamed from `_placeholder` after the 2026-04-26 audit so the name cannot be mistaken for proved; the others (dolan_grady_from_chevalley, davies_roan_classification, a1_is_sub_hopf_algebra, oq_*, small_uq_*) still carry result-suggestive names.
what_falls_short: Each is a named mathematical result standing in for content not formalized. None is currently a paper headline (dolan_grady appears in paper8 only as the DG-COEFF arithmetic, not the cubic relation; the others have no paper citation), so they are supporting/leaf weaknesses rather than overclaims — but they inflate the theorem count and the un-suffixed ones (dolan_grady_from_chevalley, davies_roan_classification, a1_is_sub_hopf_algebra, oq_coideal_property_B0_statement, small_uq_dim_statement, small_uq_to_su2k_connection) risk a future paper citing them as proved.
remediation: Apply the `_TODO` rename discipline uniformly to the six un-suffixed members (dolan_grady_from_chevalley → dolan_grady_from_chevalley_TODO, etc.) so none can be cited as proved. Then discharge opportunistically: dolan_grady_from_chevalley is a purely algebraic computation in the quotient ring (CoidealEmbedding docstring + Phase-5b/q-Onsager DR gives the explicit derivation) → 'easy/medium' Lean work; a1_is_sub_hopf_algebra is a finite combinatorial check on the Sq^n coproduct → 'easy'; davies_roan_classification and the small_uq dimension/fusion bridges need rep-category infrastructure → 'medium'. None is a literature wall.
