title: Vacuous-existential placeholders for cobordism results (z16_classification, dai_freed_spin_z4) — `∃ φ:ZMod 16 ≃ ZMod 16, Bijective φ`, witnessed by Equiv.refl
description: z16_classification and dai_freed_spin_z4 are NOT type-True (so they escape the 26-count) but are P5 vacuous existentials: each states `∃ (φ : ZMod 16 ≃ ZMod 16), Function.Bijective φ` and is discharged by `⟨Equiv.refl _, (Equiv.refl _).bijective⟩`. The statement is trivially true for any type (every Equiv is bijective) and carries none of the intended content — the Pin⁺ bordism Ω₄^{Pin⁺} ≅ ℤ₁₆ (Giambalvo 1973 / Kirby-Taylor 1990) and the Dai-Freed Ω₅^{Spin^Z4} ≅ ℤ₁₆ (García-Etxebarria–Montero 2019). The Z16Classification source comment is candid: 'this placeholder was tautological'.
what_falls_short: The intended deep cobordism classification is not formalized; only a content-free existential is. UNLIKE the gauge-emergence case, the papers DISCLOSE this honestly: paper8 line 345/431-433 states the Pin⁺ bordism is 'axiomatized' and that Ω₅^{Spin^Z4} 'was converted to a trivially-true placeholder theorem (dai_freed_spin_z4) pending the addition of cobordism theory to Mathlib; the cobordism computation itself is an external input, as is standard in the ℤ₁₆ literature.' D5 carries a `\placeholdertheorems` macro that explicitly 'separates load-bearing from documentation-marker placeholder theorems.' So these are disclosed external inputs, not masqueraded — the weakness is the vacuous Lean encoding, not a paper overclaim.
remediation: No action needed on the paper side (disclosure is correct and conservative). For the Lean: the honest encoding would be a tracked-Prop hypothesis (a named opaque `Prop` recording Ω₄^{Pin⁺} ≅ ℤ₁₆ as an external input) rather than a self-discharging existential, so the declaration cannot be mistaken for proved content by tooling/counters. Full discharge requires Thom-Pontryagin + Adams spectral sequence + Ext over A(1) in Mathlib — a genuine literature wall (no proof assistant has formalized these bordism groups), so leave as a documented external input.
evidence: Z16Classification.lean:55 `theorem z16_classification : ∃ (φ : ZMod 16 ≃ ZMod 16), Function.Bijective φ := ⟨Equiv.refl _, (Equiv.refl _).bijective⟩` with comment 'this placeholder was tautological'; Z16AnomalyComputation.lean:51 same shape for dai_freed_spin_z4; paper8:431-433 discloses 'converted to a trivially-true placeholder theorem'; D5:1315 `\placeholdertheorems` separation macro.

title: `h2_discharged : True := trivial` — a theorem named 'discharged' that discharges nothing
description: h2_discharged is a type-`True` theorem (`:= trivial`) whose name asserts that hypothesis H2 (the change-of-rings isomorphism premise in the Adams spectral sequence: A is a B-algebra, P is a free B-module, ε is an augmentation) has been discharged. The docstring argues H2 is 'an algebraic identity, not a topological hypothesis' and that the three conditions hold (one VERIFIED in A1Resolution.lean, two 'ALGEBRAIC FACT'), but the theorem body proves `True`, establishing none of them. This is the P5 'theorem named after its conclusion but body restates/proves nothing' pattern — and worse than a neutral marker because 'discharged' positively asserts work was done.
what_falls_short: The name implies a discharge that the proof term does not perform. If the three conditions are genuinely proved elsewhere (A1Resolution.lean for freeness; the two algebraic facts), the theorem should CALL them (a conjunction of the three lemmas) — not assert True. As written it is a tautology wearing a load-bearing name.
remediation: Replace the body with the actual conjunction: `theorem h2_discharged : (A is B-algebra) ∧ (P free B-module) ∧ (ε augmentation) := ⟨..., A1Resolution.<freeness lemma>, ...⟩`, importing A1Resolution and the two algebraic facts. If the algebraic facts aren't yet formalized, rename to `h2_discharge_plan_TODO` and state which conjunct remains. This is straightforward Lean work since the docstring claims the inputs already exist.
evidence: ChangeOfRings.lean:104-110: docstring lists '2. P is a free B-module — VERIFIED in A1Resolution.lean ... None of these require topology' then `theorem h2_discharged : True := trivial`.

title: Three content-bearing `True` synthesis/caveat stubs in the chirality/SMG chain (smg_three_conditions, tpf_z16_combined, disentangler_3d_requires_gap1, misumi_instability_caveat) plus the FLRW/cosmo conditional-summary stubs
description: smg_three_conditions and tpf_z16_combined are `True := trivial` 'synthesis' theorems standing in for the logical combination (GS no-go evasion ∧ Z16 anomaly cancellation ∧ spectral gap → SMG) — the substantive conjunction is asserted in the docstring but the body proves nothing. disentangler_3d_requires_gap1 is a `True` stub for the conditional 'gapped_interface + anomaly-free → disentangler exists' (its referenced gapped_interface_axiom was retired 2026-05-19 → now the tracked Prop H_GappedInterface, so the stub now stands for a conditional over a tracked hypothesis). misumi_instability_caveat is a `True` stub recording a literature caveat. cosmological_perturbations_summary and flrw_dynamics_summary are `True` module-end markers whose docstrings enumerate the real falsifier theorems that live in the same file (which ARE proven) — so these two are closer to docs markers than content stubs.
what_falls_short: The synthesis theorems (smg_three_conditions, tpf_z16_combined) and the conditional (disentangler_3d_requires_gap1) name real logical content but encode it as True. They could and should be genuine implications: e.g. disentangler_3d_requires_gap1 should be `H_GappedInterface ∧ AnomalyFree → ∃ W : SymmetryDisentangler, ...` (the prior audit, item with SPTClassification, recommended exactly this). The two _summary stubs are benign (the real content is co-located and proved). misumi_instability_caveat is a literature note with no provable content — acceptably a marker but mis-typed as a theorem.
remediation: For smg_three_conditions / tpf_z16_combined: write the actual conjunction-implication referencing the proven GS-evasion, Z16, and gap theorems (the gap conjunct is conjectural → make it a hypothesis, yielding a genuine conditional theorem). For disentangler_3d_requires_gap1: restate as the implication over the H_GappedInterface tracked Prop. For misumi_instability_caveat: demote to a doc comment or a `def caveat : String`, not a `theorem`. The two _summary stubs: leave (co-located proven content) or fold into the cosmetic-marker class.
evidence: SMGClassification.lean:245 `theorem smg_three_conditions : True := trivial  -- synthesis: GS + TPF + Z₁₆ + gap`; GaugingStep.lean:107-110 `theorem disentangler_3d_requires_gap1 : ... True := trivial` with comment 'gapped_interface_axiom + anomaly-free → disentangler exists ... We can only state this as a conditional'; gapped_interface_axiom retired per SPTClassification.lean:16 'pre-2026-05-19 axiom gapped_interface_axiom'.

title: ~30 `*_module_summary_marker : True := trivial` cosmetic documentation markers (GR/cosmology/substrate modules)
source: lean/SKEFTHawking/BBN.lean:429 (module_summary_marker) and ~30 per-wave/per-module variants: _phase6f_w1..w8_*_module_summary_marker, _phase6g_w1..w10_*_module_summary_marker (across LorentzianMetric, Curvature, RiemannTensor, ADMFormalism, AreaTheorem, PenroseSingularity, etc.), QuantumNetwork/Basic.lean:26 (quantumNetwork_substrate_ready), VestigialInflationNoGo.lean:299 (phase6b_w3_structural_no_go_marker), CosmologicalPerturbations.lean:659, FLRWDynamics.lean:351, uqsl2_affine_hopf_summary, _wave5_module_summary_marker
description: A grep for type-exactly-True theorems finds 43 total (vs the 26 in counts.json's placeholder category); the extra ~17-30 are per-wave/per-module `_module_summary_marker` declarations in the general-relativity and cosmology modules (Phase 6f/6g GR build-out), plus quantumNetwork_substrate_ready ('marker that the substrate root compiles; replaced by real definitions in Wave 1+') and phase6b_w3_structural_no_go_marker ('no operational content; serves as the audit-trail anchor'). These are explicitly self-labeled 'non-substantive' / 'module-summary marker' in their docstrings and exist as audit-trail anchors, not as claimed results. They are honest and harmless individually but collectively inflate the type-True theorem population and the discrepancy vs counts.json (26 vs 43) should be reconciled by the counter.
what_falls_short: Nothing substantive — these are correctly-labeled cosmetic markers. The only issue is hygienic: (a) they are `theorem`s rather than non-counting markers, so they show up in any type-True grep and pad the count; (b) the counts.json placeholder figure (26) does not match the on-disk type-True count (43), indicating the counter classifies the per-wave GR markers differently — a reconciliation item, not a correctness defect.
remediation: Optional cleanup: convert the cosmetic markers from `theorem foo : True := trivial` to a non-counting form (e.g. a `def fooMarker : Unit := ()` or a `/-- ... -/` section comment) so they stop appearing in theorem counts and type-True audits. At minimum, reconcile counts.json's placeholder count (26) with the actual on-disk type-True total (43) so the audit ground-truth is accurate. No urgency — these do not misrepresent any result.
evidence: BBN.lean:428-429 `/-- Module-summary marker (non-substantive). -/ theorem module_summary_marker : True := trivial`; QuantumNetwork/Basic.lean:24-26 'Marker that the Phase-6AA substrate root compiles; replaced by real definitions in Wave 1+'; grep type-True count = 43 (30 are *_module_summary_marker), vs counts.json placeholder=26.

title: Honestly-labeled definitional `:= rfl` re-export theorems (pillar3_*_DEFINITIONAL, sVec_fermion_dim_DEFINITIONAL, dg_generator_count/dg_relation_count) — P3 trivialities, mostly self-disclosed
description: A set of P3 (multiplication/identity-only) trivialities named as physics/algebra content. The project has SELF-CORRECTED the worst ones flagged by the 2026-04-26 audit: pillar3_onsager_dg_DEFINITIONAL (`DG_COEFF = 16 := rfl`) and pillar3_contraction_target_DEFINITIONAL (`sl2_dim = 3 := rfl`) now carry `_DEFINITIONAL` suffixes and docstrings stating they are 're-exports of the constant for downstream cross-reference, not a fresh proof'; sVec_fermion_dim_DEFINITIONAL (`(1:ℝ)=1 := rfl`) is labeled 'carries no genuine super-modular content ... unit-dimension axiom of any fusion category'. Two remain un-suffixed but are pure trivialities: dg_generator_count (`(2:ℕ)=2 := rfl`, docstring 'DG presentation has exactly 2 generators') and dg_relation_count (`(2:ℕ)=2 := rfl`). These are the P3/definitional-unfolding-as-physics pattern.
what_falls_short: dg_generator_count/dg_relation_count encode a structural fact (2 generators, 2 relations) as the syntactic tautology 2=2, which catches no typo and proves no physics — the count is hardcoded in the statement, so it is rfl-trivial. The _DEFINITIONAL-suffixed ones are honestly disclosed (the substantive content lives in OnsagerAlgebra.lean / Z16Classification.sVec_global_dim), so they are acceptable transparent re-exports rather than masquerades. paper8 cites the DG content only via the substantive DG_COEFF computation, not the 2=2 counts, so nothing load-bearing rests on the trivialities.
remediation: For dg_generator_count/dg_relation_count: either delete (the fact is already implicit in the DG presentation definition) or restate against the actual generator/relation set so the count is derived, not hardcoded (e.g. `Finset.card dgGenerators = 2 := by decide` over a real Finset). The _DEFINITIONAL re-exports are fine as-is — they exemplify the project's transparent-naming discipline working. The good counterexample to keep in mind: BBN.lean:171 y_p_central_in_physical_range (`0 < Y_p < 1`) correctly REPLACED the prior within-2σ-band tautology y_p_within_pdg_2sigma — confirming the strengthening discipline is active and the worst band-tautologies are already gone.
evidence: OnsagerAlgebra.lean:309 `theorem dg_generator_count : (2 : ℕ) = 2 := rfl` (docstring 'DG presentation has exactly 2 generators'); ChiralityWallMaster.lean:112 `theorem pillar3_onsager_dg_DEFINITIONAL : DG_COEFF = 16 := rfl` (docstring 're-export ... not a fresh proof'); BBN.lean:164-172 confirms y_p_within_pdg_2sigma was replaced by the physical-range form. No remaining `within_2sigma` band tautologies found project-wide (grep empty).

title: Rokhlin 16∣σ headline is unconditional only because the topological factor 2∣σ/8 is baked into the SmoothSpinManifold4 structure as an undischarged field, never proven for any concrete manifold
description: The project's flagship Phase-5q.B result is `16 ∣ σ` (Rokhlin's theorem, the origin of the Z16 generation count). The ALGEBRAIC half `8 ∣ σ` (van der Blij) IS genuinely, kernel-purely unconditional: the two named inputs — Hasse-Minkowski `[HM]` (`hasIsotropicVector`) and theta-modularity `[Θ]` (`eight_dvd_latticeSig_of_definite`) — are now PROVEN theorems, not hypotheses, and `lean_verify` on `sixteen_convergence_unconditional` returns axiom closure exactly `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (zero project axioms). The factor-of-TWO upgrade from 8 to 16, however, is supplied by the structure field `topo : (2 : ℤ) ∣ latticeSig form / 8` — the genuinely-topological input (Â-genus even / Arf(q̄)=0). `SmoothSpinManifold4.rokhlin : 16 ∣ M.sig` consumes `M.topo`, and `sixteen_convergence_unconditional` quantifies `∀ M : SmoothSpinManifold4, 16 ∣ M.sig` — but EVERY inhabitant of that structure carries `topo` in its very definition. No concrete `SmoothSpinManifold4` is ever constructed anywhere in the library (grep found zero `.mk`/`where`/`⟨…⟩` instances), so `topo` is never discharged for an actual spin manifold. `ArfInvariant.lean` develops the abstract Arf-genus theory (democratic majority, Gauss sum) that WOULD feed `topo` but stops short of producing `2 ∣ σ/8` from a manifold's spin structure.
what_falls_short: The substantive Rokhlin content — what distinguishes 16 from van der Blij's 8, i.e. Freedman's E8-manifold obstruction made precise — is moved INTO the structure definition (`topo` field) rather than proven. The theorem `16 ∣ M.sig` is therefore 'unconditional' only in the narrow sense of carrying no hypothesis BEYOND the structure; it is a defining-the-conclusion pattern at the structure level (CLAUDE.md preemptive-strengthening check #5). The genuinely unconditional, fully-proven claim is `8 ∣ σ`, not `16 ∣ σ`.
remediation: Discharge `topo` for the concrete intersection forms the project actually uses (E8, -E8, H normal-form pieces from the classification). The Arf/Â-genus chain is seeded in ArfInvariant.lean; completing `2 ∣ latticeSig form / 8` from even-unimodularity + the spin quadratic refinement (Wu's formula → Arf(q̄)=0 → factor of 2) would make `16 ∣ σ` unconditional in the same sense `8 ∣ σ` already is. This is research-grade index-theoretic math (the genuinely-topological half of Rokhlin), not 'just more Lean work' — but the project has already cracked the algebraic half end-to-end, so it is tractable, not a named literature wall.
evidence: lean_verify SKEFTHawking.sixteen_convergence_unconditional → {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}. SpinRokhlinInterface.lean:63 `topo : (2 : ℤ) ∣ latticeSig form / 8`; line 76-77 `SmoothSpinManifold4.rokhlin := sixteen_dvd_latticeSig M.form M.even_unimod M.topo`. grep for SmoothSpinManifold4 instances → none. File's own docstring line 33: 'The only remaining tracked hypothesis is topo (2∣σ/8), which is irreducibly topological (not an axiom, not algebraic).'

title: paper10 advertises 'unconditional 16∣σ' without flagging that the topological factor 2∣σ/8 is an undischarged structure-field assumption
description: Paper10 §formal-verification writes: 'SpinRokhlinInterface.lean (unconditional 16 ∣ σ and sixteen_convergence_unconditional, zero sorry)' and 'The unconditional 16 ∣ σ derivation above does not route through the Adams spectral sequence; it proceeds via the lattice-signature classification (van der Blij plus the topological factor 2 ∣ σ/8).' The paper DOES name 'the topological factor 2 ∣ σ/8' explicitly — so it is not a fabrication — but it labels the result 'unconditional 16 ∣ σ' twice without disclosing that this factor is an assumed structure field (`topo`), never proven for a concrete manifold. A reader naturally reads 'unconditional 16 ∣ σ' as 'we formally proved Rokhlin's theorem,' which overstates what holds: 8∣σ is proven; the upgrade to 16 is assumed.
what_falls_short: The word 'unconditional' is accurate for the Lean theorem-as-stated (no hypothesis beyond the SmoothSpinManifold4 structure) but misleading at the physics-claim level, where the substantive Rokhlin upgrade is carried as a structure-baked assumption. Contrast with the EXEMPLARY honesty of the D8 paper on the parallel Ross-Selinger gate (which states the gap 'plainly' and carries it 'as an explicit hypothesis in the theorem statement').
remediation: Add one clause: '…unconditional given the topological factor 2 ∣ σ/8 (Â-genus even / Arf(q̄)=0), which we carry as a structure field rather than derive; the algebraic half 8 ∣ σ is proven outright.' Mirror the D8 paper's disclosure pattern. Trivial LaTeX edit; the underlying status is honest, only the framing needs a hedge.
evidence: paper_draft.tex:323 '(unconditional 16 ∣ σ ...)'; :329-331 'The unconditional 16 ∣ σ derivation above ... proceeds via the lattice-signature classification (van der Blij plus the topological factor 2 ∣ σ/8).' Compare D8 paper_draft.tex:495-509 which states its parallel gate 'plainly' and 'carried as an explicit hypothesis in the theorem statement — never [smuggled].'

title: Ancilla-free single-qubit Ross-Selinger ∀U-unconditional synthesis is NOT proven — it is gated on the §6 relative-norm / prime-density existence (the named literature wall)
description: The ancilla-free Ross-Selinger O(log 1/ε) synthesis headlines are explicitly conditional: `rossSelinger_compile_log_length` takes `ht : KMM.gridFindT (compileColumn U k) k b = some t` as a hypothesis (the grid finder must succeed), and `rossSelinger_synth_of_residual` isolates that gate as the relative-norm existence `t₀†t₀ = √2^{2k} − u·u*` (Ross thesis Problem 3.2.4). `gridFindT_isSome_of_residual` lifts `diophantineSearch_complete` (a genuine algorithm-completeness theorem: if a bounded witness exists, the search finds it — verified, not tautological). The remaining gap — that SOME grid candidate's residual is a relative norm — is the prime-density input that the source literature itself (Selinger arXiv:1212.6253 Hypothesis 29 = Ross-Selinger arXiv:1403.2975v3 Hypothesis 8.3) discharges only as a RANDOMIZED algorithm under a prime-distribution hypothesis. This is the named literature wall (a) in the audit brief: Mathlib Dirichlet is insufficient; the analytic-NT short-interval existence is open in the formalized-math ecosystem.
what_falls_short: The fully-unconditional ∀U claim for the ancilla-FREE route is not shipped. The efficiency (O(log 1/ε), exponent 1, length ≤ N₃+8k) is delivered UNCONDITIONALLY on the residual existence, and the residual existence is the precise, isolated, literature-grounded gate. The headline as advertised ('the project synthesizes any SU(2) gate in O(log 1/ε)') is NOT false because it is delivered by a DIFFERENT route (see next finding) — but the specific ancilla-free Ross-Selinger theorem remains gated.
remediation: No remediation needed for correctness — the gap is honestly carried as an explicit theorem hypothesis and disclosed plainly in the D8 paper. Genuine unconditional discharge requires either (i) the analytic-NT relative-norm density result (strictly weaker than prime-density per the D8 paper, plausibly unconditional via Landau-Ramanujan half-dimensional sieves but far beyond current formal libraries), or (ii) accepting the 2-ancilla route (next finding) as the project's unconditional ∀U deliverable. Option (ii) is already done. This is a legitimate decomposition-backed wall, not an effort-descope.
evidence: LogLengthHeadline.lean:69-78 `rossSelinger_compile_log_length (... ht : KMM.gridFindT ... = some t) ...`; :137-142 `rossSelinger_synth_of_residual (ht₀ : t₀ ∈ boundedZOmega b) (he : ZOmega.normSq t₀ = ...)`; :130-133 docstring names 'Selinger arXiv:1212.6253 Hypothesis 29 = arXiv:1403.2975v3 Hypothesis 8.3'. GridSolver.lean:83-86 diophantineSearch_complete is a real completeness lemma, not a tautology.

title: The 2-ancilla KMM ∀U∈SU(2) synthesis headline IS genuinely unconditional (no prime-density) — so the project's universal-synthesis claim is delivered, just via the ancilla route not the ancilla-free Ross-Selinger route
description: This is the COUNTERWEIGHT to the previous finding and the reason the project's advertised universal-synthesis headline is NOT an overclaim. `kmm_universal_headline` proves: ∀ U ∈ SU(2), ∀ precision k, there is an explicit 3-qubit (system + 2 ancilla) Clifford+T word of length ≤ 50400k+812 (linear in k, i.e. O(log 1/ε) exponent 1) and a global phase c (‖c‖=1) such that for EVERY unit system state the embedded word lands within squared ℓ²-distance 9·(2/4ᵏ + 2√2/2ᵏ) = O(2^{-k}) of the ideal output. `lean_verify` returns axiom closure exactly `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` — fully unconditional. The proof routes via SU(2) Euler decomposition + three approximate z-rotation words interleaved with two EXACT system-Hadamards, with errors adding through unitarity (chain_bound). The docstring is explicit: 'No prime-density hypothesis — the inputs are Lagrange four-squares, the constructive column lemma, and the Euler decomposition; this is the KMM arXiv:1212.0822 result, fully unconditional.' The 2 ancillas are precisely what sidesteps the prime-density wall that gates the ancilla-free route.
what_falls_short: Nothing falls short of the theorem as stated — this is a genuine unconditional ∀U result. The only caveat for honest framing is that it is a 3-qubit (2-ancilla) word, not a single-qubit word; papers must not present the ancilla-free Ross-Selinger O(log 1/ε) scaling as unconditional ∀U by conflating it with this ancilla result. The D8 paper correctly keeps the two distinct.
remediation: No remediation needed — this is a correctly-proven unconditional headline. Flagged only to document that the project's 'universal SU(2) synthesis in O(log 1/ε)' claim IS substantively delivered (via ancillas), preventing the previous finding from being mis-read as 'the project cannot synthesize arbitrary gates.' Bundles should cite kmm_universal_headline for the unconditional ∀U claim and rossSelinger_synth_of_residual for the ancilla-free-conditional claim, never swapping them.
evidence: lean_verify SKEFTHawking.RossSelinger.Gate3.kmm_universal_headline → {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}. KMMUniversal.lean:248-256 theorem statement (∀ U, ∀ k, ∃ W c, length ≤ 50400k+812, ∀ unit state, dist² ≤ 9·(2/4ᵏ+2√2/2ᵏ)); :240-247 docstring 'No prime-density hypothesis ... fully unconditional.'

title: Four SPT/Standard-Model anomaly-matching headlines are gated on TPFConjecture (Thorngren-Preskill-Fidkowski), a research-grade conjecture open in 3+1D/4+1D
description: Four theorems take `(H : TPFConjecture)` as an explicit hypothesis parameter and produce the SM gapped-interface / chiral-gauge-theory existence claims from it: `anomaly_free_implies_chiral_gauge`, `sm_generation_gapped_interface` (16 Majorana per generation), `sm_three_gen_gapped_interface` (n=48), and the contrapositive `no_gap_implies_anomalous`. Each proof is a one-line application of `H` (e.g. `H spt h_free`) — the entire substantive content IS the conjecture. TPFConjecture is the def-Prop form of what was `axiom gapped_interface_axiom` until 2026-05-19 (converted to a tracked Prop per Invariant #15). Underlying status: 'plausible but unproven' per TPF's own 2026 assessment; proven only in the 1+1D analog (VillainHamiltonian.k3450_gappable) and 2+1D analog (FK.fk_summary, explicit 16×16 Hamiltonian, gap Δ=14); the 3+1D/4+1D case is at the literature frontier with no known constructive approach.
what_falls_short: The SM anomaly-matching → chiral-gauge-theory existence is NOT proven by the project — it is the TPF conjecture restated and propagated. The honest tracked-Prop framing makes this visible at the type-signature level, which is the correct posture; the risk is only if a downstream bundle drops the dependency.
remediation: Keep as KEEP_AS_TRACKED (per PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md §4) — discharging requires either a constructive 3+1D/4+1D TPF proof (open at the frontier) or a counterexample (field-revolutionizing). Both out of scope. Verified that paper9 (SM anomaly/Drinfeld) correctly labels these 'TPF conjecture' / 'conditional' — no bundle overclaim found. Maintain the dependency propagation in any future bundle that cites these four theorems.
evidence: SPTClassification.lean:317 `theorem sm_generation_gapped_interface (H : TPFConjecture) : ... := H _ spt_one_generation_anomaly_free`; 4 consumers total confirmed by grep. PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md §4 documents conversion from axiom gapped_interface_axiom (2026-05-19), dimensional ladder (1+1D + 2+1D proven, 3+1D/4+1D conjectural). paper9 paper_draft.tex:284-315 correctly frames as 'TPF conjecture ... conditional.'

title: Phase6h hypercharge-splitting structure embeds an OPEN-AT-FRONTIER Prop field (light-quark mass predictions for c/s/u/d) but its consuming theorem sidesteps it
description: The structure `Phase6hHyperchargeSplittingHypothesis` carries a field `predictsBandSelectiveLightQuarks : Prop` whose docstring states it is 'OPEN-AT-FRONTIER for c, s, u, d (only b is partially captured by the AS framework)' — an undischarged Prop embedded as a bare structure field. The theorem that consumes the structure, `phase_6h_supersession_suppression_lt_one`, proves `exp(-δ_b/α_∗) < 1` using ONLY the positivity fields (`delta_b_pos`, `alpha_pos`) and never touches the open Prop. So the open Prop is inert: it is carried in the structure but not load-bearing for any shipped theorem.
what_falls_short: The structure advertises a light-quark-prediction capability (the `predictsBandSelectiveLightQuarks` field) that is open at the frontier and never used. This is lower-stakes: the flagship result here is the `light_quark_hierarchy_fallthrough_bundle`, which is itself a NO-GO / fall-through verdict (substrate does NOT close the light-quark hierarchy), honestly framed — not a positive prediction. The suppression theorem is substantive but trivial (sign of δ_b/α_∗).
remediation: Either (a) drop the inert `predictsBandSelectiveLightQuarks : Prop` field since no theorem consumes it (it advertises an unproven capability for no benefit), or (b) if it is meant as a future hook, document it as a deferred-discharge placeholder in PERMANENT_TRACKED_HYPOTHESES.md so its open-frontier status is catalogued rather than buried in a struct field. The fall-through bundle itself is honest and needs no change.
evidence: LightQuarkHierarchyFallthrough.lean:208-212 field `predictsBandSelectiveLightQuarks : Prop` with docstring 'OPEN-AT-FRONTIER for c, s, u, d'; :222-229 phase_6h_supersession_suppression_lt_one uses only delta_b_pos/alpha_pos, never the Prop field. Bundle :250-254 is a NO-GO verdict (ratios + 'Hierarchy NOT uniform-geometric').

title: QuantumNetwork distillation-rate optimality headlines correctly carry their open conjectures as explicit non-claims (Fortescue-Lo, W-state =1, Caves/CCR floor) — no overclaim, verified
description: Audited the QuantumNetwork conditional candidates named in the brief (Caves added-noise / CCR floor). Findings: (1) the multipartite distillation upper-bound of 1 'is the open Fortescue-Lo conjecture, NOT claimed here'; (2) the W-state asymptotic `E_t^∞(W₃)=1` 'is an open conjecture and is deliberately NOT formalized' — only the strict-below-1 finite-round yield is proven; (3) the quantum-FDT noise floor is DERIVED from the bosonic CCR ladder algebra [a,a†]=1 'rather than taken as the Caves hypothesis' (`fdt_noise_floor_amplifier`). All three are honestly framed as either explicit non-claims or derived-not-assumed. The D9-cited `teleportAvgFidelity_horodecki_unconditional` exists and resolves.
what_falls_short: Nothing — this is a clean audit result documenting that the QuantumNetwork conditionals are NOT presented as unconditional. Flagged for completeness so the audit record shows the Caves/CCR candidate was checked and found correctly hedged.
remediation: No action needed — these are model citizens of honest conditional framing. The open conjectures (Fortescue-Lo optimality, W-state asymptotic) are out of project scope and correctly excluded from claims; the CCR floor is derived from first-principles ladder algebra, not assumed as the Caves hypothesis.
evidence: MultipartiteComparison.lean:103 'the open Fortescue–Lo conjecture, NOT claimed'; WStateRate.lean:15 '=1 is an open conjecture and is deliberately NOT formalized'; QuantumFDTFloor.lean:185 'rather than taken as the Caves hypothesis'; HaarPauli.lean:125 teleportAvgFidelity_horodecki_unconditional exists.

title: Track B entropic-gravity "8/8 unanimous NO-GO" headline rests on a verdict-by-stipulation function (rDIndependentNoGo returns hardcoded true for every constructor)
source: SK_EFT_Hawking/lean/SKEFTHawking/EntropicGravityDarkEnergy.lean:413-449 (def rDIndependentNoGo, theorem r_d_anchoring_partial_rescue_does_not_save_class_b_or_class_d:442, theorem entropic_gravity_no_go_count_eight:474); bridged by DarkSectorClassificationExtension.lean:212 entropic_gravity_unanimous_no_go_first_in_phase6m
description: The paper-45 / D5-bundle publication-novelty headline is "the first complete-mechanism-family NO-GO closure: 8 of 8 entropic-gravity DE candidates excluded." In Lean this is two theorems: (1) `entropic_gravity_no_go_count_eight : allEntropicGravityCandidates.length = 8 := rfl` (a list-length fact over a hand-typed 8-element constructor list), and (2) `r_d_anchoring_partial_rescue_does_not_save_class_b_or_class_d : ∀ c, rDIndependentNoGo c = true := by cases c <;> rfl`. The function `rDIndependentNoGo : EntropicGravityCandidate → Bool` is defined to return `true` for ALL 8 constructors (verbatim `| .verlinde2017 => true ... | .odintsovDonofrioPaul => true`). So the "∀ candidate is NO-GO" theorem is `cases <;> rfl` over a function stipulated to be constantly true.
what_falls_short: Defining-the-conclusion / verdict-by-stipulation anti-pattern: the physics verdict (each candidate is r_d-independently excluded) is asserted in the def, and the theorem merely re-reads the def. No candidate's exclusion is *derived* in this theorem; it is a faithful re-statement of a hand-curated literature-review table. The genuinely-falsifiable per-candidate content lives in OTHER theorems in the same file (dec_cpl_excluded_from_desi_dr2_1sigma:318 does real CPL-region geometry; tsallis_log_bayes ≥ 5.0:361, hde_event_horizon_wa_sigma ≥ 3.0:352 are published-bound comparisons) — those are good. But the aggregate "8/8 unanimous" headline theorem itself carries no proof load beyond list-length and a constant-true cases split.
remediation: The bundle headline is honest about the SOURCE of each verdict (the file's docstrings are meticulous and per-candidate primary-source-cited), so the fix is presentational, not a fabrication. Either (a) reframe the Lean claim as bookkeeping (rename to *_classification_table_consistent and state in D5 that the 8/8 verdict is a literature-review ledger whose individual legs are the per-candidate quantitative theorems, NOT a single derived no-go), or (b) make rDIndependentNoGo COMPUTE each verdict from that candidate's quantitative falsifier theorem (e.g. derive verlinde2017's true from the Halenka-Miller >5σ leg, tsallis from tsallis_log_bayes ≥ jeffreys) so the aggregate is a genuine conjunction of the substantive legs rather than a constant. Both are 'medium' Lean work.
evidence: rDIndependentNoGo def lines 413-425 return `true` for all 8 constructors with only comments distinguishing them; theorem at 442 is `intro c; cases c <;> rfl`; D5 anchor confirmed: papers/AutomatedReviews/2026-05-01-1625-bundle-stage13/D5.md:35 cites `entropic_gravity_no_go_count_eight` as backing the '8 of 8 unanimous NO-GO' §9 headline.

title: EmergentGravityBounds physics 'results' are bare integer/arithmetic identities (4×4=16, 16−6=10, 2+4+4=10, 6>1) with all physics content in comments
description: A cluster of theorems in the tetrad/mode-counting and spin-connection-gap sections of the emergent-gravity-obstruction module are pure `norm_num`/`rfl` arithmetic on bare numerals: `tetrad_components : 4*4 = 16`, `physical_modes : 16-6 = 10`, `mode_decomposition : 2+4+4 = 10`, `tetrad_modes_nf_independent (N_f:ℕ) : 10 = 10 := rfl`, `three_obstacles_exist : 3 = 3 := rfl`, `spin_connection_gap : 6 > 1`, `metric_independent_components : (4*(4+1))/2 = 10`, `G_c_nlo_invariance : (0:ℝ)=0 := rfl`. The substantive physics (a tetrad is 4×4; 6 NG modes eaten; dim SO(3,1)=6 vs dim U(1)=1) lives entirely in the docstrings; the Lean propositions are number facts decoupled from any tetrad, mode, or gauge-group object.
what_falls_short: Definitional-unfolding-as-physics (the '0.34 = 2×0.17' anti-pattern). The numerals are not bound to formalized tetrad/mode/group structures, so the theorems are true regardless of whether the physics claim is true — e.g. `spin_connection_gap : 6 > 1` proves nothing about SO(3,1) vs U(1); it proves 6>1. `tetrad_modes_nf_independent (N_f:ℕ) : 10 = 10` even takes an unused N_f argument to dress `10=10:=rfl` as 'N_f-independence'.
remediation: These back the D3 Wen/ADW obstruction narrative but are not the obstruction's load-bearing math (the real content is wen_adw_ratio_eq / wen_adw_ratio_bracket later in the file, which ARE substantive closed-form/numeric-bracket theorems). Either delete the arithmetic stubs and keep only the wen_adw_ratio theorems + the genuine `spin_connection_gap` reframed over an actual dim(SO(3,1))=6 vs dim(U(1))=1 computation on formalized Lie-algebra-dimension functions, or demote them to `def`/`Nat` literals + a comment. Low impact: these are leaf decorations, not cited as the obstruction proof.
evidence: Direct reads: line 160 `theorem tetrad_components : 4 * 4 = (16 : ℕ) := by norm_num`; line 172 `theorem tetrad_modes_nf_independent (N_f : ℕ) : 10 = (10 : ℕ) := rfl`; line 180 `theorem three_obstacles_exist : 3 = (3 : ℕ) := rfl`; line 185 `theorem spin_connection_gap : (6 : ℕ) > 1 := by norm_num`.

title: TPFEvasion no-go-evasion 'results' are integer arithmetic (9−3<9, 3≥1, 3+2=5, 4+1=5) standing in for the GS-condition-counting argument
description: The 'TPF evades the Golterman-Shamir no-go' chirality-wall result is partly carried by theorems that are bare integer arithmetic: `tpf_violation_count_ge_three : 3 ≥ 1`, `potential_violation_count : 3 + 2 = 5`, `violation_extra_dim : 4 + 1 = 5`, `nogo_fails_with_three_violations : 9 - 3 < 9`, `nogo_fails_with_one_violation : 9 - 1 < 9`, with the same `9-3<9` / `9-1<9` duplicated in LatticeHamiltonian.lean. The mapping '9 GS conditions, TPF violates 3, so ≤6 hold, so the conjunction fails' is entirely in the comments; the propositions are arithmetic.
what_falls_short: Multiplication/arithmetic-only-as-physics (P3) and definitional-unfolding. `9-3<9` is true for reasons unrelated to whether TPF actually violates 3 GS conditions; the count '3' is a comment, not a proven property of TPF. The file DOES contain the genuine evasion content (violation_I3:112 ¬e-dimensionality of ℓ²(ℤ), violation_C1:118 round() discontinuity — both real Mathlib-level proofs), so the arithmetic theorems are redundant scaffolding around real lemmas, and the general structural form lives correctly in LatticeHamiltonian.conjunction_fragile:384 / nogo_contrapositive:396.
remediation: Delete the bare-arithmetic count theorems; the load-bearing evasion content (violation_I3, violation_C1, two_violations_proved) and the general structural theorem (conjunction_fragile / nogo_contrapositive, which quantify over an abstract Fin 9 → Prop condition family) already carry the argument properly. If a count is wanted, state it as a property of the formalized GSCondition list (ChiralityWall already does this via condition_conservation), not as `9-3<9`. Easy cleanup, low impact (paper7 references nogo_fails_with_violations, a differently-named structural theorem, not these arithmetic stubs).
evidence: TPFEvasion.lean:141 `theorem nogo_fails_with_three_violations : 9 - 3 < 9 := by norm_num`; :94 `theorem tpf_violation_count_ge_three : 3 ≥ 1 := by norm_num`; LatticeHamiltonian.lean:388 `theorem tpf_breaks_nogo : 9 - 3 < 9 := by norm_num`. Registry: constants.py:3751 maps TPFEvasion to chirality-wall bundle; paper7 tex references nogo_fails_with_violations (a structural theorem), not these.

title: DEC orthogonality-principle exclusion is defining-the-conclusion: model hand-set with gibbsDuhemEvaded:=false, so IsViable=false by rfl
description: `dec_fails_orthogonality_principle : IsViable dec_orthogonality_model = false := by unfold ...; rfl`. The model `dec_orthogonality_model` is hand-constructed with field `gibbsDuhemEvaded := false` and the other three viability factors `true`; `IsViable` is the conjunction of all four, so `IsViable = false` is forced by `rfl` from the stipulated definition. The theorem unfolds the definition it was handed.
what_falls_short: Defining-the-conclusion anti-pattern: the substantive claim (DEC really is GD-locked, so factor #1 is false) is the assertion `gibbsDuhemEvaded := false` inside the `def`, and the theorem merely reads it back. The genuine GD-lock derivation lives elsewhere (GibbsDuhemTheorem.wVac_eq_neg_one_of_rhoV_ne_zero, referenced in the comment), but THIS theorem does not invoke it — it just unfolds a flag that was set by hand.
remediation: Make dec_orthogonality_model.gibbsDuhemEvaded *derive* from GibbsDuhemTheorem (call wVac_eq_neg_one_of_rhoV_ne_zero in the proof to establish the flag value), so the cross-bridge in the docstring ('Phase 5y → Phase 6m proof chain') is actually realized in the body per the project's own P6 cross-module-bridge-integrity discipline. The companion theorem dec_cpl_excluded_from_desi_dr2_1sigma:318 is already the genuine substantive form and should be preferred as the headline leg.
evidence: Line 327-331 def sets gibbsDuhemEvaded := false explicitly; line 341-343 `theorem dec_fails_orthogonality_principle : IsViable dec_orthogonality_model = false := by unfold IsViable dec_orthogonality_model; rfl`. Contrast: dec_cpl_excluded_from_desi_dr2_1sigma:318 does real region-membership disproof via norm_num on -0.8 ≤ -1.

title: Track C 'highest survival' / unimodular-escape counts are list-filters over hand-assigned verdict fields (classification bookkeeping dressed as derived theorems)
source: SK_EFT_Hawking/lean/SKEFTHawking/JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy.lean:417-478 (def admitsUnimodularEscape, def cleared_R5_count, theorem track_c_at_least_5_cleared_r5, theorem unimodular_reformulation_admits_track_c_survivors); bridged by DarkSectorClassificationExtension.lean:226 track_c_highest_survival, :240 unimodular_admits_all_classes_except_class_a
description: `track_c_at_least_5_cleared_r5 : cleared_R5_count ≥ 5 := by decide` and `unimodular_reformulation_admits_track_c_survivors : (filter admitsUnimodularEscape).length = 8 ∧ admitsUnimodularEscape .kss = false := by decide`. `cleared_R5_count` filters a candidate list by each candidate's STORED verdict field (.clearedR5 / .noGoR5 etc.), and `admitsUnimodularEscape` maps `.outsideSakharov => false, _ => true`. The 'result' is the count of a hand-curated classification table.
what_falls_short: Verdict-by-stipulation / count-by-rfl: the physics judgments (which candidate cleared R5, which admits unimodular escape) are data set by the literature reviewer on each constructor, and the theorem `decide`s arithmetic over those flags. Faithful bookkeeping, but the 'highest-survival track' and 'admits 8 of 9' headlines are not derived — they re-count assigned labels. Slightly better than the entropic case (admitsUnimodularEscape has one structural exception rather than constant-true), and it is transparently a classification table.
remediation: Same class of fix as the entropic no-go: either reframe explicitly as a verified-consistent classification ledger (so reviewers know the verdicts are reviewer-assigned, not theorem-derived), or wire each verdict field to the per-candidate substantive falsifier so the count becomes a genuine conjunction. Keep the honest docstrings; adjust the framing of the count theorems in the D5/paper-45 narrative so they are not presented as derived survival results.
evidence: JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy.lean:462-469 cleared_R5_count filters on stored verdict constructors; :417-420 admitsUnimodularEscape `.outsideSakharov => false | _ => true`; :476-478 `theorem track_c_at_least_5_cleared_r5 : cleared_R5_count ≥ 5 := by unfold ...; decide`.

title: BBN per-candidate violators are conditional headlines: violation follows only from a hand-supplied delta_n_eff value taken as hypothesis
description: `z16_singlet_s0_violates_bbn_under_thermalization {delta_n_eff_S0:ℝ} (h_thermalize : delta_n_eff_S0 = 3.0) : ¬(delta_n_eff_S0 ≤ N_eff_2sigma_slack)` reduces, after rw [h_thermalize], to `¬(3.0 ≤ 0.34)` by norm_num. Likewise fg_torsion_violates_bbn assumes delta_n_eff_FG = 1.0 then proves ¬(1.0 ≤ 0.34). The substantive physics — whether the sterile/torsion sector actually thermalizes to that delta_n_eff — is taken as a free hypothesis, not derived.
what_falls_short: Conditional-headline / borderline hypothesis-restating: once the specific delta_n_eff value is granted as an assumption, the residual is a within-bound norm_num comparison (3.0 > 0.34). The thermalization input IS the physics and it is assumed. The module is honest about this ('discharging the thermalization tracked hypothesis ... closes the conformance verdict') and explicitly removed the prior literal identity-function P5 wrapper (decoupling_at_minus_fifty), so this is a transparently-labeled tracked-hypothesis-conditional result, not a hidden triviality.
remediation: No relabeling needed — the docstrings already disclose the hypothesis dependence and the result is correctly framed as conditional on a tracked thermalization hypothesis (a Phase-6 oscillation-rate computation would discharge it). Closing it fully (deriving delta_n_eff = 3.0 / 1.0 from a thermalization-rate vs Hubble-rate computation at T_BBN) is genuine physics-modeling work, hence 'hard'. Acceptable as-is given the honest framing; flag only so the D-bundle narrative does not over-claim the violation as unconditional.
evidence: BBN.lean:304-309 theorem takes `(h_thermalize : delta_n_eff_S0 = 3.0)` then `rw [h_thermalize]; norm_num` on ¬(3.0 ≤ 0.34); docstring lines 294-302 explicitly call it the 'correctness-push falsifier' that closes on discharging the thermalization tracked hypothesis.

title: ChiralityWall GS-no-go structural theorems decide over a hand-curated condition list whose applies_to_tpf/evaded flags encode the physics judgment
description: Theorems like `gs_nogo_requires_all : applicable_count gs_conditions_core4 = 2 ∧ ... := by decide`, `condition_conservation : evaded_count + applying_count = .length := by decide`, and `translation_invariance_applies : gs_translation.applies_to_tpf = true := by rfl` operate over `gs_conditions_core4`, a list whose per-condition `applies_to_tpf` / `evaded` Boolean fields are set by the literature-review judgment. The theorems `decide` count-arithmetic over those hand-set flags.
what_falls_short: Same verdict-by-stipulation/count-by-decide pattern as the dark-sector cases: which GS conditions TPF evades is asserted in the data, and the theorems re-count it. The GENUINELY substantive structural theorem in the family is nogo_contrapositive (LatticeHamiltonian.lean:396), which quantifies over an abstract Fin 9 → Prop family and proves the real logical fact (chiral spectrum ⇒ ∃ violated condition) without hand-set flags — that one is good and should be the headline.
remediation: Keep nogo_contrapositive / conjunction_fragile (abstract, genuinely structural) as the load-bearing form; demote the decide-over-flags count theorems to clearly-labeled bookkeeping. The translation_invariance_applies := rfl is a pure flag-readback and should either derive from a formalized translation-invariance property or be dropped. Easy, low impact.
evidence: ChiralityWall.lean:241-243 `theorem gs_nogo_requires_all : applicable_count gs_conditions_core4 = 2 ∧ ... := by decide +revert`; :271 `theorem translation_invariance_applies : gs_translation.applies_to_tpf = true := by rfl`; contrast LatticeHamiltonian.lean:396 nogo_contrapositive over abstract (conditions_hold : Fin 9 → Prop).

title: vestigial_phase_eta_violates_microscope_bound compares bare literals (1.0 > 1e-15) not the named constants it claims to relate
description: `vestigial_phase_eta_violates_microscope_bound : (1.0 : ℝ) > 1.0e-15 := by norm_num`. The docstring says it compares `VESTIGIAL_PHASE_ETA_MAX = 1.0` to `MICROSCOPE_BOUND = 1e-15`, but the theorem statement uses bare numeric literals, not the named constants. If either constant drifts in constants.py, the theorem stays green while silently decoupling from the physics it claims to encode. (Notably this exact theorem is cited verbatim in CLAUDE.md as the GOOD example of a published-bound comparison.)
what_falls_short: Borderline: it IS a published-bound comparison (the GOOD pattern), and the inequality 1.0 > 1e-15 is genuinely falsifiable physics (vestigial-phase EP violation exceeds MICROSCOPE by 15 orders). The weakness is only that it hardcodes the operands rather than referencing the Lean-side constants, so it does not actually detect drift in those constants — a minor cross-bridge-integrity gap, not a triviality. Listed for completeness and because it is the project's own cited exemplar.
remediation: Restate over the named Lean constants (vestigialPhaseEtaMax > microscopeBound, unfolding to the literals) so the theorem is wired to the same symbols the physics narrative names and would break on drift. Trivial edit. Genuinely good otherwise — do not over-weight.
evidence: EquivalencePrinciple.lean:495-496 `theorem vestigial_phase_eta_violates_microscope_bound : (1.0 : ℝ) > 1.0e-15 := by norm_num`; docstring lines 491-493 reference VESTIGIAL_PHASE_ETA_MAX and MICROSCOPE_BOUND as the intended operands but they do not appear in the statement.

title: T_dS_double_TGH and the *_module_summary_marker:True cluster are honestly-labeled definitional/cosmetic placeholders (class note, not individual flags)
source: SK_EFT_Hawking/lean/SKEFTHawking/CosmologicalConstant.lean:90 (T_dS_double_TGH); ~26 `*_module_summary_marker : True := trivial` across physics modules incl VestigialInflationNoGo.lean:299, FLRWDynamics.lean:351, CosmologicalPerturbations.lean:659, Curvature.lean:672, etc.
description: Two transparent classes: (1) `T_dS_double_TGH : de_sitter_temperature_volovik H = 2 * gibbons_hawking_temperature H := field_simp` is H/π = 2·(H/2π), a definitional-unfolding identity, with docstring explicitly stating 'tracked here only as the arithmetic relation' and the physics 'captured by deep research'. (2) ~26 `module_summary_marker : True := trivial` theorems (matching the ground-truth count of 26 placeholder-True theorems) are explicit audit-trail anchors with 'no operational content' per their own docstrings.
what_falls_short: Definitional-unfolding-as-physics (T_dS = 2 T_GH) and placeholder-True. Both are honestly disclosed and carry no concealed claim: the True markers are documentation anchors, and T_dS_double_TGH's docstring openly says the physical distinctness is NOT what the theorem proves. The companion T_dS_gt_T_GH:107 (strict inequality) carries marginally more weight. Flagged as a single class for completeness; not load-bearing physics claims dressed as results.
remediation: No action required for correctness; the True markers are already a known/counted population (26, matches docs/counts.json placeholder bucket) and are honestly documented. If tightening is wanted, the *_TODO True stubs (ocneanu_rigidity_TODO etc., noted in ground truth) are the ones to track toward real statements; the summary markers can stay as audit anchors or be moved to comments.
evidence: CosmologicalConstant.lean:90-93 `theorem T_dS_double_TGH ... := by unfold ...; field_simp` with docstring lines 85-88 disclaiming physics content; VestigialInflationNoGo.lean:299 `theorem phase6b_w3_structural_no_go_marker : True := trivial`; grep found ~20 `_module_summary_marker : True := trivial` in physics/geometry modules, consistent with the 26 placeholder-True count in docs/counts.json.
